overview: Gap closure PRD v3.0 for lhdn_payroll_integration Frappe app. US-001 to US-050 (v2.0) are complete. US-051 to US-095 (v3.0) add full LHDN compliance: PCB categories, TP1/TP3 reliefs, Zakat/CP38 offsets, complete EA Form, RM1,700 minimum wage, BIK module, SOCSO/EPF/EIS rate enforcement, bank disbursement files, statutory forms, and Employment Act compliance checks.
goals: Implement correct PCB Category (1/2/3), TP1 reliefs, Zakat offset, CP38 additional deduction
Fix CP39 CSV format to match LHDN e-PCB Plus upload specification with all mandatory columns
Rebuild EA Form with all mandatory Section A/B/C/D fields per P.U.(A) 107/2021
Update minimum wage validation to RM1,700 (Feb 2025 amendment)
Implement SOCSO bracketed table, EPF rate differential, EIS ceiling enforcement
Add bank disbursement files: Maybank IBG, CIMB IBG, DuitNow ISO 20022 pain.001.001.03
Implement SOCSO Borang 3/4, EPF i-Akaun export, PERKESO e-Caruman export
Add foreign worker levy tracking, CP107 tax clearance, age-based statutory transitions

userStories.title: Add AdditionalDocumentReference for registration ID in XML
userStories.description: LHDN v1.1 requires cac:AdditionalDocumentReference elements to carry the supplier's NRIC/BRN/Passport number separately from the TIN. Employee fields custom_id_type and custom_id_value are stored but never emitted into XML.
acceptanceCriteria: Both build_salary_slip_xml() and build_expense_claim_xml() emit AdditionalDocumentReference with id_type, DocumentType, and id_value in ExternalReference/URI
Works for individual and consolidated invoices
Test asserts AdditionalDocumentReference node present with correct values
technicalNotes: File: lhdn_payroll_integration/services/payload_builder.py
Add helper _add_additional_doc_reference(root, id_type, id_value) emitting cac:AdditionalDocumentReference
Call after building party elements in _build_invoice_skeleton()

userStories.title: Fix token expiry check and status poller base_url bug
userStories.description: get_access_token() returns cached token unconditionally (LHDN tokens expire after 3600s). userStories.title: Implement PCB/MTD monthly calculation validation
userStories.description: No PCB calculation engine exists. The app relies entirely on HR staff manually entering PCB deduction amounts with no validation against LHDN PCB Schedule (Jadual PCB). MTD Method 1 vs Method 2 not handled.
acceptanceCriteria: New pcb_calculator.py with calculate_pcb(annual_income, resident=True, married=False, children=0) implementing LHDN progressive tax scale
validate_pcb_amount(doc) whitelisted function warns (not blocks) if PCB deviates >10% from calculated estimate
Warning shown on Salary Slip form when PCB appears incorrect
Tests: single resident, married with children, non-resident flat 30%, zero-income scenarios
technicalNotes: New file: lhdn_payroll_integration/services/pcb_calculator.py
Non-resident: flat 30% on all income
Register validate_pcb_amount as @frappe.whitelist()

userStories.title: Implement NRIC format validation on Employee save
userStories.description: custom_id_value accepts any string up to 20 chars. Malaysian NRIC format is 12 digits (YYMMDDPBXXXX). Incorrectly entered NRICs silently pass through to LHDN causing rejections.
acceptanceCriteria: validate_nric(value) in validation.py: exactly 12 digits, valid YYMMDD date portion, valid state/birth code (01-16 local, 21-59 foreign)

userStories.title: Add IssueTime element to Invoice header
userStories.description: LHDN v1.1 requires both cbc:IssueDate and cbc:IssueTime in the Invoice root. Code emits only IssueDate. Without IssueTime, documents fail schema validation and UBL element ordering is incorrect.
acceptanceCriteria: _build_invoice_skeleton() emits <cbc:IssueTime>HH:MM:SS</cbc:IssueTime> immediately after IssueDate
Time uses frappe.utils.now_datetime() formatted as HH:MM:SS

userStories.title: Add InvoicePeriod to individual Salary Slip and Expense Claim invoices
userStories.description: build_salary_slip_xml() and build_expense_claim_xml() do not emit cac:InvoicePeriod. Only consolidated invoices have it. LHDN expects billing period (pay period dates) for payroll self-billed invoices.
acceptanceCriteria: Both builders add cac:InvoicePeriod with StartDate = doc.start_date, EndDate = doc.end_date
Test asserts InvoicePeriod present with correct dates in generated XML
technicalNotes: File: lhdn_payroll_integration/services/payload_builder.py
Add _add_invoice_period(root, start_date, end_date) helper
Call after IssueTime in build_salary_slip_xml() and build_expense_claim_xml()

userStories.title: Fix PCB detection to use custom_is_pcb_component flag
userStories.description: Hardcoded PCB_COMPONENT_NAMES frozenset used instead of custom_is_pcb_component flag on Salary Component. Non-English PCB component names (e.g. 'Cukai Pendapatan') silently excluded from WithholdingTaxTotal.
acceptanceCriteria: PCB detection checks frappe.db.get_value('Salary Component', name, 'custom_is_pcb_component') == 1 OR name in fallback frozenset
Hardcoded set retained as fallback with deprecation comment
Test with custom-named PCB component verifies inclusion in WithholdingTaxTotal
technicalNotes: File: lhdn_payroll_integration/services/payload_builder.py
Replace name in PCB_COMPONENT_NAMES with: frappe.db.get_value('Salary Component', d.salary_component, 'custom_is_pcb_component') == 1 or d.salary_component in PCB_COMPONENT_NAMES

userStories.title: Fix credit note to use LHDN TIN not ERPNext employee code for Supplier
userStories.description: Credit note AccountingSupplierParty/ID uses doc.employee (ERPNext internal code like EMP-0001) instead of employee.custom_lhdn_tin. LHDN rejects credit notes where supplier TIN does not match original invoice. PostalAddress and PartyTaxScheme also missing from credit note party elements.
acceptanceCriteria: Credit note Supplier party uses employee.custom_lhdn_tin for cbc:ID
PostalAddress and PartyTaxScheme added to both Supplier and Buyer parties matching _build_invoice_skeleton()
Test verifies credit note XML supplier ID matches LHDN TIN
technicalNotes: File: lhdn_payroll_integration/services/credit_note_service.py
Load employee = frappe.get_doc('Employee', doc.employee) and use employee.custom_lhdn_tin
Call _add_postal_address() and _add_party_tax_scheme() on both supplier and buyer party elements

userStories.title: Fix consolidated invoice to use eligible earnings not net_pay
userStories.description: build_consolidated_xml() uses raw doc.net_pay for line amounts. Individual invoices filter to eligible earnings (excluding employer statutory + custom-excluded components). Creates reconciliation mismatches.
acceptanceCriteria: Line amount = sum of eligible earnings excluding EMPLOYER_STATUTORY_COMPONENTS and custom_lhdn_exclude_from_invoice components
Test confirms consolidated line amount matches sum of individual invoice line amounts for same documents
technicalNotes: File: lhdn_payroll_integration/services/payload_builder.py
In build_consolidated_xml() replace _quantize(doc.net_pay) with same eligible-earnings filter logic as build_salary_slip_xml()

userStories.title: Generate CP39 monthly PCB remittance file
userStories.description: CP39 is mandatory monthly employer PCB remittance format for LHDN's e-PCB portal. Every employer with PCB deductions must submit monthly. Not present anywhere in the app.
acceptanceCriteria: New Script Report cp39_pcb_remittance with filters: Company, Month, Year
Columns: Employee TIN, IC/Passport Number, Employee Name, Gross Salary, PCB Amount, Period
CSV export in LHDN e-PCB portal compatible format
Sources only submitted Salary Slips (docstatus=1) with PCB deduction > 0
Test verifies output columns and row count
technicalNotes: New files: lhdn_payroll_integration/report/cp39_pcb_remittance/cp39_pcb_remittance.json + .py
Query tabSalary Slip joined to tabSalary Detail for PCB deductions
Report type: Script, is_standard: Yes

userStories.title: Generate EA Form (Borang EA) for employees
userStories.description: EA Form is mandatory annual employee tax statement that every employer must provide to employees by 28 February each year. Not present in the app.
acceptanceCriteria: New Script Report ea_form with filters: Company, Employee (optional), Year
Aggregates all submitted Salary Slips for the year per employee
Output fields: Total Gross Remuneration, EPF Employee, SOCSO Employee, EIS Employee, PCB Total, Net Pay
HTML Print Format renders as LHDN-compatible EA Form layout
Test verifies aggregation accuracy across 12 months
technicalNotes: New files: lhdn_payroll_integration/report/ea_form/ea_form.json + .py
New print format: lhdn_payroll_integration/print_format/ea_form/ea_form.json + .html
Aggregate by employee across all salary slips for the year using SUM on deduction/earning amounts

userStories.title: Generate Borang E (Form E) employer annual return
userStories.description: Borang E is mandatory employer annual return to LHDN due 31 March each year (Income Tax Act 1967, Section 83). Non-compliance carries penalty under Section 120. Company-level summary of all PCB, headcount, and total remuneration.
acceptanceCriteria: New Script Report borang_e with filters: Company, Year
Output: company details, total employees, total gross remuneration, total PCB withheld, total EPF employer, total SOCSO employer
CP8D employee list (per-employee annual income + PCB) included as sub-table
Test verifies company-level totals match sum of individual EA Form data
technicalNotes: New files: lhdn_payroll_integration/report/borang_e/borang_e.json + .py
Aggregate all ea_form data across all employees for the company and year
Include per-employee breakdown as CP8D sub-section

userStories.title: Generate EPF Borang A monthly contribution schedule
userStories.description: EPF (KWSP) Borang A is mandatory monthly contribution remittance form listing each employee's contribution. No EPF reporting exists. Required for all employers registered with EPF.
acceptanceCriteria: New Script Report epf_borang_a with filters: Company, Month, Year
Columns: Employee Name, NRIC, EPF Member Number, Wages, Employee EPF, Employer EPF, Total
New field custom_epf_member_number (Data) on Employee
CSV export compatible with EPF i-Akaun upload format
Test verifies contribution amounts per employee
technicalNotes: New files: lhdn_payroll_integration/report/epf_borang_a/epf_borang_a.json + .py
Add custom_epf_member_number (Data) to custom_field.json on Employee
Query tabSalary Slip + tabSalary Detail filtered by EPF component names

userStories.title: Generate SOCSO Borang 8A monthly contribution schedule
userStories.description: SOCSO (PERKESO) Borang 8A is mandatory monthly contribution schedule. No SOCSO reporting exists in the app.
acceptanceCriteria: New Script Report socso_borang_8a with filters: Company, Month, Year
Columns: Employee Name, NRIC, SOCSO Number, Wages, Employee SOCSO, Employer SOCSO, Total
New field custom_socso_member_number (Data) on Employee
Test verifies amounts sourced from SOCSO deduction lines
technicalNotes: New files: lhdn_payroll_integration/report/socso_borang_8a/socso_borang_8a.json + .py
Add custom_socso_member_number (Data) to custom_field.json on Employee
Filter for SOCSO Employee and SOCSO - Employer salary components

userStories.title: Generate EIS monthly contribution report
userStories.description: EIS (Sistem Insurans Pekerjaan) monthly contributions filed separately via EIS portal. Employer must submit monthly schedule. No EIS report exists.
acceptanceCriteria: New Script Report eis_monthly with filters: Company, Month, Year
Columns: Employee Name, NRIC, Wages, EIS Employee, EIS Employer, Total
Sources data from submitted Salary Slips with EIS deduction/earning lines
Test verifies correct EIS amounts per employee
technicalNotes: New files: lhdn_payroll_integration/report/eis_monthly/eis_monthly.json + .py

userStories.title: Add bonus/irregular payment classification for PCB differentiation
userStories.description: LHDN and PCB rules treat irregular payments (bonus, commissions, gratuity) differently from regular salary for PCB calculation. acceptanceCriteria: New field custom_is_irregular_payment (Check) on Salary Component
pcb_calculator.py accepts bonus_amount parameter; applies one-twelfth annualisation rule for bonus PCB per LHDN Schedule
Test verifies bonus PCB calculation differs from regular salary PCB for same income
technicalNotes: Files: custom_field.json, services/pcb_calculator.py
Add custom_is_irregular_payment (Check, default 0) to Salary Component in custom_field.json
In calculate_pcb(): if bonus_amount provided, annualise as (monthly_income * 12 + bonus_amount) then compute tax; subtract tax on monthly_income*12 alone

userStories.title: Add non-resident flat-rate 30% tax flag and handling
userStories.description: Non-resident employees (present in Malaysia fewer than 182 days in a calendar year) are taxed at flat 30%. No custom_is_non_resident flag and no flat-rate calculation path exists.
acceptanceCriteria: New field custom_is_non_resident (Check) on Employee; distinct from custom_is_foreign_worker
pcb_calculator.py returns 30% flat rate when resident=False
PCB validation warning on Salary Slip uses flat rate for non-residents
Test covers non-resident 30% calculation vs progressive scale
technicalNotes: Files: custom_field.json, services/pcb_calculator.py
Add custom_is_non_resident (Check, default 0) to Employee in custom_field.json
In calculate_pcb(): if not resident return round(annual_income * 0.30, 2) / 12

userStories.title: Distinguish director fee vs director salary at invoice level
userStories.description: LHDN treats director fees (board service) and director salary (executive role) differently. Both use single custom_worker_type = Director with no sub-classification. Default classification code 036 applied uniformly.
acceptanceCriteria: New field custom_director_payment_type (Select: Director Salary/Director Fee) on Employee, visible only when custom_worker_type = Director
get_default_classification_code() returns 036 for Director Fee, 004 for Director Salary
Test verifies correct classification codes per director payment type in generated XML
technicalNotes: Files: custom_field.json, services/exemption_filter.py
Add custom_director_payment_type with depends_on: eval:doc.custom_worker_type=='Director'
Update _WORKER_TYPE_CLASSIFICATION to check director sub-type from employee record

userStories.title: Add submission failure email notification for HR Manager
userStories.description: When Salary Slip marked Invalid after LHDN rejection, no notification reaches HR managers. Silent failures can result in months of non-compliance before detection.
acceptanceCriteria: _write_response_to_doc() sends frappe.sendmail() to all users with role HR Manager when status set to Invalid
Email includes: document name, employee name, first 500 chars of error log, direct link to document

userStories.title: Fix status poller to use per-document company for base_url and token

userStories.title: Add PCB Category Code (1/2/3) on Employee with CP39 and EA Form integration
userStories.description: LHDN requires three distinct PCB category codes: Category 1 (single or married with working spouse), Category 2 (married non-working spouse Ã¢â‚¬â€� RM4,000 spouse relief), Category 3 (single parent). The current calculator uses married=True/False which does not handle Category 3, nor transmit the category in CP39 or EA Form. LHDN CP39 CSV upload format requires a PCB Category column. Wrong category causes systematic under/over-deduction triggering penalties under ITA Section 107C(10) of RM200-RM20,000 per offence.

userStories.title: Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration
userStories.description: LHDN Borang TP1 allows employees to declare 20+ relief categories to reduce monthly PCB. The current PCB calculator hardcodes only personal relief (RM9,000), spouse (RM4,000), and child relief. All other declared reliefs Ã¢â‚¬â€� life insurance, medical insurance, education fees, SSPN, childcare, lifestyle, PRS, serious illness, parents medical, disability, SOCSO employee, EPF employee Ã¢â‚¬â€� are ignored, causing systematic PCB over-deduction. Regulatory basis: ITA Rules 1994 (IT(P)(E) Rules), Rule 6A.

userStories.title: Implement Zakat PCB Offset (Ringgit-for-Ringgit Deduction)
userStories.description: ITA 1967 Section 6A(3): Zakat reduces PCB payable ringgit-for-ringgit (not just from chargeable income). Net PCB = Gross PCB - (annual_zakat / 12). The app module docstring correctly documents this formula but the implementation does not apply it. Zakat has a dedicated column in LHDN CP39 CSV upload that must be populated. Failure to offset means Muslim employees are over-taxed via PCB.
acceptanceCriteria: Add custom_annual_zakat (Currency) field on Employee
calculate_pcb(... , annual_zakat=0) subtracts annual_zakat/12 from calculated monthly PCB; result floored at 0
CP39 report CSV includes Zakat Amount column per employee row
EA Form Section C5 reflects total annual Zakat
Tests verify Zakat offset reduces PCB correctly; PCB never goes negative
technicalNotes: Files: fixtures/custom_field.json, services/pcb_calculator.py, report/cp39_pcb_remittance/cp39_pcb_remittance.py, report/ea_form/ea_form.py
Add custom_annual_zakat Currency field to Employee in custom_field.json
In calculate_pcb(): monthly_zakat = annual_zakat / 12; net_pcb = max(0, monthly_pcb - monthly_zakat)
CP39 query must JOIN Employee to get annual_zakat for each row

userStories.title: Implement CP38 Additional Deduction Fields and Payroll Integration
userStories.description: LHDN issues CP38 notices directing employers to deduct additional PCB above normal MTD to recover underpaid tax (ITA Section 107(1)(b)). Non-compliance makes employer personally liable for undeducted amount plus 10% surcharge (ITA Section 107(3A)). The CP38 amount is fixed per employee per month, has a notice reference number and an expiry date. No CP38 fields exist anywhere in the app.

Issuing an incomplete EA Form is a criminal offence under ITA Section 120(1)(b) carrying fines up to RM20,000 or imprisonment.

userStories.title: Update Minimum Wage Validation to RM1,700 (Feb 2025 Amendment)
userStories.description: The Minimum Wages Order (Amendment) 2025 increased the national minimum wage to RM1,700/month (RM8.17/hour) effective 1 February 2025 for companies with 5+ employees, and 1 August 2025 for all remaining companies. Previous rate was RM1,500/month. No minimum wage check currently exists. Paying below minimum wage is a criminal offence under Employment Act Section 99J with fines up to RM10,000 per contravention.

userStories.title: Apply RM400 Personal and Spouse Tax Rebates in PCB Calculator
userStories.description: ITA 1967 Section 6A provides tax rebates that directly reduce computed tax payable: RM400 personal rebate for residents with chargeable income <= RM35,000, and RM400 spouse rebate for married individuals (Category 2/3) with same threshold. For majority of Malaysian employees (median income well below RM35,000/year), PCB is systematically overstated by up to RM800/year because calculate_pcb() does not apply these rebates.
acceptanceCriteria: After computing annual tax from progressive scale: apply annual_tax = max(0, annual_tax - 400) when chargeable_income <= 35000
Apply additional max(0, annual_tax - 400) when category in (2, 3) and chargeable_income <= 35000
Monthly PCB = adjusted annual_tax / 12
Tests: employee RM30,000/year chargeable income Ã¢â‚¬â€� verify RM800 total rebate applied (Category 2); employee RM36,000/year Ã¢â‚¬â€� verify no rebate
technicalNotes: File: services/pcb_calculator.py
After computing annual_tax from progressive bands, apply personal rebate then spouse rebate
PERSONAL_REBATE = 400; REBATE_INCOME_LIMIT = 35000
Logic: if chargeable_income <= REBATE_INCOME_LIMIT: annual_tax = max(0, annual_tax - PERSONAL_REBATE); if category in [2,3]: annual_tax = max(0, annual_tax - PERSONAL_REBATE)

userStories.title: Implement MTD Method 2 (Year-to-Date Recalculation Formula)
userStories.description: LHDN Computerised PCB guidelines mandate Method 2 for payroll software. Method 2 computes PCB based on actual YTD income: MTD_n = [(P-M)*R+B]/(n+1) - (X/n) where P=annualised YTD income, M=band floor, R=marginal rate, B=tax on lower bands, n=remaining months, X=PCB already deducted YTD. This is more accurate than Method 1 for employees with variable income. Regulatory basis: LHDN PCB Guidelines, Appendix D.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Company-level custom_pcb_method Select (Method 1 / Method 2) with default Method 2
New calculate_pcb_method2(current_month_gross, ytd_gross, ytd_pcb_deducted, tp1_reliefs, category, month_number, annual_zakat) function
Salary Slip custom fields: custom_ytd_gross (Currency, read-only), custom_ytd_pcb_deducted (Currency, read-only) Ã¢â‚¬â€� auto-populated from prior submitted slips
before_submit hook populates YTD fields by querying submitted slips for same employee, same year, earlier months
Tests: verify Method 2 gives same annual tax as Method 1 for constant-income employee; verify Method 2 smooths PCB for variable-income employee
technicalNotes: Files: services/pcb_calculator.py, fixtures/custom_field.json, hooks.py
Add custom_pcb_method to Company; add custom_ytd_gross and custom_ytd_pcb_deducted to Salary Slip
calculate_pcb_method2(): annualised_income = ytd_gross * (12/month_number); then apply Method 2 formula
YTD query: frappe.db.sql('SELECT SUM(gross_pay), SUM(pcb_deducted) FROM tabSalary Slip WHERE employee=%s AND year=%s AND end_date<%s AND docstatus=1')

userStories.title: Implement Benefits-in-Kind (BIK) Prescribed Value Calculation Module
userStories.description: BIK provided by employers is taxable employment income under ITA Section 13(1)(b). LHDN prescribes specific annual values for common BIK items (Public Ruling No. 3/2013 updated 2019). Company car (RM1,200-RM50,000/year by price bracket), fuel (RM300/month), driver (RM600/month), accommodation (30% of gross income), club memberships (actual fee). BIK omission understates chargeable income and PCB Ã¢â‚¬â€� LHDN audits routinely identify this.

userStories.title: Implement Perquisite Exemption Thresholds on Salary Components
userStories.description: Certain perquisites are partially exempt from tax under ITA Section 13(1)(a) and Public Ruling No. 5/2019. Key exemptions: petrol/car allowance up to RM6,000/year (business use), childcare up to RM2,400/year, mobile phone handset (1 unit, fully exempt), group insurance premiums (wholly exempt), medical/dental/optical (wholly exempt). Currently PCB calculator adds full allowance amounts to income without applying exemption ceilings, overstating taxable income.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to Salary Component custom_field.json: custom_exemption_type Select (None/Transport/Childcare/Group Insurance/Medical/Mobile Phone/Other), custom_annual_exemption_ceiling (Currency, 0=unlimited)

userStories.title: Implement CP107 DocType for Foreign Employee Tax Clearance Workflow
userStories.description: When a non-citizen employee ceases employment, employer must withhold final month remuneration and apply for Tax Clearance Letter via CP107 before releasing payment. Existing CP21 DocType handles departure notification but does not implement the CP107 withholding workflow.

userStories.title: Implement Ordinary Rate of Pay (ORP) Calculator and Overtime Validation
userStories.description: Employment Act 1955 Section 60A(3) mandates OT rates: 1.5x normal day, 2.0x rest day (full day), 3.0x public holiday. These apply to employees earning <=RM4,000/month. The Ordinary Rate of Pay (ORP) = Monthly Salary / 26 (daily) or / contracted_hours (hourly). No ORP calculation or OT validation exists. OT underpayment is both an Employment Act offence and creates PCB under-deduction.
acceptanceCriteria: calculate_orp(monthly_salary, contracted_hours_per_month=None) utility in utils/employment_compliance.py

userStories.title: Implement Foreign Worker Levy Tracking (FWCMS / Multi-Tier Levy Model)
userStories.description: Foreign Workers Levy Act 2021 requires annual FWCMS levy per foreign worker (RM410-RM2,500/year depending on sector and nationality). Multi-Tier Levy Model (MTLM) effective January 2025 sets rates based on local-to-foreign worker ratio. Non-payment leads to immigration enforcement. custom_is_foreign_worker exists but is only used for e-invoice TIN substitution. Levy obligations are not tracked anywhere.

userStories.title: Implement Payroll Bank Disbursement File Generator (Maybank + CIMB + DuitNow)
userStories.description: A payroll system without bank disbursement file generation forces manual bank transfers, making the system impractical for payroll operations. Malaysian payroll files must conform to bank portal formats. The PayNet DuitNow Bulk ISO 20022 pain.001.001.03 format with SALA purpose code is the emerging standard (PayNet migration deadline November 2025). Maybank M2E and CIMB BizChannel are the two highest market share legacy formats.
acceptanceCriteria: New DocType Payroll Bank Disbursement linked to Payroll Entry with bank Select (Maybank/CIMB/Public Bank/RHB/DuitNow Bulk), disbursement_date, total_amount
Add Employee fields: custom_bank_name Select, custom_bank_code (8-digit PayNet code), custom_account_type (Savings/Current)
generate_bank_file(payroll_entry_name, bank) service returns file bytes for download
Maybank format: pipe-delimited Name|IC|Account|Amount with 5-digit org code header
CIMB format: CSV with Header/Detail/Footer structure
Generate Payroll Entry Generate Bank File button visible to Payroll Officer role
Tests: Maybank file has correct pipe format; CIMB has correct Header/Detail/Footer
technicalNotes: New files: services/bank_disbursement_service.py, doctype/payroll_bank_disbursement/
Add custom_bank_name, custom_bank_code, custom_account_type to Employee in custom_field.json
Maybank M2E format: pipe-delimited, ORG_CODE|PAY_DATE|NAME|NRIC|ACCOUNT|AMOUNT per line
CIMB: HEADER: H|date|org_code; DETAIL: D|name|account|amount; FOOTER: T|count|total
Add custom_maybank_org_code and custom_cimb_org_code to Company

userStories.title: Fix HRDF Levy Rate Ã¢â‚¬â€� 1% Mandatory for All Employers with 10+ Employees
userStories.description: The current custom_hrdf_levy_rate field offers '0.5% for 10-49 employees, 1.0% for 50+' which is factually incorrect. HRD Corp regulations (HRD Act 2001 amended 2021) prescribe 1% for ALL employers with 10+ Malaysian employees in mandatory sectors regardless of headcount above 10. The 0.5% voluntary option only applies to companies with 5-9 employees choosing to join voluntarily. Systematic underpayment at 0.5% creates HRD Corp surcharge liability.
acceptanceCriteria: Update custom_hrdf_levy_rate options to: '0.5% (Voluntary Ã¢â‚¬â€� 5-9 employees)' and '1.0% (Mandatory Ã¢â‚¬â€� 10+ employees)'
Add custom_hrdf_mandatory_sector (Check) on Company Ã¢â‚¬â€� enforce 1% when checked and headcount >= 10
hrdf_monthly_levy.py warns if the levy rate in use does not match mandatory rate for company headcount
Tests: 10-employee company in mandatory sector uses 1%; 7-employee company allows 0.5%
technicalNotes: Files: fixtures/custom_field.json, report/hrdf_monthly_levy/hrdf_monthly_levy.py
Update the options string for custom_hrdf_levy_rate on Company
Add custom_hrdf_mandatory_sector Check field on Company
In report: compare custom_hrdf_levy_rate against expected rate based on employee count and mandatory_sector flag

userStories.title: Enforce EPF Employer Rate Differential (13% for Salary <=RM5,000, 12% for >RM5,000)
userStories.description: EPF employer contribution rate: 13% for employees earning <=RM5,000/month, 12% for earnings >RM5,000/month (EPF Contribution Rate Revision 2022). Current fixture seeds a single EPF - Employer component with no rate differential logic. Employers paying 12% for employees below RM5,000 are underpaying EPF Ã¢â‚¬â€� KWSP can impose late payment dividend surcharge under EPF Act Section 45.
acceptanceCriteria: New calculate_epf_employer_rate(monthly_gross) in utils/statutory_rates.py returns 0.13 if gross <= 5000, else 0.12
EPF Borang A report validates employer EPF amount against correct rate per employee; flags discrepancies
Salary component validation hook warns when EPF - Employer component deviates from statutory rate by >5%
Tests: employee at RM5,000 Ã¢â€ â€™ employer 13%; employee at RM5,001 Ã¢â€ â€™ employer 12%

EPF_LOWER_SALARY_THRESHOLD = 5000

userStories.title: Implement SOCSO Contribution Bracketed Table Lookup (Jadual Kadar Caruman)
userStories.description: SOCSO contributions are NOT a straight percentage Ã¢â‚¬â€� they are fixed amounts determined by wage bracket per SOCSO First Schedule (Jadual Kadar Caruman), with 72 wage brackets from RM0 to RM6,000+. Ceiling wage is RM6,000/month (updated October 2024). Using wrong contribution amounts risks both under- and over-deduction. SOCSO Borang 8A currently passes through whatever amounts were entered without validation.
acceptanceCriteria: calculate_socso_contribution(wages, scheme='both') in utils/statutory_rates.py implements the First Schedule table; returns {'employee': x, 'employer': y}
Wage ceiling capped at RM6,000 (updated from RM5,000 Ã¢â‚¬â€� October 2024 amendment)
SOCSO Borang 8A validation warns if reported SOCSO amounts deviate >5% from scheduled amounts
Tests: wages at RM1,500, RM3,000, RM5,500, RM6,000, RM6,001 return correct scheduled amounts
technicalNotes: File: utils/statutory_rates.py
Embed SOCSO_TABLE as dict of wage ranges to (employee_amount, employer_amount) tuples
SOCSO_WAGE_CEILING = 6000 (updated October 2024)
calculate_socso_contribution(wages): wages = min(wages, SOCSO_WAGE_CEILING); find bracket; return amounts

userStories.title: Enforce EIS Contribution Ceiling (RM6,000) and Age/Foreign Worker Exemptions
userStories.description: EIS (SIP) under EIS Act 2017 Second Schedule: 0.2% employee + 0.2% employer on insured wages capped at RM6,000/month. Employees aged <18 or >=60 are exempt. Foreign workers are NOT covered. Ceiling updated October 2024 (aligned with SOCSO). These exemptions and updated ceiling are not enforced Ã¢â‚¬â€� EIS monthly report passes through whatever salary component amounts were entered.
acceptanceCriteria: calculate_eis_contribution(wages, date_of_birth, is_foreign) in utils/statutory_rates.py: returns 0 if foreign or age<18 or age>=60; else min(wages, 6000) * 0.002
EIS monthly report validates amounts against calculation; flags exempt employees incorrectly included or wrong ceiling
Tests: foreign worker Ã¢â€ â€™ 0; age 17 Ã¢â€ â€™ 0; age 60 Ã¢â€ â€™ 0; wages RM7,000 Ã¢â€ â€™ EIS on RM6,000 only
technicalNotes: File: utils/statutory_rates.py (extend from US-073/US-074)
EIS_WAGE_CEILING = 6000; EIS_RATE = 0.002
Age calculation: from datetime import date; age = (payroll_date - date_of_birth).days // 365
report/eis_monthly/eis_monthly.py: validate each row against calculate_eis_contribution()

userStories.title: Implement Age-Based EPF/SOCSO/EIS Statutory Rate Transitions at Age 60
userStories.description: At age 60, statutory contribution rules change: EPF employee rate changes to 5.5% statutory (or 0% minimum elected); EPF employer rate drops to 4%; SOCSO coverage ceases; EIS coverage ceases. The system has no age-tracking logic Ã¢â‚¬â€� employees who turn 60 mid-year will have wrong deductions until manually corrected. Regulatory basis: EPF Act 1991 Third Schedule; SOCSO Act 1969; EIS Act 2017.
acceptanceCriteria: get_statutory_rates_for_employee(employee_name, payroll_date) in utils/statutory_rates.py returns correct EPF/SOCSO/EIS rates based on employee age at payroll date

Dashboard alert on Employee record when employee is within 3 months of turning 60

technicalNotes: File: utils/statutory_rates.py (extend), hooks.py
EPF_OVER_60_EMPLOYEE_RATE = 0.055; EPF_OVER_60_EMPLOYER_RATE = 0.04
In get_statutory_rates_for_employee(): get employee.date_of_birth, calculate age, return rate dict
Daily scheduler: check_approaching_age_60() Ã¢â‚¬â€� query employees where date_of_birth within 90 days of 60th birthday

userStories.title: Implement TP3 Carry-Forward Declaration for New Hires (Prior Employer YTD)
userStories.description: When employee joins mid-year having worked for a previous employer, PCB calculation must account for income and PCB already deducted by the previous employer. Without TP3 data, new employer's calculator starts from zero, understating annualised income and under-deducting PCB. Employee submits Borang TP3 to new employer with prior income and PCB details. Regulatory basis: LHDN TP3 form.
acceptanceCriteria: New DocType Employee TP3 Declaration: employee, tax_year, previous_employer_name, previous_employer_tin, prior_gross_income, prior_epf_deducted, prior_pcb_deducted, joining_month
calculate_pcb()/calculate_pcb_method2() accepts tp3_prior_gross and tp3_prior_pcb; adds prior income to YTD for annualisation; subtracts prior PCB from YTD PCB deducted
CP22 workflow triggers reminder to collect TP3 when joining month is not January
Tests: employee joining July with RM30,000 prior income Ã¢â‚¬â€� annualised income uses combined figure
technicalNotes: New files: doctype/employee_tp3_declaration/
get_tp3_for_employee(employee, tax_year) returns prior_gross and prior_pcb for the year
In calculate_pcb_method2(): ytd_gross += tp3_prior_gross; ytd_pcb_deducted += tp3_prior_pcb

userStories.title: Extend CP8D with Income Type Breakdown (Bonus, Commission, Gratuity, Other)
userStories.description: LHDN CP8D e-Filing specification (2024 revision) requires separate columns for income sub-categories: Total Gross Income, Gross Bonus/Commission, Gross Gratuity, Other Income, Total EPF, Total PCB. Current CP8D reports submit only three income figures. Incomplete CP8D data can trigger LHDN queries on employer submissions.

Covers contractors and commission agents who are NOT employees. Relevant for companies with sales agent networks or referral programs.

userStories.title: Track Maternity/Paternity Leave and Validate Maternity Pay Rate
userStories.description: Employment Act 1955 Section 37 (A1651): 98 consecutive days maternity leave. Section 60FA: 7 consecutive days paternity leave for up to 5 live births. Maternity allowance must be paid at Ordinary Rate of Pay. System has no mechanism to track leave taken, validate payment, or alert HR when limits are approached. Underpaying maternity allowance is an Employment Act offence.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to Employee: custom_maternity_leave_taken (Int, cumulative days), custom_paternity_leave_taken (Int), custom_paternity_births_claimed (Int, max 5)

Payroll Compliance report adds Leave Compliance section flagging over-entitlement or underpayment
Tests: maternity pay below ORP triggers warning; days >98 triggers warning; paternity claims >5 births triggers warning
technicalNotes: Files: fixtures/custom_field.json, utils/employment_compliance.py
MATERNITY_LEAVE_DAYS = 98; PATERNITY_LEAVE_DAYS = 7; MAX_PATERNITY_BIRTHS = 5
validate_maternity_pay(): get ORP via calculate_orp(employee.basic_salary); check maternity component >= ORP * days

userStories.title: Implement Working Hours Compliance Check (45-Hour Weekly Limit)
userStories.description: Employment Act 1955 Section 60A(1) post-2022 amendment: maximum 45 hours per week (reduced from 48). Excessive OT can cause total monthly hours to breach legal limits. Penalty for exceeding working hours: Employment Act offence. No automated check exists in the system.
acceptanceCriteria: validate_weekly_hours(salary_slip) calculates total hours (contracted + OT) and warns if any implied week exceeds 45 hours

userStories.title: Implement Termination and Lay-Off Benefits Calculator
userStories.description: Employment (Termination and Lay-Off Benefits) Regulations 1980: statutory minimum termination payment is 10 days wages per year of service for <2 years, 15 days for 2-5 years, 20 days for >5 years. Without a calculator, HR may inadvertently underpay, creating Employment Act liability. Currently CP22A DocType has no benefits calculation.
acceptanceCriteria: calculate_termination_benefits(employee, termination_date) in utils/employment_compliance.py: calculates years of service, determines rate, returns statutory_minimum

CP22A DocType extended with: years_of_service (Float, auto-calculated), statutory_minimum_termination_pay (Currency, auto-populated), actual_termination_pay (Currency, manual), underpayment_warning (Read Only)

technicalNotes: Files: utils/employment_compliance.py, doctype/lhdn_cp22a/lhdn_cp22a.py
TERMINATION_RATE = {2: 10, 5: 15, 999: 20} Ã¢â‚¬â€� days per year of service by service bracket
years_of_service = (termination_date - employee.date_of_joining).days / 365

userStories.title: Implement Expatriate Gross-Up Calculator and DTA Country Table
userStories.description: Expatriates on tax-equalised packages require iterative gross-up to find gross salary that produces desired net after Malaysian tax. The 182-day residency rule determines resident vs non-resident treatment. Double Tax Agreement (DTA) countries (Singapore, UK, US, Australia) may provide treaty exemptions. No gross-up calculation or DTA lookup exists.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to Employee: custom_dta_country Select (ISO country codes with DTA agreements), custom_is_tax_equalised (Check), custom_malaysia_presence_days (Int, YTD)
calculate_gross_up(desired_net, annual_reliefs, category, max_iterations=50) iterative solver in services/expatriate_service.py
DTA country list with key treaty provisions (183-day rule) stored as fixture or system setting
Tests: gross-up for net RM10,000/month converges to correct gross; residency test flags non-resident at <182 days
technicalNotes: New file: services/expatriate_service.py
Iterative solver: start with gross = desired_net / (1 - marginal_rate); iterate until |computed_net - desired_net| < 0.01
DTA_COUNTRIES dict: {country_code: {treaty_rate, days_threshold, notes}}
custom_malaysia_presence_days on Employee tracks YTD days for 182-day residency rule

userStories.title: Implement ESOS/Share Option Gain Calculation and EA Form B10
userStories.description: ITA 1967 Section 25 and Public Ruling No. 1/2021: gains from ESOS/ESPP exercise are taxable employment income in year of exercise. Gain = (Market Price on exercise date - Exercise Price) x shares exercised. Must be included in annual income for PCB and disclosed in EA Form Section B10. No ESOS module exists.
acceptanceCriteria: New DocType Employee Share Option Exercise: employee, grant_date, exercise_date, exercise_price, market_price_on_exercise, shares_exercised, taxable_gain (auto-calculated)

technicalNotes: New files: doctype/employee_share_option_exercise/
taxable_gain = (market_price_on_exercise - exercise_price) * shares_exercised
Add to PCB calculation: get_esos_gain_for_month(employee, month, year) and treat as bonus income
EA Form query: SELECT SUM(taxable_gain) FROM employee_share_option_exercise WHERE employee=%s AND YEAR(exercise_date)=%s

userStories.title: Implement Approved Pension Scheme Full Retirement Gratuity Exemption
userStories.description: ITA 1967 Schedule 6 paragraph 30: retirement gratuity from an approved company pension scheme for employee retiring at age 55 (or compulsory retirement at 60) is FULLY exempt. Current pcb_calculator.py only implements partial RM1,000/year exemption (para 25), missing full exemption for approved scheme retirees.

userStories.title: Assess and Implement XAdES XML Digital Signature for MyInvois Self-Billed Phase 2
userStories.description: MyInvois e-Invoice Guidelines (LHDN SDK v3.x) state API submissions must use XAdES standard with RSA-SHA256 and certificates from MSC Trustgate or DigiCert Malaysia. Phase 2 implementors (RM25M-RM100M revenue, mandatory from January 2025) are now live. If digital signature is required for self-billed payroll e-invoices, payload_builder.py which emits no ds:Signature element will produce non-compliant XML.
acceptanceCriteria: Add custom_enable_xml_signature (Check, default off) on Company and custom_digital_cert_path (Data), custom_digital_cert_password (Password)

technicalNotes: New file: utils/xml_signer.py
Try import xmlsec; if not available, log warning and skip signing
XAdES BeS: C14N canonicalization + RSA-SHA256; include SignedProperties in signature

userStories.title: Build Employee Self-Service Portal for Payslips, EA Forms, and TP1 Submission
userStories.description: Employment Act 1955 Section 25A requires employers to provide wage slips. A self-service portal reduces HR workload for payslip distribution and EA form issuance. Employees should be able to view payslips, download EA Forms, and submit TP1 relief declarations online without emailing HR.

userStories.title: Implement PCB Change Audit Trail DocType

userStories.title: Generate DuitNow Bulk Payroll File (ISO 20022 pain.001.001.03 with SALA Purpose Code)
userStories.description: PayNet hard migration deadline for ISO 20022 on H2H/SFTP gateways is November 2025. CIMB, Maybank, and RHB are upgrading gateways to XML-native by Q4 2025.

Tests: statutory employer generates EC Form headers; non-statutory employer uses EA Form
technicalNotes: New files: report/ec_form/ec_form.json + ec_form.py
EC Form: reuse same ea_form.py logic but substitute EC-specific header text
Add custom_is_statutory_employer Check to Company in custom_field.json

userStories.title: Implement Multi-Tier Levy Model (MTLM) Rate Calculation for Foreign Workers
userStories.description: Multi-Tier Levy Model (MTLM) effective January 2025 sets foreign worker levy rates based on employer dependency ratio (local:foreign worker ratio). Higher dependency = higher levy rate. Tier 1 (low dependency): RM410/year; Tier 2 (medium): RM1,230/year; Tier 3 (high): RM2,500/year. Extends the base foreign worker levy tracking in US-070.
acceptanceCriteria: calculate_fw_levy_tier(local_headcount, foreign_headcount, sector) utility returning levy rate per worker
Foreign Worker Levy report shows tier calculation and total annual levy liability
Tests: dependency ratio above Tier 3 threshold returns highest rate
technicalNotes: File: doctype/foreign_worker_levy_payment/ (extend from US-070)
dependency_ratio = foreign_headcount / (local_headcount + foreign_headcount)
Add to Company: custom_local_employee_count and custom_foreign_employee_count for MTLM calculation

userStories.title: Fix CSRF token acquisition in test suite ERPNextSession login
userStories.description: All 28 POST-based tests fail with HTTP 400 CSRFTokenError because ERPNextSession._login() in tests/base.py does not properly obtain the Frappe session CSRF token after login. The login endpoint returns HTTP 200 but does NOT include the CSRF token in its response body or headers. The token is only available by parsing frappe.boot from the /app page. All 28 affected tests use session.api() (POST) and receive 400 CSRFTokenError instead of the expected 200. Affected: PA-01,02,04,05,07,09,10,11,12, LP-06,18, AC-01,02,05,07, REG-05,11, INT-05,06,07,09,10,11, EC-12, PF-07,08,10,14.
acceptanceCriteria: After _login(), self._csrf_token is a non-empty string
All 28 previously-failing POST tests return 200 (not 400)
Method: after POST /api/method/login, GET /app and extract csrf_token from boot JSON via regex
Fallback: read X-Frappe-CSRF-Token response header if boot parse fails
Concurrent thread safety: _csrf_token is read-only after login, no lock needed
Smoke + unit:prisma_ai suites pass after fix
technicalNotes: File: tests/base.py Ã¢â‚¬â€� ERPNextSession._login() method
After login POST, add: boot_resp = self.session.get(f'{self.base_url}/app')
Parse CSRF: import re; m = re.search(r'"csrf_token"\s*:\s*"([a-f0-9]{32})"', boot_resp.text); self._csrf_token = m.group(1) if m else ''
Add 'import re' at top of tests/base.py
Verify: .venv-tests/Scripts/python.exe tests/run_tests.py --suite unit:prisma_ai

userStories.title: Fix fields parameter encoding in ERPNextSession.resource() helper
userStories.description: 4 tests fail because GET /api/resource/<Doctype>?fields=[...] only returns the name field, ignoring the specified fields list. Affected: AC-11 (Salary Slip employee/gross_pay), AC-15 (Report report_type), AC-16 (Company custom_company_tin_number), INT-04 (Salary Slip custom_lhdn_status). Root cause: the requests library URL-encodes the fields JSON array incorrectly, or Frappe's REST resource endpoint doesn't parse it as expected. Fix: rewrite affected tests to use frappe.client.get_list API instead of /api/resource, which has a more reliable field filtering contract. Additionally, AC-16 should skip gracefully if custom_company_tin_number is absent (myinvois_erpgulf may not be in all environments).
acceptanceCriteria: AC-11: Salary Slip records include employee and gross_pay when requested
AC-15: Report records include report_type when requested
AC-16: Company record includes custom_company_tin_number, or test skips gracefully if field absent
INT-04: Salary Slip records include custom_lhdn_status when requested
No passing tests are broken by the fix
technicalNotes: Files: tests/api_contract/test_api_contract.py, tests/integration/test_workflows.py
Replace self.session.resource(doctype, params={'fields': '[...]', 'limit': N}) calls
With: self.session.api('frappe.client.get_list', doctype=doctype, fields=['name','employee'], limit=3)
For AC-16: add if 'custom_company_tin_number' not in record: self.skipTest('Field absent Ã¢â‚¬â€� myinvois not installed')
Run: .venv-tests/Scripts/python.exe tests/run_tests.py --suite api_contract integration

userStories.title: Add fallback_base_url field to Prisma AI Settings doctype schema
userStories.description: REG-04 regression test fails because fallback_base_url is absent from the Prisma AI Settings DocType schema. The field is used by the 3-tier fallback LLM feature (commit a121645) in prisma_assistant/api/chat.py but was never added to the doctype JSON definition. The REST GET response for Prisma AI Settings confirms the field is missing. Without this schema entry, the Frappe desk form cannot display or save the fallback2 base URL, causing the feature to silently use None/empty. This is a real product bug, not just a test issue.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/resource/Prisma AI Settings/Prisma AI Settings includes fallback_base_url in the response
Field appears in the Prisma AI Settings desk form under the Fallback LLM section
Field type: Data, label: Fallback Base URL, reqd: 0, in_list_view: 0
bench --site frontend migrate runs without error
REG-04 test passes after docker cp + migrate
technicalNotes: File: prisma_assistant/prisma_assistant/prisma_assistant/doctype/prisma_ai_settings/prisma_ai_settings.json
Add to fields array: {"fieldname": "fallback_base_url", "fieldtype": "Data", "label": "Fallback Base URL", "insert_after": "fallback_model"}

Deploy: docker cp prisma_assistant/.../prisma_ai_settings.json prisma-erp-backend-1:/home/frappe/frappe-bench/apps/prisma_assistant/prisma_assistant/prisma_assistant/doctype/prisma_ai_settings/prisma_ai_settings.json
Then: docker exec prisma-erp-backend-1 bench --site frontend migrate
Verify: GET /api/resource/Prisma AI Settings/Prisma AI Settings Ã¢â€ â€™ fallback_base_url present
Run: .venv-tests/Scripts/python.exe tests/run_tests.py --suite regression

userStories.title: Fix SEC-06 CSRF enforcement test Ã¢â‚¬â€� incorrect test design
userStories.description: SEC-06 (test_sec06_post_without_csrf_fails) fails because the test expects frappe.client.set_value to fail when called without an explicit X-Frappe-CSRF-Token header, but the call actually succeeds and modifies the Administrator first_name field. Root cause: In Frappe v16, an absent CSRF header may not always be rejected for API calls from an authenticated session Ã¢â‚¬â€� the CSRF check compares the header only if it is present. The test assumption is incorrect. Additionally, the test has a side effect of mutating Administrator first_name if it passes the CSRF check, which can interfere with other tests. The test must be redesigned: verify a security property that is actually enforced (e.g. unauthenticated access blocked), use a safe reversible operation, and add tearDown cleanup.
acceptanceCriteria: SEC-06 passes without false-positive or false-negative
If Frappe does enforce absent-CSRF-header as error: test verifies 400 CSRFTokenError
If Frappe does NOT enforce absent-CSRF-header: test is redesigned to check a different security property (e.g. unauthenticated set_value is blocked Ã¢â€ â€™ 403)
Test does NOT permanently mutate Administrator first_name Ã¢â‚¬â€� add tearDown/finally to restore value
Security suite passes 100% after fix
technicalNotes: File: tests/security/test_security.py Ã¢â‚¬â€� TestCSRFProtection.test_sec06_post_without_csrf_fails
Read frappe/api.py validate_auth() to confirm if absent CSRF header is rejected in v16
Redesign: use anon session (no login) to call set_value Ã¢â€ â€™ expect 403/401 (unauthenticated blocked)
Or: make set_value call then tearDown restores first_name regardless of test outcome
Safe alternative mutation: set_value on a test-only field, or use get_value only
Run: .venv-tests/Scripts/python.exe tests/run_tests.py --suite security

userStories.title: Generate Employment Act S.61 Compliant Payslip with All Mandatory Fields
userStories.description: Employment Act 1955 Section 61 (amended by Employment (Amendment) Act 2022, effective 1 Jan 2023) extended the mandatory payslip obligation to ALL employees regardless of salary. Payslips must itemize gross wages, all allowances, all deductions (EPF/SOCSO/EIS/PCB/advances), and net pay. Non-compliance is an offence under Section 99A (fine up to RM10,000). Currently no story covers generating a compliant payslip document.
acceptanceCriteria: Payslip PDF/HTML generated per Salary Slip record includes: employee full name, NRIC, gender, citizenship status, wage period, payment date, itemized gross earnings (basic, OT, allowances), itemized deductions (EPF, SOCSO, EIS, PCB, zakat, advance repayment), and net pay
Employer name, address, and place of employment appear on every payslip
Payslip generation works for all employee types: full-time, part-time, foreign workers
Bulk payslip generation (all employees for a payroll run) produces individual PDFs or a ZIP archive
Payslip is accessible via Employee Self-Service portal (if implemented) and downloadable by HR
technicalNotes: Employment Act 1955, Section 61 (Wage registers and salary records) Ã¯Â¿Â½ mandatory for all employees post-2022 amendment
Section 61(1B): payslip must be delivered on or before each payment of wages
Use Frappe's print format / Jinja template for PDF generation via wkhtmltopdf or WeasyPrint
Mandatory fields enumerated in Employment Act Third Schedule (Ninth Schedule after amendment)

userStories.title: Implement Salary Advance Loan Module with Employment Act S.22/24 50% Deduction Cap
userStories.description: Employment Act 1955 Sections 22-24 govern employer salary advances and lawful deductions. Advances under Section 22 (interest-free) can be recovered without separate employee consent, but total deductions in any wage period cannot exceed 50% of monthly wages. No existing story models advance loans, outstanding balances, or enforces the 50% monthly deduction ceiling across all concurrent deduction types.
acceptanceCriteria: Salary Advance DocType stores: advance date, amount, approved repayment schedule, outstanding balance, and linked salary slips
Payroll run automatically adds advance repayment line to Salary Slip and deducts from net pay
System enforces the 50% cap: if EPF + SOCSO + EIS + PCB + advance repayment > 50% of gross wages, advance deduction is reduced to respect the cap and the balance carried to the next period
Advance balance ledger tracks remaining amount and projected clearance date
HR can view all active advances and outstanding balances in a list report
technicalNotes: Employment Act 1955 Section 22: Advance of wages; Section 24(1): Lawful deductions; Section 24(2): 50% deduction ceiling
Exception to 50% ceiling: final wages on resignation/termination (full deduction allowed)
New Frappe DocType: 'Salary Advance Loan' with fields: employee, advance_date, amount, repayment_amount_per_period, outstanding_balance, status
Hook into before_submit of Salary Slip to compute cap and adjust repayment line

userStories.title: Implement Part-Time Employee ORP Proration and Overtime Eligibility under EA Third Schedule
userStories.description: Employment Act 1955 Third Schedule (Part-Time Employees) defines a separate ORP formula for employees whose contracted hours are 70% or less of normal full-time hours. The ORP for part-time workers is based on agreed monthly wage divided by contracted monthly hours. Overtime rate and rest day pay follow the same 1.5x/2x multipliers on this adjusted HRP. The existing ORP story covers monthly-rated employees; part-time workers have a distinct formula not yet captured.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record has a field 'Employment Type' with options: Full-Time, Part-Time
Part-time employees have fields: 'Contracted Hours Per Week' and 'Contracted Days Per Week'
ORP for part-time calculated as: agreed_monthly_wage divided by contracted_hours_per_month, where contracted_hours_per_month = contracted_hours_per_week * 52 / 12
Overtime entitlement check applies: part-time employee must be earning RM4,000/month equivalent or less to be covered under EA
Overtime pay line on Salary Slip uses part-time HRP x 1.5 for normal day OT, x 2 for public holiday OT
System prevents OT payments above 104 hours/month for EA-covered part-time employees
technicalNotes: Employment Act 1955, Third Schedule - Minimum Terms and Conditions of Employment for Part-Time Employees (Regulation 5)
Part-time definition: works fewer than 70% of normal full-time hours
Employment (Amendment) Act 2022 extended EA coverage to employees earning up to RM4,000/month (previously RM2,000)
ORP formula reference: Employment Act Third Schedule, Regulation 5(3) for hourly rate derivation
dependencies: Implement Ordinary Rate of Pay (ORP) Calculator and Overtime Validation

userStories.title: Implement Director Fee-Only MTD Lump-Sum Formula (Recipients Without Monthly Salary)
userStories.description: LHDN's PCB specification defines a distinct MTD formula for directors who receive only lump-sum director fees (no monthly salary). The formula divides total fees by the number of months covered, computes MTD on that monthly equivalent, then multiplies by months. This differs from the bonus-month annualization formula (which adds to existing monthly salary) and from the existing 'Distinguish director fee vs director salary' story (which operates at the MyInvois XML level, not the PCB calculation level).
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record has 'Director Status' flag and 'Director Fee Payment Frequency' (Monthly, Quarterly, Annually, Irregular)
For fee-only directors (no monthly salary): MTD computed as: tax_on_monthly_equivalent x months_covered, where monthly_equivalent = total_fee / months_covered
For directors with both monthly salary and lump-sum fee: bonus-month annualization formula applied (existing logic) - not the fee-only formula
Director fee Salary Slip distinguishes between Employment Income and Director Fee income type on CP39 and EA Form
System produces correct PCB amount verified against LHDN's worked examples in the PCB specification
technicalNotes: LHDN PCB Specification 2026: Section 5 - MTD computation method for director fee recipients
PCB Spec PDF: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/arvlrzh5/spesifikasi-kaedah-pengiraan-berkomputer-pcb-2026.pdf
Director fees must appear under Jumlah Imbuhan (B5 on EA Form), separate from basic salary (B1)
CP39 row for director fee uses distinct income type code per LHDN specification

userStories.title: Update PCB TP1 Relief Tables for YA2025 (New Housing Loan Interest Relief and Increased Disability Amounts)
userStories.description: Budget 2025 introduced new and revised personal income tax reliefs effective YA2025 that must be reflected in the TP1 declaration and PCB calculation engine. Key changes: (1) NEW housing loan interest relief (RM7,000 for first home RM500K or less; RM5,000 for RM500K-RM750K, valid for SPA signed 1 Jan 2025 to 31 Dec 2027); (2) disabled individual additional relief raised to RM7,000 from RM6,000; (3) disabled spouse additional relief raised to RM6,000 from RM5,000. The existing TP1 story created the infrastructure; the YA2025 relief values need updating.
acceptanceCriteria: TP1 DocType Relief Items table includes new line item: Housing Loan Interest - First Home RM500K or less (max RM7,000) with SPA date validation (1 Jan 2025 to 31 Dec 2027)
New line item: Housing Loan Interest - First Home RM500K to RM750K (max RM5,000) with same SPA date validation
Disabled individual additional relief updated to RM7,000 maximum (from RM6,000)
Disabled spouse additional relief updated to RM6,000 (from RM5,000)
PCB calculation engine validates that YA2025 relief caps are applied per the updated LHDN relief table
Relief limits are version-controlled by assessment year so historical YA2024 calculations remain unaffected
technicalNotes: LHDN Tax Reliefs for YA2025: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/individual/individual-life-cycle/income-declaration/tax-reliefs/
Crowe Malaysia YA2025 relief guide: https://www.crowe.com/my/insights/malaysia-personal-income-tax-relief-for-ya-2025
Housing loan interest relief: only for SPA dated between 1 Jan 2025 and 31 Dec 2027 (sunset clause); not applicable to SPA before 2025
Store relief caps in a configuration table keyed by (assessment_year, relief_type) to support future annual updates
dependencies: Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration

userStories.title: Implement HRDF Levy Contribution Base Classifier (Fixed Allowances vs. Variable Pay)
userStories.description: PSMB Act 2001 requires HRD Corp levy to be computed on wages which includes basic salary and fixed allowances (housing, transport, shift allowance) but excludes overtime, bonuses, commissions, service charges, and reimbursements. The existing HRDF stories cover the 1%/0.5% rate and Borang PSMB/6 generation, but no story classifies salary components into levy-base vs. non-levy-base, nor validates that the levy is computed on the correct base each month.
acceptanceCriteria: Salary component configuration has a boolean field 'Is HRDF Levy Base' (default: True for fixed allowances, False for variable/performance components)
HRDF levy calculated as: sum of components where is_hrdf_levy_base = True, multiplied by levy_rate (1% or 0.5%)
Monthly Salary Slip shows HRDF levy base amount and levy contribution computed
HRDF levy base excludes: overtime pay, bonus, commission, service charge, gratuity, one-time payments
HRDF levy base includes: basic salary, housing allowance (fixed), transport allowance (fixed), shift allowance (fixed)
Report shows monthly HRDF levy base vs. gross wages so HR can audit classification
technicalNotes: PSMB Act 2001 definition of wages for levy: Section 2 interpretation and Third Schedule
HRD Corp levy contribution guide: https://hrdcorp.gov.my/faq
Add 'is_hrdf_levy_base' custom field to Salary Component via fixture
Monthly levy payment due to HRD Corp by 15th of following month via HRD Corp employer portal
dependencies: Add HRDF levy validation and basic reporting
Fix HRDF Levy Rate - 1% Mandatory for All Employers with 10+ Employees

userStories.title: Add Wage Payment Deadline Compliance Alerts (Employment Act S.19 7-Day Rule)
userStories.description: Employment Act 1955 Section 19 (post-2022 amendment) requires wages to be paid no later than 7 days after the end of each wage period. Overtime wages must be paid within 10 days of the end of the wage period. Non-compliance exposes employers to fines up to RM50,000 per occurrence (Section 100A). No current story tracks payroll run timing or alerts HR when payroll processing is running late relative to the legal deadline.
acceptanceCriteria: System tracks Wage Period End Date and Payment Due Date (= wage period end + 7 days) per payroll run
HR Manager receives email/Frappe notification alert 2 days before payment due date if Salary Slips are not yet in Submitted status
Dashboard widget shows payroll compliance status: On-Time, At Risk (2 or fewer days remaining), or Overdue
Overtime payment deadline tracked separately: wage period end + 10 days
Alert configuration is per-company and respects custom wage period definitions (weekly, fortnightly, monthly)
technicalNotes: Employment Act 1955, Section 19(1): normal wages must be paid within 7 days; Section 19(1A): overtime wages within 10 days - post-2022 amendment
Frappe notification/scheduler: use background job to check daily and send Frappe notifications
Payment due date = last day of wage period + 7 calendar days (not business days)
Section 100A fine: RM50,000 per violation per employee (effective 2022 amendment)

userStories.title: Implement Payroll Wage Register with 6-Year Retention and LHDN/MOHR Audit Export
userStories.description: Employment Act 1955 Section 61 and Income Tax Act 1967 Section 82 require employers to maintain wage records for a minimum of 6 and 7 years respectively. During LHDN or MOHR (Department of Labour) audits, employers must produce a wage register for each employee covering the audit period. No current story implements a formal wage register with enforced retention policy and structured export for auditors.
acceptanceCriteria: Wage Register report generates a per-employee annual summary: all wage periods, gross pay, deductions, net pay, payroll dates
Records cannot be deleted or purged if within 7-year retention window (archive lock enforcement)
Export formats: PDF (print-ready tabular format) and XLSX for any selected date range
Audit export includes: employer details, employee details, every salary slip within the range, and total annual figures matching EA Form values
System-level data retention policy document accessible from LHDN Payroll workspace
technicalNotes: Income Tax Act 1967, Section 82: records must be kept for 7 years
Employment Act 1955, Section 61(1A): wage register retained for not less than 6 years
Frappe archive lock: prevent delete for Salary Slip records within retention window; set custom_lhdn_archived = 1 only after 7-year retention period expires
Integrate with the existing 'Define custom_lhdn_archived field in custom_field.json fixture' story
dependencies: Define custom_lhdn_archived field in custom_field.json fixture

userStories.title: Auto-Generate EIS Form IA New Employee Registration File for PERKESO ASSIST
userStories.description: Employment Insurance System Act 2017 Section 34 requires employers to register every new eligible employee with EIS within 30 days of employment commencement. PERKESO provides Form IA (EIS-1A) for batch employee registration submitted via the ASSIST portal. The existing SOCSO Borang 3 story covers new employee notification to PERKESO for SOCSO; EIS Form IA is a separate, parallel registration requirement that should auto-generate on employee onboarding.
acceptanceCriteria: On Employee DocType save (status = Active, age 18-60, employment date set), system creates a Pending EIS Registration flag
HR can trigger batch EIS Form IA file generation for all pending employees covering a date range
EIS Form IA file (CSV/text per PERKESO ASSIST specification) includes: employer reference number, employee NRIC, employee name, date joined, monthly wage bracket
System marks employee as EIS Registered after HR confirms PERKESO ASSIST upload acknowledgement
Alert generated if employee reaches 30 days of employment without EIS registration confirmation
Foreign workers and employees over age 60 are automatically excluded from EIS Form IA generation
technicalNotes: Employment Insurance System Act 2017 (Act 800), Section 34: employer registration obligation for new employees
PERKESO ASSIST portal: https://assist.perkeso.gov.my - supports batch CSV upload for employee registration
EIS eligibility: Malaysian/PR employees aged 18-60 only; foreign workers, civil servants, and domestic workers exempt
Differentiate from SOCSO Borang 3 (SOCSO registration) - EIS Form IA is submitted via the same ASSIST portal but is a distinct form and file format
dependencies: Implement SOCSO Borang 3 - New Employee Notification to PERKESO

userStories.title: Integrate Private Retirement Scheme (PRS) Voluntary Deduction with TP1 RM3,000 Relief
userStories.description: Private Retirement Scheme Act 2012 allows employees to make voluntary PRS contributions which qualify for RM3,000 personal tax relief per year. When employers facilitate PRS payroll deductions, the contribution amount must appear on the payslip as a voluntary deduction and auto-populate the PRS relief field in the employee's TP1 declaration. No existing story connects PRS payroll deductions to the TP1 relief accumulation.
acceptanceCriteria: Salary component 'Private Retirement Scheme (PRS)' of type Deduction exists in fixture, marked as voluntary and non-statutory
PRS deduction on Salary Slip accumulates in a YTD counter per employee per year, capped at RM3,000 for relief reporting purposes
TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType has PRS relief field auto-populated from YTD PRS payroll deduction total
PRS provider name and employee account number stored on Employee for remittance reference
Payslip shows PRS deduction as a separate line item (after statutory deductions, before net pay)
EA Form Part D (deductions) includes PRS amount in the correct row for Private Retirement Scheme contributions
technicalNotes: Private Retirement Scheme Act 2012 (Act 739); SC-approved PRS providers listed at https://www.sc.com.my/development/private-retirement-scheme
LHDN Tax Relief: PRS = RM3,000/year, this is a separate bucket from EPF + life insurance (RM7,000 combined bucket)
PRS deduction does NOT reduce EPF/SOCSO/EIS contribution base - applied after statutory deductions
EA Form: PRS contributions declared under the row for contributions to approved Private Retirement Schemes
dependencies: Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration

userStories.title: Implement DTA 183-Day Residency Tracker and Short-Term Assignee PCB Exemption Workflow
userStories.description: Malaysia's Double Taxation Agreements (DTAs) with 70+ countries include a short-term employment exemption: non-citizen employees from DTA countries working in Malaysia for fewer than 183 days in a calendar year may be exempt from Malaysian income tax on employment income. Payroll systems must track days-in-Malaysia, flag employees approaching 183 days, and support a PCB exemption override. The existing 'Implement Expatriate Gross-Up Calculator and DTA Country Table' story covers grossed-up salary for tax residents; this story covers the pre-183-day exemption tracking workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record has Tax Residency Status field: Resident, Non-Resident, Short-Term Assignee (DTA Exempt)
Days-in-Malaysia counter tracks calendar year presence from a structured Travel Log sub-table (date, direction: Entry/Exit)
System generates alert when Short-Term Assignee exceeds 150 days in-Malaysia in a calendar year (approaching 183-day threshold)
PCB exemption flag on Salary Slip suppresses PCB deduction and generates a zero-PCB CP39 record for that employee
When Short-Term Assignee exceeds 183 days, system automatically removes exemption flag and triggers backpay-of-PCB calculation for prior months in the same year
DTA country table lists treaty partner countries; system validates that employee home country has an active DTA before allowing exemption flag
technicalNotes: LHDN DTA page: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/international/double-taxation-avoidance-agreement-dtadtaa/
Income Tax Act 1967, Section 13(2)(h): employment income exemption under DTA for short-term assignments
183 days counted per calendar year (1 Jan to 31 Dec), including partial days of entry and exit
Employer must retain documentation for treaty claim and may need to file LHDN exemption notification in some DTA scenarios
dependencies: Implement Expatriate Gross-Up Calculator and DTA Country Table

userStories.title: Implement Multi-Currency Salary Conversion for Expatriate PCB Base Calculation
userStories.description: Some expatriate employees receive salaries denominated in foreign currencies (USD, SGD, GBP, AUD) per their employment contracts. For PCB purposes, LHDN requires all income to be converted to Malaysian Ringgit using the exchange rate prevailing at the date of payment (Bank Negara Malaysia middle rate). No existing story handles foreign-currency salary slips or the forex conversion step for PCB and EA Form reporting. This is distinct from the existing 'Add foreign currency conversion to Expense Claim XML builder' which covers expense reimbursements only.
acceptanceCriteria: Salary Slip supports a Salary Currency field (defaults to MYR) and an Exchange Rate to MYR field
For non-MYR salary slips, all salary components are stored in both original currency amount and MYR equivalent amount
PCB calculation uses MYR equivalent amount for all tax computations
EA Form and CP39 always report values in MYR regardless of salary currency
Exchange rate can be manually entered or fetched from ERPNext Currency Exchange rates table (BNM middle rate is the official reference)
Payslip shows both original currency amount and MYR equivalent for each component for employee transparency
technicalNotes: LHDN PCB Specification: all income reported in RM; foreign-currency income converted at kadar pertukaran pada tarikh pembayaran (rate on payment date)
Bank Negara Malaysia forex rates: https://www.bnm.gov.my/exchange-rates
Income Tax Act 1967, Section 13: employment income includes amounts received in foreign currency
ERPNext has built-in Currency Exchange DocType; salary foreign currency conversion should use the same exchange rate lookup mechanism

userStories.title: Build Monthly e-PCB Plus Submission Reconciliation View
userStories.description: Since 15 January 2025, all PCB submissions go exclusively through the e-PCB Plus system on MyTax portal (legacy e-CP39, e-PCB, and e-Data PCB systems are closed for input). Employers need a monthly reconciliation view comparing PCB amounts computed by the payroll system against amounts acknowledged by e-PCB Plus. The existing 'Build year-end PCB vs submission reconciliation report' covers annual reconciliation; this story provides the monthly operational view used by payroll administrators to catch errors within the same month.
acceptanceCriteria: Monthly PCB Reconciliation report for a selected month shows: employee name, employee NRIC, system-computed PCB, e-PCB Plus submitted PCB (from imported acknowledgement file), and variance
Submission records can be imported from the e-PCB Plus acknowledgement/confirmation file (CSV/text downloaded from MyTax portal) and matched to Salary Slip records by employee NRIC and month
Unmatched employees (in system but not in submission, or vice versa) are highlighted as exceptions requiring investigation
Report exports as XLSX for HR audit trail
Summary row shows: total system PCB, total submitted PCB, total variance, and count of unmatched employees
technicalNotes: e-PCB Plus system mandatory from 15 January 2025 at https://mytax.hasil.gov.my
e-PCB Plus generates an acknowledgement file on successful submission - system should store the reference number and submission timestamp per monthly payroll run
This report is distinct from the year-end PCB reconciliation (CP159/annual Borang E supporting schedule) - monthly view is for ongoing payroll operations
BusinessToday e-PCB Plus mandate announcement: https://www.businesstoday.com.my/2025/01/08/deadline-for-submission-of-data-and-monthly-tax-deduction-payments-via-e-pcb-plus/
dependencies: Implement e-CP39 API Submission to LHDN MyTax / e-PCB Plus
Fix CP39 CSV Format - Add All Mandatory LHDN e-PCB Plus Columns

userStories.title: Implement Zakat State Rate Table for Multi-State Employer Offices
userStories.description: The ringgit-for-ringgit Zakat PCB offset (Section 6A, Income Tax Act 1967) allows zakat deducted at source to offset the employee's PCB liability dollar-for-dollar. However, zakat rates are set independently by each Malaysian state's religious authority and differ between states. The existing 'Implement Zakat PCB Offset' story implements the offset mechanism but assumes a single rate; multi-state employers need state-specific rates and remittance routing to the correct state authority.
acceptanceCriteria: Zakat State Rate Table DocType stores: state, zakat authority name, current annual rate (percentage of income), income threshold (nisab), and effective date
Employee record has a Zakat State field (selects applicable state for zakat computation)
Monthly zakat deduction computed using the rate and nisab applicable to the employee's registered state
Zakat remittance report groups zakat amounts by state authority for correct payment routing
PCB offset applies: PCB liability for the month reduced by exact zakat deducted that month (1:1 offset)
If monthly zakat deduction exceeds PCB liability for that month, excess is not refunded but can be documented for employee's annual self-assessment offset
technicalNotes: Income Tax Act 1967, Section 6A: zakat/fitrah PCB offset - potongan zakat boleh ditolak daripada cukai
Zakat authorities by state: LZS (Selangor), MAIWP (Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya, Labuan), LZZT (Terengganu), MAIAMP (Pahang), etc.
Annual nisab threshold approximately RM22,000 (varies by state; based on current gold/silver price)
Payroll deducts zakat monthly (1/12 of annual estimate based on projected annual income) with year-end adjustment
dependencies: Implement Zakat PCB Offset (Ringgit-for-Ringgit Deduction)

userStories.title: Auto-Submit CP22 via e-CP22 MyTax Within 30 Days of New Employee Hire
userStories.description: Effective 1 September 2024, LHDN mandates that all CP22 (new employee notifications) must be submitted exclusively via e-CP22 on the MyTax portal â€” manual paper submissions are no longer accepted. Employers must notify LHDN within 30 days of the employee's start date, and only the company director or an appointed Employer Representative (both registered roles on MyTax) can submit. Non-compliance carries fines of RM200 to RM20,000 under Section 83(2) ITA 1967.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType has a field 'CP22 Submission Status' (Select: Pending / Submitted / Not Required) defaulting to Pending on Active employee creation

A background scheduled job runs daily and generates a Frappe notification/alert for all Active employees whose employment date is within the last 25 days and whose CP22 Submission Status is still Pending
HR can manually mark CP22 Submission Status as Submitted (with a date and reference number field) after completing the MyTax submission
Alert escalates to HR Manager if an employee reaches 28 days of employment without CP22 Submitted status
CP22 is not required for employees who already have a Tax Reference Number (TIN) pre-filled â€” system allows HR to mark as 'Not Required' with reason
Report 'Pending CP22 Submissions' lists all employees with Pending status, days since hire, and days remaining before 30-day deadline
technicalNotes: LHDN official page: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/employers/notifications-of-new-employee/
Mandatory online-only from 1 September 2024; e-CP22 accessible via MyTax portal (mytax.hasil.gov.my)
Statutory basis: Income Tax Act 1967, Section 83(2) â€” employer must notify LHDN within 30 days of new employment
No batch API exists for CP22 â€” submission must be done manually on MyTax; the story covers tracking, alerting, and status recording
Employer Representative role must be set up in MyTax before submissions can be delegated; add a Company-level field 'MyTax Employer Representative Name' for reference

userStories.title: Auto-Submit CP21/CP22A/CP22B via e-SPC MyTax at Cessation or Departure
userStories.description: From 1 January 2025, CP21 (leaving Malaysia), CP22A (private sector resignation/retirement/death), and CP22B (public sector equivalent) must be submitted exclusively via the e-SPC module on MyTax â€” manual forms are no longer accepted. Employers must file at least 30 days before the cessation date or departure, and failure to comply exposes the employer to liability for the employee's unpaid tax. These forms trigger the Surat Penyelesaian Cukai (SPC/tax clearance letter) which employers must obtain before issuing the final payment.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType has cessation-related fields: 'Cessation Type' (Select: Resignation / Retirement / Termination / Departure from Malaysia / Death), 'Cessation Date', 'SPC Reference Number', 'SPC Status' (Pending / Submitted / Cleared / Not Required)
When Cessation Date is set, system automatically determines the correct form: CP22A for private sector resignation/retirement, CP21 for employees flagged as leaving Malaysia permanently
Alert sent to HR Manager 35 days before Cessation Date if SPC Status is still Pending (ensuring 30-day advance submission window)
Final Salary Slip submission is blocked with a warning if Cessation Date is set and SPC Status is not 'Cleared' or 'Not Required'
SPC Reference Number and clearance date stored on Employee for audit trail
Test: employee with cessation date 30 days away triggers alert; final salary slip blocked if SPC not cleared
technicalNotes: LHDN e-SPC User Manual: https://mytax.hasil.gov.my/assets/pdf/e-SPC.pdf
Mandatory online-only from 1 January 2025; accessible at mytax.hasil.gov.my under e-SPC module
Income Tax Act 1967, Section 83(3): employer must withhold final salary until SPC received
CP21 for employees leaving Malaysia for >3 months; CP22A for private sector; CP22B for government/statutory employer
Processing time: 14 working days â€” the 35-day alert provides buffer for processing before cessation date
Distinct from the existing CP22 (new hire) story â€” this covers the departure/cessation side

userStories.title: Register PCB Administrator Role on MyTax e-PCB Plus and Store Credential Reference on Company
userStories.description: As of 3 February 2025, all PCB monthly data submission and payments flow exclusively through the e-PCB Plus system on the MyTax portal. Before payroll staff can submit, the employer must designate a PCB Administrator (a specific named role on MyTax tied to an individual's NRIC) who is authorized to manage PCB submissions on behalf of the company. Without this setup, the e-PCB Plus file upload fails. Currently no story covers storing the PCB Administrator identity, managing Employer Representative delegation, or guiding the initial e-PCB Plus company registration.
acceptanceCriteria: Company DocType has a 'LHDN e-PCB Plus Settings' section with fields: 'PCB Administrator NRIC', 'PCB Administrator Name', 'Employer Representative NRIC' (optional), 'Employer Reference Number (E Number)', 'e-PCB Plus Registration Status' (Select: Not Registered / Registered)
LHDN Payroll workspace shows a setup checklist item: 'e-PCB Plus PCB Administrator registered' with status indicator
A dedicated 'e-PCB Plus Setup Guide' page in the LHDN Developer Tools panel links to the MyTax portal and documents the 4-step admin registration process
On CP39 file generation, system validates that 'e-PCB Plus Registration Status' = Registered before allowing download â€” if not, shows a blocking setup warning with link to the setup guide
Test: CP39 file generation on unregistered company raises validation error; registered company proceeds normally
