technicalNotes: e-PCB Plus mandatory from 3 February 2025: https://www.signature-trust.com/post/hasil-launches-integrated-e-pcb-plus-platform-on-mytax-portal
PCB Administrator setup guide: https://taxpod.com.my/articles/set-up-pcb-admin-mytax-epcb/
Employer reference number (E number) is issued by LHDN during employer registration and is required on all e-PCB Plus submissions
Role hierarchy: Employer (company director) > Employer Representative > PCB Administrator â€” each registered separately in MyTax with own NRIC
No direct API token for e-PCB Plus â€” all submission is via portal file upload (CSV/text format); this story is about the credential reference tracking, not automated API integration

userStories.title: Implement EPF i-Topup (Voluntary Excess Contribution) Payroll Deduction and RM7,000 PCB Relief Tracking
userStories.description: EPF's i-Topup facility allows employees to contribute more than the statutory EPF rate. The additional voluntary contribution (above mandatory 11%/9%) combines with compulsory EPF to form a combined RM7,000 PCB relief pool (RM4,000 for mandatory + RM3,000 for voluntary, shared with life insurance). Employers must submit i-Topup applications via i-Akaun (Employer) and may deduct the voluntary amount from the employee's salary. No existing story models voluntary EPF deductions distinct from mandatory, or tracks the combined RM7,000 relief cap for PCB.
acceptanceCriteria: Salary Component 'EPF Voluntary (i-Topup)' of type Deduction exists in fixture, marked as non-statutory and voluntary
Employee record has 'EPF i-Topup Monthly Amount' (Currency) field to store the agreed voluntary top-up amount
Monthly payroll run deducts 'EPF Voluntary (i-Topup)' and adds to EPF contribution total for the month
YTD voluntary EPF deduction counter tracked per employee per year; combined with YTD mandatory EPF for relief cap validation
PCB calculator respects the RM7,000 combined relief limit: mandatory EPF relief capped at RM4,000; remaining RM3,000 shared with life insurance premium and additional voluntary EPF
TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType has 'Additional Voluntary EPF Contribution (i-Topup)' field auto-populated from YTD voluntary EPF deduction, subject to the RM3,000 sub-cap
EA Form reflects mandatory EPF and voluntary EPF contributions in their respective relief rows
technicalNotes: KWSP i-Topup Employer Guide: https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/employer/responsibilities/contributing-more-than-the-statutory-rate
KWSP i-Topup Member Guide: https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/member/savings/voluntary-excess
Tax relief structure: EPF mandatory RM4,000 (Section C, Income Tax Return); EPF voluntary + life insurance combined RM3,000 additional (same relief row); total EPF+life = RM7,000 max
Employer must submit KWSP Form 17A (Khas) via i-Akaun (Employer) to register employee's i-Topup amount before deducting from salary
Voluntary EPF is excluded from SOCSO, EIS, and HRDF contribution base calculations
dependencies: Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration

userStories.title: Apply YA2025 PCB Schedule 1 Tax Brackets Including New 30% Tier for Income Exceeding RM2 Million
userStories.description: Malaysia's income tax Schedule 1 (Jadual Pertama) is updated for each year of assessment. For YA2025, the progressive rate structure is confirmed to include a top tier of 30% applicable to chargeable income exceeding RM2,000,000. The existing PCB calculator story (US-004) implements the base engine; this story ensures the YA2025 tax bracket table is correctly encoded and the LHDN PCB Specification 2025 amendments (relating to deduction item changes published in the official PCB 2025 PDF) are applied. The PCB spec is version-specific and must be maintained per assessment year.
acceptanceCriteria: Tax bracket table is stored in a configuration DocType or fixture keyed by assessment year, not hardcoded
PCB calculator selects the correct bracket table based on the payroll year (YA = payroll_year for Jan-Dec)
Unit tests verify correct tax for income of RM5,000, RM35,000, RM100,000, RM400,000, RM2,000,000, and RM2,500,000 against LHDN YA2025 PCB specification values
YA2024 calculations (for any historical recomputation) continue to use YA2024 brackets without interference
Personal tax rebate of RM400 applied when chargeable income does not exceed RM35,000 (confirmed unchanged for YA2025)
technicalNotes: LHDN PCB Specification 2025 PDF: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/mdahzjwi/spesifikasi-kaedah-pengiraan-berkomputer-pcb-2025.pdf
LHDN official tax rate page: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/individual/individual-life-cycle/income-declaration/tax-rate/
Budget 2025 confirmation: no change to MTD formula structure, but deduction item values revised for YA2025
30% top tier on chargeable income >RM2M is effective for YA2024 onwards (introduced Budget 2023); ensure YA2025 spec aligns
Store as 'PCB Tax Bracket' table with fields: assessment_year, chargeable_income_from, chargeable_income_to, rate_pct, cumulative_tax_at_lower_bound
dependencies: Implement PCB/MTD monthly calculation validation (US-004)

userStories.title: Implement Public Holiday Work Pay Calculator (Section 60D Employment Act) with Replacement Leave Tracking
userStories.description: Employment Act 1955 Section 60D entitles EA-covered employees to 11 paid public holidays per year. When required to work on a public holiday, the employer must pay the employee their ordinary daily wage plus two additional days' wages (effectively triple pay), or alternatively offer an Off-In-Lieu replacement day. Overtime on a public holiday (beyond normal working hours) is at three times the hourly rate. For employees earning above RM4,000/month (not EA-covered), public holiday pay is governed by their employment contract. No existing payroll story models the Section 60D three-tier pay structure or replacement leave.
acceptanceCriteria: Salary Slip can include a 'Public Holiday Work' additional earnings line that auto-computes: 2 x ORP (ordinary rate of pay per day) as the additional premium (the base day pay is already in the regular salary)
ORP per day for monthly-rated employees computed as monthly_salary / 26 per Section 60I of the Employment Act
Overtime on public holiday (hours beyond normal working hours) calculated at 3x hourly rate (3 x monthly_salary / 26 / normal_daily_hours)
Employee record tracks 'Public Holiday Off-In-Lieu Balance' counter; HR can convert public holiday worked into an OIL day credit instead of triple-pay
System validates that EA coverage applies (employee monthly wage <= RM4,000) before auto-applying EA rates; employees above threshold get a reminder to refer to employment contract
Public Holiday calendar integration: system flags payroll runs that include dates matching Malaysia's gazette public holidays
Tests: monthly RM3,000 employee works 8 hours on a PH â†’ additional premium of RM3,000/26 x 2 = RM230.77; overtime beyond 8 hours â†’ RM3,000/26/8 x 3 per OT hour
technicalNotes: Employment Act 1955, Section 60D: public holiday entitlement and payment obligations
Employment Act 1955, Section 60I: Ordinary Rate of Pay definition â€” monthly salary / 26 for monthly-rated employees
Employment Act 1955, Section 60A: overtime on public holiday at 3x hourly rate
Post-2022 amendment: EA covers all employees with monthly wages not exceeding RM4,000 for overtime/rest day purposes
Public holidays list maintained by MOHR gazette; state-specific public holidays also apply (state holidays differ by state)
dependencies: Implement Ordinary Rate of Pay (ORP) Calculator and Overtime Validation

userStories.title: Implement Commission-as-Normal-Remuneration MTD Formula for Commission-Heavy Pay Structures
userStories.description: LHDN PCB specification distinguishes between commission treated as 'normal remuneration' (received monthly alongside or instead of salary) versus commission treated as 'additional/irregular remuneration' (received ad-hoc on top of regular salary). When commission is the primary or only monthly income, it is classified as normal remuneration and uses the standard monthly PCB annualization formula â€” not the bonus lump-sum formula. Payroll systems that always route commission through the bonus formula will over-deduct PCB for commission-only salespeople or underpay for irregular commissions. No existing story captures the commission-type classification field or the formula switch logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Salary Component DocType has a field 'Commission Classification' (Select: Normal Remuneration / Additional Remuneration) visible when component type = Earning and commission-related

PCB calculation applies: if Commission Classification = Normal Remuneration, annualize as (salary + commission) x 12 using Method 1; if Additional Remuneration, apply the irregular payment formula from LHDN PCB spec Section 3.2
Employee record allows overriding default classification via 'Commission Type Override' field for cases where the same component type is used differently per employee
Salary Slip shows computed PCB with a tooltip indicating which formula was applied (Normal or Additional)

Tests: commission-only employee with RM5,000/month commission â†’ PCB using standard formula; same employee receives RM20,000 year-end performance bonus â†’ additional remuneration formula for that month only
technicalNotes: LHDN PCB Specification 2026, Section 3: 'Imbuhan Tetap' (Fixed Remuneration) vs 'Imbuhan Tambahan' (Additional Remuneration) classification
PCB Spec reference: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/arvlrzh5/spesifikasi-kaedah-pengiraan-berkomputer-pcb-2026.pdf
Normal remuneration commission: monthly PCB = [(P - M) x R + B - (Z + X)] / remaining_months where P includes commission
Additional remuneration formula: PCB = [(P + (commission/12) - M) x R + B - (Z + X)] - existing PCB already paid
Talenox guide on commission PCB: https://blog.talenox.com/malaysia-tax-guide-how-do-i-calculate-pcb-mtd-part-2-of-3/
dependencies: Implement PCB/MTD monthly calculation validation (US-004)

userStories.title: Track Statutory Compliance Submission Calendar and Auto-Escalate Overdue Filings
userStories.description: Malaysian employers face multiple concurrent statutory filing deadlines each month: PCB (15th), EPF (15th), SOCSO (15th), EIS (15th), HRDF levy (15th), and various annual deadlines (EA Form by 28 Feb, Borang E by 31 Mar, CP22 within 30 days of hire, CP21/CP22A at least 30 days before cessation). Late filings attract automatic penalties â€” 10% late payment surcharge for EPF/PCB, 6% p.a. for SOCSO, RM200-RM20,000 fines for Borang E and EA Form. No existing story provides a unified compliance calendar that tracks whether all monthly submissions have been completed and escalates overdue items.
acceptanceCriteria: New DocType 'Statutory Compliance Submission' records: compliance type (PCB / EPF / SOCSO / EIS / HRDF / EA Form / Borang E / CP22 / CP21), payroll period/month, due date, submission status (Pending / Submitted / Overdue), submission date, reference number
Payroll Entry submission automatically creates Statutory Compliance Submission records for PCB, EPF, SOCSO, EIS, and HRDF for that month with due_date = last_day_of_month + 15 days
Dashboard widget 'Compliance Status' shows all pending submissions color-coded: green (>5 days remaining), amber (2-5 days), red (0-2 days or overdue)
Daily scheduled job sends email and Frappe notification to HR Manager for any submission reaching 0 days remaining without Submitted status
Annual filings (EA Form, Borang E) auto-created at year-end payroll run with their respective statutory deadlines
Penalty information tooltips show on overdue items: e.g., 'EPF late payment: 6% p.a. from due date'
technicalNotes: EPF: contributions due by 15th of following month â€” EPF Act 1991, Section 43
SOCSO: contributions due by 15th â€” ESSA 1969; 6% per annum late penalty
PCB: remittance due by 15th â€” ITA 1967, Section 83(6); 10% late payment penalty
EA Form: issued to employees by 28 February â€” ITA 1967, Section 83(1A)
Borang E: submitted to LHDN by 31 March â€” ITA 1967, Section 83(1)
HRDF levy: due by 15th of following month â€” PSMB Act 2001

userStories.title: Generate CP159 Annual PCB Reconciliation Schedule Supporting Borang E Submission
userStories.description: CP159 (Kenyataan Potongan Cukai Bulanan) is the statement of monthly tax deductions that supports the Borang E annual employer return. It reconciles total PCB deducted per employee across the year against the amounts submitted via e-PCB Plus. While the existing 'Build year-end PCB vs submission reconciliation report' story addresses a comparison view, CP159 is a specific LHDN-prescribed schedule that must be submitted alongside Borang E, with prescribed column headers and formatting. Generating a CP159-compliant schedule directly from payroll data reduces manual recompilation by HR at year-end.
acceptanceCriteria: New Script Report 'CP159 Annual PCB Reconciliation Schedule' accepts year parameter and generates per-employee rows: employee name, NRIC/Passport, total taxable remuneration for the year, total PCB deducted Jan-Dec (month by month columns), total CP38 deducted, total PCB + CP38, and variance vs e-PCB Plus submissions
Report output matches CP159 prescribed format with column headings in Bahasa Malaysia as per LHDN specification
Totals row at bottom matches the aggregate figures on Borang E (total employees, total remuneration, total MTD)
Export to XLSX for Borang E supporting documents; export to PDF (print-ready A4 landscape format)
Report validates that sum of monthly CP39 submissions matches sum of CP159 monthly columns per employee, flagging any variance as an exception line
Test: year with 12 months of payroll for 3 employees produces correct per-employee and aggregate totals
technicalNotes: CP159 is referenced in LHDN employer obligations: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/employers/responsibility-of-employer/
CP159 = Borang Kenyataan Potongan Cukai Berjadual; prescribed format by LHDN; submitted with Borang E
Borang E deadline: 31 March annually; CP159 must be prepared for the preceding year
Distinct from CP8D (which is the employee-level remuneration list also submitted with Borang E) â€” CP159 focuses on PCB deduction reconciliation
Link existing e-PCB Plus monthly submission records (from Monthly e-PCB Plus Reconciliation story) to populate the 'submitted PCB' column
dependencies: Build Monthly e-PCB Plus Submission Reconciliation View

userStories.title: Implement Aggregate Multi-Run Payroll PCB for Mid-Month Salary Advances and Supplementary Runs
userStories.description: Some employers run supplementary or advance payroll mid-month (e.g., for festive bonuses paid before month-end) in addition to their main monthly run. LHDN PCB specification requires that all payments made to an employee within a calendar month be aggregated into a single monthly PCB computation. Running separate PCB calculations per payroll run and submitting multiple CP39 rows for the same employee in the same month is incorrect and violates the LHDN e-PCB Plus submission format (one row per employee per month). No existing story handles multi-run aggregation logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Payroll Entry has a flag 'Is Supplementary Run' (Check) to mark mid-month or top-up payroll runs
When generating the CP39 file, the system aggregates all Salary Slips for the same employee within the same calendar month (across main and supplementary runs) into a single CP39 row

Aggregated CP39 row shows: total taxable remuneration (sum of all runs), total PCB already submitted for the month (from prior runs in same month), and net additional PCB to remit for this supplementary run
System prevents duplicate CP39 row submission for the same employee/month combination
PCB calculation for a supplementary run uses YTD income including the main run's income to correctly recompute monthly PCB under Method 1 (YTD formula)
Test: main run PCB = RM500 submitted; supplementary bonus run triggers additional PCB of RM200; CP39 for supplementary shows RM200 additional only (not RM700 total)
technicalNotes: LHDN PCB Specification 2025/2026: all income within a calendar month aggregated; CP39 submission = one row per employee per month
Method 1 (YTD formula) must use cumulative YTD including supplementary run income: pcb = (annual_tax - pcb_already_paid) / remaining_months
e-PCB Plus rejects duplicate submissions for same E-number + NRIC + month combination â€” system must prevent this
Salary advance deducted in main run but wage topup paid in supplementary run: net PCB difference must be computed correctly
Reference: https://actpayroll.com/complete-guide-to-pcb-calculations/ â€” section on non-recurring income within the same month
dependencies: Implement PCB/MTD monthly calculation validation (US-004)

userStories.title: HRD Corp Levy Monthly Payment File and HRDF Employer Portal Integration
userStories.description: PSMB Act 2001 requires registered employers (10+ Malaysian employees mandatory at 1%; 5-9 employees optional at 0.5%) to pay HRD Corp levy by the 15th of each following month via the HRD Corp employer portal. The levy base is basic salary plus fixed allowances (excluding overtime, bonus, commissions). The existing HRDF stories cover rate determination and Borang PSMB/6 annual return. A missing operational gap is the generation of a monthly HRDF levy payment summary file (per the HRD Corp portal format) that payroll staff can upload directly, analogous to how EPF Borang A and SOCSO Borang 8A are auto-generated.
acceptanceCriteria: Monthly HRDF Levy Payment report generates a summary: company HRD Corp registration number, month, total levy-base wages, applicable levy rate (1% or 0.5%), total levy amount payable
Per-employee detail breakdown shows: employee name, NRIC, levy-base wage components, levy amount for that employee
Report output includes both a human-readable PDF and an upload-compatible text/CSV file in HRD Corp portal format (if HRD Corp publishes a batch upload specification)
Monthly levy payment due date (15th of following month) tracked in the Statutory Compliance Submission calendar
Levy calculation cross-checks against the HRDF Levy Contribution Base Classifier to ensure only levy-base components are included
Test: employee with RM4,000 basic + RM500 fixed transport + RM800 OT â†’ levy base = RM4,500; levy = RM45 at 1%
technicalNotes: PSMB Act 2001, levy payment obligation: https://hrdcorp.gov.my/faq
HRD Corp employer portal for levy payment: https://www.hrdcorp.gov.my
Levy due date: 15th of the following month; late payment attracts interest
Levy base definition: wages = basic salary + fixed allowances; excludes overtime, bonus, commission, reimbursements â€” per PSMB Act 2001 Section 2 definition of 'wages'
HRD Corp may not publish a machine-readable batch upload spec; if so, the story delivers the PDF summary + manual payment guidance via the LHDN Payroll workspace
dependencies: Add HRDF levy validation and basic reporting
Implement HRDF Levy Contribution Base Classifier (Fixed Allowances vs. Variable Pay)

userStories.title: Implement Approved Gratuity Fund Contribution and Tax-Exempt Gratuity Declaration for EA Form
userStories.description: Income Tax Act 1967, Schedule 6, paragraph 25 exempts gratuity paid from an LHDN-approved gratuity/pension fund from income tax (up to RM1,000 per year of service). Beyond this, employees who retire from an approved company pension scheme at age 55+ qualify for full gratuity exemption (paragraph 30). No existing story models gratuity as a distinct payroll component separate from bonus, records the approved fund membership, or routes the exempted amount correctly to EA Form Part B. The existing 'Approved Pension Scheme' story (US-085) covers the full exemption at retirement; this story covers the RM1,000/year partial exemption applicable to all employees with non-approved or approved fund payments during active service.
acceptanceCriteria: Salary Component 'Gratuity' of type Earning exists in fixture with a 'Gratuity Type' option field: (Select: Ex-Gratia / Approved Fund Gratuity / Non-Approved Gratuity)
For 'Approved Fund Gratuity': system calculates the exempt amount as min(total_gratuity, RM1,000 x years_of_service) and the taxable balance
EA Form populates B5 (Gratuity) with total gratuity paid and the exemption amount is shown in the exempt income section (Schedule 6 para 25)
Gratuity Salary Slip line shows: gross gratuity, exempt portion, taxable portion, and the years-of-service multiplier used
PCB calculation excludes the exempt portion from chargeable income for the month the gratuity is paid
Test: 5-year employee receives RM10,000 gratuity from approved fund â†’ exempt RM5,000 (RM1,000 x 5 years); taxable RM5,000 subject to PCB
technicalNotes: ITA 1967, Schedule 6, paragraph 25: gratuity from approved fund â€” RM1,000 per year of service exemption
ITA 1967, Schedule 6, paragraph 30: full exemption for approved scheme at retirement age 55+ (covered by US-085)
Employee record needs 'Date Joined Company' for years-of-service calculation; if date not set, system should warn
EA Form Part B: gratuity reported in B5 (Imbuhan); exempt portion not included in chargeable employment income
LHDN Public Ruling: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/legislation/public-rulings/ â€” search for 'gratuity' and 'retirement'

userStories.title: Implement Flexi-Benefit (Cafeteria Plan) Allocation with BIK Classification and PCB Inclusion
userStories.description: Some Malaysian employers offer flexible benefit (flexi-benefit or cafeteria plan) schemes where employees select from a menu of benefits (e.g., dental, optical, gym membership, travel allowance, gadget allowance) up to an annual allocation. Each benefit type has different tax treatment: some are fully exempt (medical treatment RM10,000 per ITA Schedule 6), some are partially exempt (childcare RM3,000), and others are taxable Benefits-in-Kind. A payroll system must classify each elected benefit, compute the taxable BIK portion, and include it in PCB and EA Form reporting. No existing story addresses the flexi-benefit allocation mechanism, even though BIK prescribed values are already covered.
acceptanceCriteria: New DocType 'Employee Flexi Benefit Allocation' with fields: employee, year, total_allocation, and child table of elected benefits (benefit_type, elected_amount, taxable_amount, exempt_amount, bik_value)
Benefit type configuration table maps each benefit to: ITA exempt status, applicable Schedule 6 paragraph, annual exempt limit, and BIK/non-BIK classification
System computes taxable portion per benefit: elected_amount minus applicable exempt limit; remainder = taxable income
Taxable flexi-benefit amount added to the employee's YTD income for PCB computation (treated as additional remuneration for the month it is disbursed)
EA Form B includes taxable flexi-benefit amounts in the BIK section with per-type breakdowns
Test: RM5,000 dental benefit elected (exempt limit RM10,000 medical) â†’ fully exempt; RM2,000 gym membership (no exemption) â†’ full RM2,000 taxable BIK
technicalNotes: ITA 1967 Schedule 6: various exemptions for employer-provided benefits; key items: medical/dental RM10,000, childcare RM3,000, parking RM1,200
LHDN Public Ruling 3/2013 (Benefits in Kind) and LHDN PCB Specification define taxable BIK
Taxable BIK included in gross income for PCB â€” added to that month's additional remuneration and annualized
LHDN BIK guide: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/forms/upload/penerangan-kerakyatan/bik/pengiraan-nilai-barangan.pdf
Flexi-benefit administration is complex; this story is a foundational framework â€” BIK prescribed values story (existing) should be a prerequisite
dependencies: Implement Benefits-in-Kind (BIK) Prescribed Value Calculation Module

userStories.title: Implement Malaysia Gazetted Public Holiday Calendar Sync and State-Specific Holiday Handling
userStories.description: Malaysia has 11 mandatory national public holidays plus state-specific gazetted holidays (which vary by state â€” e.g., Johor has different holidays than Selangor). Under the Employment Act, employers must provide these gazetted holidays as paid rest. Currently, payroll systems manually configure holiday lists per employee, creating risk of missing new gazette orders (e.g., election days, royal proclamations) or misapplying state holidays to employees in the wrong state. A holiday calendar management module with state awareness would ensure correct paid-holiday deduction and public holiday pay computation.
acceptanceCriteria: New DocType 'Malaysia Public Holiday Calendar' stores: holiday date, holiday name, holiday type (National Gazetted / State Gazetted), applicable states (Multi-Select), and gazette reference
Employee record has 'Work State' field (Select: all 13 states + 3 Federal Territories) used to determine which state public holidays apply
Payroll run cross-references work dates against the employee's applicable holiday calendar (national + work state holidays) to auto-flag public holiday working days
Annual holiday calendar can be imported from a CSV template; system provides a pre-filled template for the current year's national holidays
Alert when payroll runs for months that have unconfigured state holidays for employees based in that state
Integration with Public Holiday Work Pay Calculator story: auto-triggers the Section 60D premium pay computation when a work day falls on a gazetted holiday
technicalNotes: Employment Act 1955, Section 60D and Second Schedule: 11 mandatory gazetted public holidays
Malaysian public holidays by state: gazette available at MyGovernment portal
State-specific holidays: e.g., Johor Sultan's Birthday, Selangor Sultan's Birthday, Penang Governor's Birthday â€” different dates per state
Public holidays can be published mid-year (e.g., election days) â€” system should allow ad-hoc additions
MOHR gazette reference: https://www.mohr.gov.my
dependencies: Implement Public Holiday Work Pay Calculator (Section 60D Employment Act)

userStories.title: Track HRD Corp Levy Utilization Rate and Alert on 15% Under-Utilisation Penalty
userStories.description: From 1 March 2025, HRD Corp imposes a 15% deduction on accumulated unused levy balance for employers with a balance of RM50,000+ AND a utilization rate below 50% of total contributions in the prior calendar year. Employers who fail to claim enough training by year-end face an automatic forfeiture. The system must track the ratio of approved training claims vs. levy contributions per year and warn HR managers when utilization is trending below the 50% threshold.
acceptanceCriteria: A new field or child table on Company or HRD Corp Settings stores year-to-date levy contributions and approved training claims in MYR
A dashboard tile on the LHDN Payroll workspace shows the current utilization percentage for the active calendar year
When utilization rate drops below 60% by 1 October of any year, an automatic email notification is sent to the HR Manager role
A background job computes utilization monthly: (total approved claims YTD) / (total levy contributions YTD) * 100
Report includes the employer's estimated 15% penalty exposure if training claims remain below 50% by year-end
Applies only to employers whose accumulated levy balance exceeds RM50,000
technicalNotes: HRD Corp eTRIS portal (etris.hrdcorp.gov.my) is where training claims are approved â€” store approved claim amounts manually or via CSV import
Levy base formula: (Basic Salary - Unpaid Leave + Fixed Allowances) x 1% per month
The 15% deduction is applied by HRD Corp directly from the levy balance â€” no employer action required to trigger it (only to avoid it)
Threshold conditions: levy balance >= RM50,000 AND utilization < 50%; both must be true
Grace period historically runs Jan-Feb; build alert cadence around Q3 warning and year-end final alert
dependencies: HRD Corp Levy Monthly Payment File and HRDF Employer Portal Integration
Implement HRDF Levy Contribution Base Classifier (Fixed Allowances vs. Variable Pay)

userStories.title: Pre-Submission e-PCB Plus Employee TIN and Tax Category Completeness Validation
userStories.description: LHDN announced that the e-PCB Plus system will outright reject CP39 submissions where any employee record is missing a valid TIN or tax category code. Unlike the existing per-employee on-save NRIC/TIN validation, this is a batch-level gate that must run before the payroll period is closed and the e-CP39 file is generated. Employers need a blocking report listing all employees in the current payroll cycle who are missing required fields so HR can resolve them before submission.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Run e-PCB Plus Pre-flight Check' button is available on the Payroll Period or Salary Slip List view
The check queries all Salary Slips in the selected period and flags employees missing: TIN (custom_lhdn_tin), PCB Category (1/2/3), and IC/Passport type
A blocking dialog is shown with the list of non-compliant employees; the e-CP39 generation action is disabled until all issues are resolved
A report titled 'e-PCB Plus Employee Data Gaps' is accessible from LHDN Developer Tools or the payroll workspace
Resolved employees are automatically excluded from the gap report on rerun
The pre-flight check can be scheduled as a reminder email to HR Manager 5 days before payroll close date
technicalNotes: e-PCB Plus rejects the entire batch if any employee row is missing TIN â€” individual row errors are not allowed
Required employee fields for e-PCB Plus: employee TIN, IC type (NRIC/Passport/Army), PCB category code (1/2/3)
Implement as a Frappe whitelisted API that returns a JSON list of non-compliant employees
Reference: LHDN advisory that employers update TIN details in payroll systems before e-PCB Plus launch
dependencies: Add PCB Category Code (1/2/3) on Employee with CP39 and EA Form integration
Fix CP39 CSV Format â€” Add All Mandatory LHDN e-PCB Plus Columns
Implement e-CP39 API Submission to LHDN MyTax / e-PCB Plus

userStories.title: Foreign Worker EPF Oct 2025 Mandate: Domestic Servant Category Exclusion Flag
userStories.description: The October 2025 EPF mandatory contribution for foreign workers explicitly excludes domestic servants (maids, cooks, cleaners, drivers, and gardeners employed in a private household). Employers who employ foreign domestic staff must not deduct or remit EPF at 2% for this category. The ERPNext system must allow flagging a foreign employee's job type as 'Domestic Servant' so the payroll engine skips EPF calculation for them, preventing over-contribution and incorrect i-Akaun CSV entries.
acceptanceCriteria: A checkbox or select field 'Is Domestic Servant (EPF Excluded)' is added to the Employee doctype via fixture, visible only when custom_is_foreign_worker is checked
PCB/EPF salary component logic skips EPF contribution for employees where this flag is enabled and the employee is a non-Malaysian citizen
The EPF i-Akaun CSV export excludes rows for domestic servant foreign employees entirely
A validation warning is shown when an HR user tries to manually add EPF to a domestic servant foreign employee's salary slip
Unit test asserts zero EPF on a domestic servant foreign employee slip after Oct 2025 effective date
The flag defaults to False for all existing foreign employees (non-breaking change)
technicalNotes: Defined exclusion categories per KWSP mandate: domestic servants including maids, cooks, cleaners, drivers, and gardeners employed in private households
Foreign workers on valid Employment Pass (EP) or Temporary Employment Visit Pass (PLKS) are covered; domestic workers typically hold Domestic Helper Pass
Effective date: October 2025 wage month, contribution due 15 November 2025
Add custom_is_domestic_servant Boolean field to Employee via custom_field.json fixture
KWSP circular reference: mandatory-contribution page on kwsp.gov.my
dependencies: Implement Foreign Worker EPF Mandatory Contribution (Effective October 2025)
Generate EPF i-Akaun Electronic Upload File

userStories.title: EPF i-Akaun CSV: Employee Citizenship Type Code for Malaysian vs Foreign Worker 2% Rate Differentiation
userStories.description: From October 2025, the EPF i-Akaun CSV upload must correctly differentiate between Malaysian employees (11%/12-13% rates) and foreign worker employees (2%/2% rates) using the citizenship type code in the contribution file. The existing EPF CSV generator uses a single rate calculation path. The file format requires a specific column distinguishing Malaysian from non-Malaysian employees so KWSP can apply the correct rate table. Without this, foreign worker contributions may be processed at the wrong rate or rejected.
acceptanceCriteria: The EPF i-Akaun CSV export includes a 'Citizen Type' column with values: '1' for Malaysian and '2' for non-Malaysian
For non-Malaysian employees, the employee contribution amount reflects the 2% rate and employer contribution reflects the 2% rate
For Malaysian employees, existing rates (11% employee, 12% or 13% employer) remain unchanged
Domestic servant foreign employees (with the exclusion flag set) are omitted from the CSV
A test asserts that a payroll run with both Malaysian and foreign employees produces a CSV with correct rate and citizenship columns for each
The CSV format matches the KWSP i-Akaun portal current upload specification for mixed-nationality payrolls
technicalNotes: KWSP i-Akaun CSV format: EPF_iAkaun.csv â€” row fields include employer code, period, employee identifier, employee contribution amount, employer contribution amount, and employee type
The citizenship differentiation may be achieved via a 'Membership Type' column or by the employee identifier type (NRIC = Malaysian, Passport/FW = Foreign)
Foreign worker EPF rates effective Oct 2025: employee 2%, employer 2%
Verify current KWSP i-Akaun portal upload spec for exact column positions
Reference: payrollpanda.my i-Akaun CSV upload guide and kwsp.gov.my employer mandatory contribution page
dependencies: Generate EPF i-Akaun Electronic Upload File
Implement Foreign Worker EPF Mandatory Contribution (Effective October 2025)
Foreign Worker EPF Oct 2025 Mandate: Domestic Servant Category Exclusion Flag

userStories.title: Employee TIN Bulk Export and Import Utility for e-PCB Plus Readiness
userStories.description: LHDN has directed employers to update all employee TINs in their payroll systems before e-PCB Plus submission. Employers with large workforces need a bulk workflow: export a CSV of all employees with current TIN status, share with employees for self-verification, collect corrected TINs in bulk, and re-import. ERPNext currently has no dedicated TIN data export/import utility tailored to LHDN e-PCB Plus readiness requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: An 'Export Employee TIN Data' action on the Employee list exports a CSV with columns: Employee ID, Full Name, NRIC/Passport, Current TIN (custom_lhdn_tin), PCB Category, TIN Status (Filled/Missing)
The CSV includes only employees with active payroll (not resigned/terminated for more than 12 months)
A companion 'Import Employee TIN Data' action accepts the same CSV format with a corrected TIN column and updates Employee records in bulk with a confirmation dialog before committing
The import validates TIN format (alphanumeric, 12-14 characters, valid prefix) before saving
An import summary report shows: records updated, records skipped (no change), records with validation errors
The export and import workflow is accessible from both the Employee list view and the LHDN Developer Tools page
technicalNotes: LHDN TIN format for individuals: starts with prefix (SG, OG, D, C, etc.) followed by 10 digits
Employees below the tax threshold may legitimately have no TIN â€” the export should flag these as 'Missing/Not Required' vs. 'Missing/Required'
Import should use Frappe data import infrastructure or a custom whitelisted API batch endpoint
The export does not expose salary data â€” suitable for sharing with employees for self-correction
Reference: LHDN e-PCB Plus early announcement requiring TIN updates before submission
dependencies: Pre-Submission e-PCB Plus Employee TIN and Tax Category Completeness Validation
Integrate LHDN TIN validation API before submission

userStories.title: SOCSO/EIS Coverage Extension to Contract and Temporary Public Sector Employees (Oct 2024)
userStories.description: The SOCSO/EIS ceiling increase to RM6,000 effective October 2024 also extended mandatory SOCSO coverage to certain public sector employees on contract or temporary status under the Employees' Social Security Act 1969 (Act 4). Employers in the government or statutory body sector who hire contract/temporary staff must now enroll and contribute SOCSO/EIS for them. The ERPNext system needs an employment type classification to distinguish permanent public servant (exempt) from contract/temporary public sector (now covered).
acceptanceCriteria: A select field 'SOCSO/EIS Coverage Type' is added to the Employee doctype with options: Standard, Government Permanent (Exempt), Government Contract/Temporary (Covered)
Payroll engine applies SOCSO/EIS contributions for employees flagged as Government Contract/Temporary (Covered) even when employer entity is a government body
SOCSO Borang 8A and e-Caruman upload file include these employees correctly
A report identifies government-sector employees and their current SOCSO coverage classification for HR audit
The Sabah/Sarawak jurisdiction flag interacts correctly with this new public sector coverage flag
technicalNotes: Affected employee types: contract officers, temporary staff, seconded officers in public sector agencies
Permanent civil servants remain exempt from SOCSO under the Pensions Act
Amendment under SOCSO Act 1969 gazette effective 1 October 2024
EIS is also extended to the same category of public sector contract/temporary employees
Reference Crowe Malaysia advisory for specific gazette and circular numbers
dependencies: Enforce SOCSO/EIS Wage Ceiling Update to RM6,000 (October 2024 Amendment)
Add Sabah/Sarawak Labour Jurisdiction Flag on Employee

userStories.title: PCB 2025 Spec: Update TP1 Medical Relief Categories â€” Co-Payment Insurance, Mental Health, and COVID Tests
userStories.description: The LHDN 2025 PCB computation specification added expanded medical treatment expense relief categories that must be reflected in TP1 form options used for PCB calculation. New sub-categories include: medical costs under co-payment insurance/takaful products, mental health consultation fees, COVID-19 self-detection test kit purchases, and self-health monitoring equipment â€” all within the existing RM10,000 annual cap (with a RM1,000 sub-limit for the exam/COVID/mental health cluster). ERPNext TP1 relief configuration must be updated to present these new categories.
acceptanceCriteria: TP1 Employee Relief Declaration doctype includes new medical sub-category options: 'Medical (Co-Payment Insurance/Takaful)', 'Mental Health Consultation', 'COVID-19 Self-Test Kits', 'Self-Health Monitoring Equipment'
PCB calculator enforces the RM1,000 sub-limit for the exam/COVID/mental health cluster within the overall RM10,000 medical relief cap
The TP1 Explanatory Notes (2025) source is referenced in field help text for the new categories
EA Form Section B correctly maps each medical sub-category to the appropriate line item
Unit test verifies that claiming all new sub-categories still caps total medical relief at RM10,000
technicalNotes: Official TP1 Explanatory Notes 2025: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/hkzf5rqd/nota-penerangan-borang-tp1-2025-en.pdf
PCB 2025 Amendment spec PDF: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/mdahzjwi/spesifikasi-kaedah-pengiraan-berkomputer-pcb-2025.pdf
Co-payment insurance relief applies when employee medical insurance has a co-payment clause (patient pays a fixed % of bill)
Mental health consultation is a new standalone relief item for FY2025
All amounts claimed by employee on TP1 and factored into PCB monthly calculation by employer
dependencies: Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration
Update PCB TP1 Relief Tables for YA2025 (New Housing Loan Interest Relief and Increased Disability Amounts)

userStories.title: PRS Tax Relief Extension to YA2030 â€” Update PCB Calculator Eligibility Date Guard
userStories.description: The RM3,000 Private Retirement Scheme (PRS) and deferred annuity tax relief was originally set to expire at YA2025. Budget 2025 extended it for an additional 5 years to YA2030. If the existing PRS PCB integration contains a hardcoded expiry year check (YA <= 2025), it will incorrectly disable the relief from YA2026 onward. This story updates the expiry guard in the PCB calculation engine and TP1 validation to YA2030.
acceptanceCriteria: The PCB calculator correctly includes PRS/deferred annuity relief up to RM3,000 for tax years YA2026 through YA2030
Any hardcoded year comparison (e.g., assessment_year <= 2025) is updated to assessment_year <= 2030
A unit test verifies PRS relief applies for YA2026, YA2028, and YA2030, and is excluded for YA2031
The TP1 form PRS relief field displays an informational note indicating the relief is valid until YA2030
EA Form correctly reflects PRS relief claimed for YA2026 onwards
technicalNotes: PRS tax relief: RM3,000 per year of assessment, claimed via TP1 form item P11
Budget 2025 extended the relief from YA2025 to YA2030 â€” announced in Malaysia Budget 2025 documents
Check pcb_calculator.py and tp1_service.py for any year-based conditional that caps PRS deduction
Private Pension Administrator Malaysia (PPA) official page: https://www.ppa.my/prs-tax-relief/
No changes to the RM3,000 cap or deduction mechanism â€” only the expiry year changes
dependencies: Integrate Private Retirement Scheme (PRS) Voluntary Deduction with TP1 RM3,000 Relief

userStories.title: FWCMS Foreign Worker Levy: 10th-of-Month Payment Deadline Alert Separate from EPF/SOCSO
userStories.description: The FWCMS foreign worker levy must be paid by the 10th of the following month â€” five days earlier than EPF, SOCSO, and EIS which are all due by the 15th. Employers with foreign workers face this distinct, earlier deadline that is currently not differentiated in the compliance calendar. The system must surface a dedicated 10th-of-month alert for the FWCMS levy, separate from the 15th-of-month batch for other statutory contributions.
acceptanceCriteria: The Statutory Compliance Submission Calendar includes a distinct entry type 'FWCMS Foreign Worker Levy' with deadline set to the 10th of each month
An automated email reminder is sent to the HR Manager role on the 7th of each month listing the number of active foreign workers and the estimated levy amount due by the 10th
The compliance calendar view visually distinguishes the 10th FWCMS deadline from the 15th EPF/SOCSO/EIS deadlines
If the employer has zero active foreign workers in the current period, no reminder is sent
The reminder email includes a direct link to the FWCMS/eServices portal for payment
technicalNotes: FWCMS levy obligation starts when the foreign worker's valid work permit is issued
Payment portal: fwcms.imi.gov.my (Immigration Department of Malaysia)
Levy rate varies by sector and nationality â€” tracked via the existing FWCMS/MTLM implementation
The 10th deadline is distinct from EPF/SOCSO/EIS which are all due by the 15th each month
Mandatory monthly payment must be made before the 10th of the following month
dependencies: Implement Foreign Worker Levy Tracking (FWCMS / Multi-Tier Levy Model)
Track Statutory Compliance Submission Calendar and Auto-Escalate Overdue Filings

userStories.title: EA Form for Deceased Employee â€” Prorated Period and Estate Administrator Delivery
userStories.description: When an employee passes away mid-year, the employer is still legally obligated to issue the EA Form (CP8A) for the period the employee worked, to assist the estate administrator in settling the deceased's tax affairs with LHDN. The current EA Form generation logic assumes a full-year or end-of-employment-date cutoff. A deceased employee requires a prorated form with a special notation and delivery to the estate or executor, not the employee. A CP22B (notification of death) must also be submitted via e-SPC within 30 days.
acceptanceCriteria: The EA Form generation supports a 'Cessation Reason' parameter with an option for 'Death'
When Cessation Reason = Death, the EA Form income figures are prorated to the date of death (from system-stored termination date)
The generated EA Form PDF includes a header notation 'Issued to Estate of [Employee Name]' and the cessation date

A checklist reminder is shown to HR when marking an employee as terminated with reason 'Death': (1) Submit CP22B via e-SPC within 30 days, (2) Withhold final salary pending LHDN clearance if required, (3) Issue EA Form to estate administrator
EA Form can be issued to a configurable 'Estate Contact' email address stored on the termination record
Unit test verifies prorated income calculation for an employee who died on day 15 of month 6
technicalNotes: CP22B is the LHDN employer notification form for employee death â€” mandatory within 30 days of death
Prorated income = sum of all submitted salary slips up to and including the period of death
Final salary withholding: under s.83(3) ITA 1967, employer must withhold wages pending LHDN clearance
e-SPC module on MyTax handles CP22B submission (same portal as CP21 and CP22A)
Reference: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/employers/notifications-of-termination-of-service
dependencies: Generate EA Form (Borang EA) for employees
Rebuild EA Form to Include All Mandatory LHDN Section A/B/C/D Fields
Auto-Submit CP21/CP22A/CP22B via e-SPC MyTax at Cessation or Departure

userStories.title: e-PCB Plus Prior Month PCB Amendment â€” Trigger Corrected CP39 Row on Salary Slip Resubmission
userStories.description: When a submitted Salary Slip is cancelled and reissued (e.g., due to salary error, retroactive bonus, or overtime miscalculation), the PCB amount for that employee in the original month changes. The e-PCB Plus portal supports amendment submissions for prior months, where the employer submits corrected records. Currently, the system has no mechanism to detect that a prior-month salary slip amendment has changed PCB and needs to generate an amendment CP39 batch for re-upload to e-PCB Plus.
acceptanceCriteria: When a previously-submitted Salary Slip is cancelled and a new one is submitted for the same employee and period, a 'PCB Amendment Required' flag is set on the payroll period or employee's PCB ledger
A 'Generate CP39 Amendment File' action on the Payroll Period lists all employees with changed PCB vs. the originally submitted CP39 for that month
The amendment CP39 file contains only changed employee rows with the corrected PCB amount and a period reference (YYYYMM)
An audit log entry is created recording the original PCB amount, amended PCB amount, and the amendment submission date
A unit test verifies that cancelling and resubmitting a salary slip triggers the PCB amendment flag
The amendment file format matches the e-PCB Plus correction submission specification
technicalNotes: e-PCB Plus allows employers to submit correction data for prior months via the same CP39 upload with a correction period identifier
The existing CP39 generation produces the full current-month file; amendment generation should be delta-only (changed employees only)
Track 'originally submitted' PCB amounts by saving a snapshot of the CP39 data at submission time to a 'PCB Submission Log' child table or doctype
Amendment use cases: salary revision, bonus reclassification, TP3 carry-forward added after initial submission
Reference: e-PCB Plus full guide at landco.my
dependencies: Implement e-CP39 API Submission to LHDN MyTax / e-PCB Plus
Fix CP39 CSV Format â€” Add All Mandatory LHDN e-PCB Plus Columns
Add audit log for manual LHDN resubmissions

userStories.title: MyTax Employer Representative (Wakil Majikan) Configuration on Company for e-Services Submissions
userStories.description: Since September 2024, CP22 and since January 2025, CP21/CP22A/CP22B can only be submitted via MyTax. Only a Company Director OR a designated Employer Representative (Wakil Majikan) registered on MyTax can perform these submissions. ERPNext currently has no field to store which MyTax user is the authorized representative for a given company. Without this, automated and manual submission workflows lack a reference for who is responsible and authorized.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'MyTax Employer Representative' section is added to the Company doctype (or a linked 'LHDN e-Services Configuration' doctype) storing: Representative Name, MyTax Login ID (masked), Role (Director / Employer Representative), and Date Authorized
When more than one company exists, each company can configure its own MyTax representative independently
A validation warning appears on e-CP22/e-SPC submission workflows if no authorized representative is configured for the company
The representative name appears in the LHDN Submission audit log for CP22/CP21/CP22A/CP22B entries
A help tooltip explains the MyTax Wakil Majikan delegation process with a link to the LHDN user guide
technicalNotes: MyTax role delegation: Company Director appoints Wakil Majikan via MyTax > Employer > Appoint Representative menu
For automated API submissions, the representative's MyTax credentials or API token are required (stored securely, not in this configuration field)
Store only the representative's name and MyTax login ID â€” not the password
e-CP22 mandatory online since 1 September 2024; e-SPC mandatory since 1 January 2025
Reference: LHDN new employee notification page (hasil.gov.my) and e-CP22 user manual on MyTax portal
dependencies: Auto-Submit CP22 via e-CP22 MyTax Within 30 Days of New Employee Hire
Auto-Submit CP21/CP22A/CP22B via e-SPC MyTax at Cessation or Departure
Register PCB Administrator Role on MyTax e-PCB Plus and Store Credential Reference on Company

userStories.title: HRD Corp eTRIS Training Claim Recording and Levy Utilization Dashboard
userStories.description: Beyond generating levy payment files, employers must track approved training claims against accumulated levy balance to manage the 15% under-utilisation penalty risk. HRD Corp's eTRIS portal stores approved claims but ERPNext has no visibility into claim history. This story adds a lightweight claim recording feature: HR managers log approved eTRIS claims (date, amount, program name) and the system computes the running utilization ratio, projecting year-end exposure to the 15% penalty.
acceptanceCriteria: A new 'HRD Corp Training Claim' doctype allows recording: claim date, approved amount (MYR), program name, eTRIS claim reference number, and linked Company
A dashboard section on the LHDN Payroll workspace shows: Total Levy YTD, Total Claims Approved YTD, Utilization %, and 15% Penalty Exposure if utilization < 50%
Penalty exposure is computed as: (levy_balance >= 50000 AND utilization < 50%) ? (total_levy_ytd - total_claims_ytd) * 0.15 : 0
A year-end summary report shows month-by-month levy contribution vs. claims for audit purposes
When a new claim record is saved, the dashboard automatically recalculates the utilization ratio
An alert triggers if the employer's levy balance exceeds RM50,000 and utilization is below 50% after 30 September
technicalNotes: eTRIS portal at etris.hrdcorp.gov.my â€” claims are approved there; ERPNext records them manually or via CSV import from eTRIS export
Levy balance threshold for 15% penalty: RM50,000 and above (introduced as policy from YA2024)
Utilization formula: (sum of approved claims in calendar year) / (sum of levy contributions in calendar year) * 100
The 'levy balance' referred to by HRD Corp is the running accumulated balance, not just the current year's contribution
Reference: HRD Corp FAQ at hrdcorp.gov.my and OTC Training Centre advisory at otc.com.my
dependencies: Track HRD Corp Levy Utilization Rate and Alert on 15% Under-Utilisation Penalty
HRD Corp Levy Monthly Payment File and HRDF Employer Portal Integration

userStories.title: Generate PERKESO Borang 34 Accident Notification Within 48-Hour Statutory Deadline
userStories.description: Under the Employees' Social Security Act 1969, employers must report any workplace accident resulting in injury, disease, or death to SOCSO (PERKESO) within 48 hours using Borang 34 (Notice and Benefit Claim Form). The form requires employee personal details, employer SOCSO code, accident description, and a 6-month wage history for benefit calculation. Non-compliance is a criminal offence. ERPNext must surface a Borang 34 generation task the moment a workplace accident is logged so HR can file within the window.
acceptanceCriteria: A new 'Workplace Accident' DocType captures: incident date/time, employee, injury description, accident location, and supervisor witness name
On saving the accident record, system auto-computes the 48-hour statutory deadline and creates a high-priority Task assigned to the HR Manager role
Borang 34 PDF auto-populates with: employee name, NRIC, SOCSO number, job title, employer PERKESO code, accident description, and 6-month average gross wages derived from Salary Slip records
Task status escalates to 'Overdue' if Borang 34 is not marked 'Submitted' within 46 hours of accident logging
A separate '6-Month Wage History' attachment is auto-generated per Borang 34 requirements showing monthly gross wages for the 6 months prior to the accident
Submitted Borang 34 records are retained and linked to the employee for PERKESO audit purposes
technicalNotes: PERKESO Borang 34 PDF layout: assist.perkeso.gov.my â€” employer logs and submits claims here
Employer PERKESO code stored on Company; employee SOCSO number stored on Employee custom field
6-month wage average is used by PERKESO to compute the Temporary Disablement Benefit daily rate
Employers can submit Borang 34 online via PERKESO ASSIST portal after logging in
Separate claim form may be needed for Permanent Disablement or Dependent Benefit (death) â€” out of scope here

userStories.title: Employment Pass Minimum Salary Threshold Validator (June 2026 Revised EP Policy)
userStories.description: Malaysia's Expatriate Services Division (ESD) announced revised Employment Pass (EP) minimum salary thresholds effective 1 June 2026 following Cabinet approval on 17 October 2025: Category I increases from RM10,000 to RM20,000; Category II from RM5,000â€“RM9,999 to RM10,000â€“RM19,999; Category III from RM3,000â€“RM4,999 to RM5,000â€“RM9,999. Employers who pay expatriate employees below the new category threshold risk EP renewal rejection and immigration penalties. ERPNext must validate expatriate salary against the correct EP category minimum before each Salary Slip submission.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType has fields: 'Employment Pass Category' (Cat I / Cat II / Cat III / Not Applicable), 'Employment Pass Number', and 'EP Expiry Date'
A configurable 'EP Salary Threshold' table stores category minimums with effective dates (supporting future revisions without code changes)
On Salary Slip submission for an EP holder where period_end >= 2026-06-01, if gross salary is below the category minimum, submission is blocked with a clear warning citing the ESD policy
HR can record an override justification (e.g., 'Salary increment letter dated X pending MyTax update'), which is logged to the audit trail
A report 'Expatriate EP Salary Compliance' lists all EP holders, their category, current salary, category minimum, and compliance status
EP holders approaching expiry within 90 days are surfaced in the compliance dashboard for proactive renewal
technicalNotes: Official ESD announcement: esd.imi.gov.my/portal/latest-news/announcement/announcement-266-ep-salary-policy-2026
Category II EP applications also require JTKSM approval (Jabatan Tenaga Kerja Semenanjung Malaysia) under S.60K
Category II and III require a Local Succession Plan submitted to ESD from June 1, 2026
Store EP category thresholds in a DocType to allow update without code deployment when MOHA revises again

userStories.title: Auto-Generate TP3 YTD Income Statement for Departing Employees
userStories.description: LHDN's TP3 form allows a new employer to factor a departing employee's year-to-date income from their previous employer into the PCB calculation. The current PRD covers the incoming TP3 workflow (new hires declare prior-employer YTD). This story covers the outgoing side: when an employee leaves mid-year, the current employer must generate a TP3 data document containing the full YTD earnings, statutory deductions, PCB paid, and approved reliefs so the employee can hand it to their next employer for accurate PCB. Without this, the next employer may under-deduct PCB.
acceptanceCriteria: When an Employee record is set to 'Left' status or a Final Settlement Salary Slip is submitted, a 'Generate Outgoing TP3' button appears on the employee record

Generated TP3 PDF is pre-filled with: current employer name and tax reference number, employee full name and NRIC and tax file number, YTD basic salary, YTD gross allowances, BIK value, EPF employee contribution YTD, SOCSO YTD, EIS YTD, Zakat YTD, CP38 YTD, and PCB deducted YTD
YTD figures aggregate from all submitted Salary Slips between 1 January and the employee's last working day
The generated TP3 uses the official LHDN TP3 layout (form code and current year are printed on the PDF)
System saves the generated TP3 record against the Employee for 7-year retention per LHDN audit requirements
The TP3 can be emailed directly to the departing employee from the record
technicalNotes: TP3 is NOT submitted to LHDN â€” it is an employer-to-employee document only; stress this in UI
Official TP3 reference: hasil.gov.my/en/employers/employer-payroll-data-specification
Employer should also update CP39 with final-month PCB before generating the TP3 to ensure figures match
PCB YTD on the TP3 must match the cumulative CP39 rows for this employee in the current calendar year
If the employee had a TP3 incoming (from previous employer) on hire, those prior-employer figures should NOT appear on this outgoing TP3 (only current employer income)
dependencies: Implement TP3 Carry-Forward Declaration for New Hires (Prior Employer YTD)

userStories.title: Extend Minimum Wage Validation to Cover Micro-Employers from August 1, 2025
userStories.description: Malaysia's Minimum Wages Order 2022 raised the minimum wage to RM1,700/month effective 1 February 2025 for employers with 5 or more employees. A grace period was granted to micro-employers (1â€“4 employees) until 1 August 2025, after which RM1,700 applies universally to ALL employers regardless of headcount. The existing PRD story ('Update Minimum Wage Validation to RM1,700') covers the February 2025 implementation for larger employers. This story adds the employer-headcount-sensitive validation so micro-employers are correctly brought into scope on August 1, 2025.
acceptanceCriteria: A configurable 'Minimum Wage Schedule' table stores: effective date, minimum wage amount, and applicable employer headcount threshold (e.g., '2025-02-01: RM1,700 for â‰¥5 employees'; '2025-08-01: RM1,700 for all')
At Salary Slip creation, system counts active employees on the Company as of the payroll period end date and applies the threshold relevant to that employer's size and the period date
For payroll periods ending 2025-02-01 to 2025-07-31 with employer headcount <5, no minimum wage validation applies (grace period)
For payroll periods ending 2025-08-01 onwards, RM1,700 minimum wage is enforced for all employer sizes
HR can record a MOHR exemption reference number to override the block for approved exceptions
A report lists employees whose fixed components total below RM1,700 grouped by employer size and effective date band
technicalNotes: Source: Malaysia Minimum Wages Order 2022 (Perintah Gaji Minimum 2022) and August 2025 gazette extension
Employer headcount = count of active Employees on Company as of Salary Slip period_end
Allowances, overtime, and bonuses cannot offset the RM1,700 basic wage requirement
Minimum wage for part-time employees is prorated proportionally to actual hours worked
MOHR enforcement contact: Jabatan Tenaga Kerja Semenanjung Malaysia (JTKSM)
dependencies: Update Minimum Wage Validation to RM1,700 (Feb 2025 Amendment)

userStories.title: e-PCB Plus Post-Submission Rejection Record Tracking and Targeted Resubmission Workflow
userStories.description: When an employer submits a CP39 batch to e-PCB Plus (MyTax), the system may reject individual employee rows due to invalid or missing TIN, mismatched NRIC, or incorrect tax category code. LHDN confirmed that incomplete data submissions are not processed and must be corrected and resubmitted. The existing PRD covers pre-submission validation (before upload). This story handles the post-submission response: parsing per-employee rejection codes from the e-PCB Plus API response, surfacing actionable errors to HR, and enabling targeted resubmission of only the failed rows without re-uploading the entire batch.
acceptanceCriteria: After submitting to e-PCB Plus, system parses the API response for per-employee error codes and stores them against the corresponding Salary Slip and CP39 row

An 'e-PCB Plus Submission Errors' panel on the Payroll Period or LHDN Developer Tools page shows: employee name, NRIC, rejection error code, human-readable error description, and a 'Fix & Resubmit' action
Common LHDN e-PCB Plus error codes are mapped to plain-language messages (e.g., 'TIN_NOT_FOUND: Employee TIN not registered in LHDN system â€” update employee TIN and resubmit')
HR can correct the employee data and trigger resubmission for only the rejected rows (not the full batch)
Resubmission creates a new CP39 amendment record for the affected employees with 'Amendment Reason: Rejection Correction'
HR Manager receives an email within 1 hour of any e-PCB Plus batch containing rejection responses
All rejection records and resubmission attempts are retained in the LHDN Submission audit log
technicalNotes: e-PCB Plus API documentation: MyTax developer portal (requires registered employer account)
Store rejection payload in a 'PCB Submission Error Log' DocType linked to CP39 submission record
Resubmission must not create a duplicate CP39 row â€” update original row status to 'Corrected' and add resubmission reference
Differs from pre-submission validation (US-pre-flight): this story is about handling actual API rejection responses after upload
dependencies: Implement e-CP39 API Submission to LHDN MyTax / e-PCB Plus
Pre-Submission e-PCB Plus Employee TIN and Tax Category Completeness Validation

userStories.title: PCB Late Payment Surcharge Computation and Advance Warning (Section 107C ITA 1967)
userStories.description: Under Section 107C(9) of the Income Tax Act 1967, if an employer fails to remit Monthly Tax Deduction (PCB/MTD) by the 15th of the following month, LHDN automatically imposes a 10% surcharge on the outstanding PCB amount. This surcharge is significant on large payrolls and cannot be waived retrospectively. ERPNext must (1) warn HR proactively before the 15th deadline and (2) compute the expected surcharge when recording a late PCB payment, so that penalty costs are tracked and reconciled against LHDN's assessed amounts.
acceptanceCriteria: Each CP39 submission record has a computed 'PCB Payment Due Date' = 15th of the month following the payroll period (adjusted for weekends/public holidays if applicable)
If CP39 payment status is not 'Paid' by the due date, system flags the record 'Overdue' and computes 10% surcharge on the outstanding PCB total
HR Manager receives an automated email alert 5 working days before the CP39 payment due date with: total PCB amount due, due date, and link to the payment record
When HR records a late payment, system prompts for the actual LHDN-assessed surcharge amount (LHDN may compute differently) and stores both the estimated (10%) and the actual assessed surcharge
A 'PCB Compliance Summary' report includes a 'Late Payment History' tab listing all overdue periods, surcharge estimated vs. actual, and total penalties YTD
Late payment events appear on the Statutory Compliance Calendar with a red overdue indicator
technicalNotes: Source: Income Tax Act 1967, Section 107C(9) â€” 10% penalty on the amount that ought to have been deducted
Surcharge formula: outstanding_PCB_amount Ã— 0.10 (flat, regardless of days late)
LHDN does not automatically grant a grace period for public holidays or weekends on the 15th â€” verify with local tax counsel
Store surcharge tracking fields on the CP39 DocType: due_date, payment_date, is_late, estimated_surcharge, actual_surcharge_assessed
The 'Track Statutory Compliance Submission Calendar' story covers calendar display; this story adds the PCB penalty calculation logic
dependencies: Implement e-CP39 API Submission to LHDN MyTax / e-PCB Plus
Track Statutory Compliance Submission Calendar and Auto-Escalate Overdue Filings

userStories.title: Borang E Nil Return Submission Workflow for Dormant and Zero-Employee Companies
userStories.description: LHDN mandates that ALL employers â€” including dormant companies and newly incorporated entities with zero employees â€” must submit Borang E annually by 31 March (or 30 April via e-Filing). Failure attracts fines of RM200 to RM20,000. Companies with no employees must file a nil return declaring no employees hired. The current system only generates Borang E/CP8D when Salary Slips exist; it cannot produce nil returns for zero-employee entities, causing silent non-compliance for holding companies and dormant entities.
acceptanceCriteria: Company DocType has a field 'Employee Status for Tax Year' with options: Has Employees / No Employees (Nil Return)
When 'No Employees (Nil Return)' is selected, system generates a Borang E PDF with employer name, employer tax reference, submission year, and '0' in all employee count and income fields
Nil return CP8D is a zero-row file with valid header â€” system generates both the Borang E PDF and the CP8D file for nil returns
A compliance reminder fires on 1 February each year for all Companies flagged as nil-return, warning HR to file by the 31 March deadline
Nil Borang E submission status tracks: Draft, Filed, Acknowledged â€” same workflow as regular Borang E
A report 'Annual Tax Filing Status' shows all Companies, their employee status, and Borang E filing status for the current year
technicalNotes: Companies (Sdn Bhd, Bhd) can ONLY file Borang E via e-Filing (MyTax e-E); non-company entities may file via postal or walk-in
Nil return CP8D = header row + zero data rows; the file must still be in the correct LHDN e-PCB Plus format
Employer tax reference (SG/OG/C number) is still required on the nil return
Source: hasil.gov.my/en/employers/responsibility-of-employer and LHDN Borang E 2024 sample form
dependencies: Generate Borang E (Form E) employer annual return

userStories.title: Enforce Paternity Leave 7-Day Consecutive Pay at ORP with EA Section 37A Eligibility Rules
userStories.description: The Employment (Amendment) Act 2022 Section 37A mandates 7 consecutive days of paid paternity leave at the Ordinary Rate of Pay (ORP) for eligible male employees. Eligibility: married, employed â‰¥12 months with current employer, maximum 5 births per employee over the full tenure. The existing PRD story 'Track Maternity/Paternity Leave and Validate Maternity Pay Rate' addresses tracking broadly. This story enforces the specific statutory constraints â€” consecutive-day requirement, 5-birth cap per employer, 12-month service eligibility check â€” which are MOHR-auditable requirements with penalty risk if violated.
acceptanceCriteria: Leave Application DocType for 'Paternity Leave' type validates that selected dates are 7 consecutive calendar days and rejects non-consecutive selections with a descriptive error

On paternity leave submission, system checks cumulative approved paternity events for the employee at this employer; if â‰¥5 previous approved events exist, the new application is blocked as ineligible
System checks employee service duration: if employment start date to leave start date is <12 months, a warning is raised requiring HR override with justification
Salary Slip for a period containing paternity leave auto-includes a 'Paternity Leave Pay' earning line equal to 7 Ã— daily ORP (monthly basic salary Ã· 26)
Paternity leave pay is subject to PCB (unlike maternity allowance which is exempt); the earning component must be flagged as PCB-applicable
All paternity leave events are retained per Employee for MOHR audit inspection with dates and any override justifications
technicalNotes: Source: Employment Act 1955 Section 37A as inserted by Employment (Amendment) Act 2022, effective 1 January 2023
ORP divisor: 26 days for monthly-rated employees per EA Third Schedule; verify against company's custom divisor if configured
Paternity leave applies to married male employees in the private sector â€” does not apply to civil servants (governed by JPA circulars)
Store paternity leave event count in a child table on Employee with dates, birth certificate reference, and approved/rejected status
5-birth cap is per employer (not lifetime) â€” if employee switches employers, the count resets at the new employer
dependencies: Track Maternity/Paternity Leave and Validate Maternity Pay Rate
Implement Ordinary Rate of Pay (ORP) Calculator and Overtime Validation

userStories.title: BIK Motor Vehicle Engine Capacity Prescribed Value Table Lookup for EA Form
userStories.description: LHDN's Public Ruling No. 3/2013 (Benefits-in-Kind) defines a graduated prescribed value table for employer-provided motor vehicles based on engine displacement (cc). Different cc bands have different annual prescribed values, e.g., â‰¤1,000cc = RM1,200/year; 2,001â€“2,500cc = RM9,000/year. The existing PRD story 'Implement Benefits-in-Kind (BIK) Prescribed Value Calculation Module' addresses general BIK as a broader feature. This story specifically implements the motor vehicle engine capacity lookup table required for accurate EA Form Section D reporting when the formula method cannot be used.
acceptanceCriteria: A configurable 'BIK Motor Vehicle Prescribed Value' DocType stores engine capacity bands (cc range) and corresponding annual prescribed values per LHDN gazette
Employee Benefit record for asset type 'Company Car' or 'Motorcycle' has fields: 'Engine Capacity (cc)', 'Petrol Provided (Y/N)', 'Driver Provided (Y/N)'
Monthly BIK value auto-computes as: (prescribed annual value from table Ã· 12) + (RM1,500/12 if petrol provided) + (RM600/month if driver provided)
EA Form Section D auto-populates the vehicle BIK value with a method indicator: 'Prescribed Value' vs 'Formula Method'
Motorcycle prescribed value follows LHDN's separate motorcycle table (lower values than cars)
A unit test asserts correct BIK monthly value for sample cc bands (e.g., 1,800cc engine should return RM4,500/12 = RM375/month prescribed value)
technicalNotes: LHDN Public Ruling No. 3/2013 (updated): prescribed value method used when vehicle cost to employer is unknown or vehicle is provided without ownership transfer
Annual prescribed values range from RM1,200 (â‰¤1,000cc) to RM50,000+ (>3,000cc) â€” store full table in DocType
Petrol add-on is a fixed RM1,500/year regardless of actual fuel cost
Formula method = (cost of vehicle Ã— 2/3) Ã— depreciation rate â€” this is a separate calculation path, not covered here
dependencies: Implement Benefits-in-Kind (BIK) Prescribed Value Calculation Module
Rebuild EA Form to Include All Mandatory LHDN Section A/B/C/D Fields

userStories.title: LHDN STAMPS Employment Contract Digital Stamp Status Tracker
userStories.description: Under the Stamp Act 1949, employment agreements are chargeable instruments and must be stamped to be legally admissible as evidence. LHDN provides e-Stamping via the STAMPS platform (stamps.hasil.gov.my), allowing employers to digitally stamp employment contracts for RM10 per instrument. Employers who fail to stamp contracts risk documents being inadmissible in court and LHDN penalty assessments for unstamped instruments. ERPNext should track stamping status per employment contract and alert HR to any unstamped contracts.
acceptanceCriteria: An 'Employment Contract' DocType (or extension to Employee) has fields: Stamp Reference Number, Stamp Date, Stamp Amount (RM10), and Stamping Method (STAMPS Digital / Manual Legacy / Pre-STAMPS Legacy)
When a new Employment Contract record is saved without a Stamp Reference Number, system automatically creates a pending task for HR: 'Stamp employment contract via LHDN STAMPS (stamps.hasil.gov.my)'
HR enters the STAMPS reference number and stamp date after completing e-stamping, which resolves the task and marks the contract 'Stamped'
A report 'Unstamped Employment Contracts' lists all active employees whose current contract lacks a valid stamp reference, with days since contract start
Contracts started before the STAMPS era (pre-2021) can be marked 'Legacy Stamped' to suppress alerts
System does not integrate directly with STAMPS (no published API); it provides a clickable link to stamps.hasil.gov.my in the pending task for HR convenience
technicalNotes: STAMPS portal: stamps.hasil.gov.my â€” employer selects instrument type 'Contract of Employment', pays RM10 via FPX, receives a digital stamp certificate
Fixed stamp duty: RM10 per employment contract under First Schedule, Stamp Act 1949
Stamping obligation applies to the instrument (contract document), not the payroll record â€” this story only tracks compliance status
Employment contract re-stamping may be required if terms are materially varied (e.g., salary revision with new contract)
LHDN penalty for unstamped instrument: stamp duty + penalty up to 20Ã— the duty amount under Stamp Act 1949 S.47A

userStories.title: CP39 Monthly PCB Payment Mode Recording and JomPay/FPX Reference Slip Generation
userStories.description: e-PCB Plus requires that PCB payment can be made via multiple channels: FPX through MyTax portal, JomPay (Bill Code 20013), or bank transfer with a CP39 reference. However, there is no mechanism in the current ERPNext payroll to record which payment channel was used or to generate the reference information required for bank transfers or JomPay. Reconciliation mismatches occur at LHDN when the bank transfer reference does not clearly identify the CP39 submission. This story adds payment mode tracking and reference slip generation for non-FPX channels.
acceptanceCriteria: CP39 DocType has a 'PCB Payment Mode' field with options: FPX via MyTax, JomPay (Bill Code 20013), Cheque, Bank Transfer (IBG/RENTAS)

When payment mode is 'Bank Transfer', system generates a payment reference slip with: employer tax reference number, CP39 batch submission reference, payroll period (YYYYMM), total PCB amount, and bank payment instructions
When payment mode is 'JomPay', system displays Bill Code 20013 and the required Ref1 format (employer tax reference) and Ref2 format (payroll period YYYYMM)
CP39 submission status progresses from 'Submitted (Data)' to 'Paid' only after HR records the payment mode and transaction reference/date
A reconciliation report 'CP39 Payment vs Submission' matches each CP39 data submission against its payment record for audit purposes
FPX payments via MyTax are self-confirmed in the portal; HR records the MyTax payment receipt number externally in ERPNext
technicalNotes: JomPay Bill Code for LHDN PCB payments: 20013 (verified from LHDN payment guide on hasil.gov.my)
Bank transfer: employer must include the e-PCB Plus submission reference number as the bank transfer description/payment details
FPX payment through MyTax is completed in-portal after uploading the CP39 data file â€” ERPNext records this outcome, not the payment itself
Source: e-PCB Plus full submission guide at bispointgroup.com/blogs/e-pcb-plus-submission-payment-guide
dependencies: Implement e-CP39 API Submission to LHDN MyTax / e-PCB Plus
Fix CP39 CSV Format â€” Add All Mandatory LHDN e-PCB Plus Columns

userStories.title: Fix failing test: SalarySlipCustomFields â€” lp07 custom lhdn status field exists
userStories.description: Automated test `test_lp07_custom_lhdn_status_field_exists` is failing with status FAIL. This indicates a regression or missing implementation in the unit:lhdn_payroll suite. LP-07: custom_lhdn_status field exists on Salary Slip.
acceptanceCriteria: Test `tests.unit.lhdn_payroll.test_payroll_doctypes.TestSalarySlipCustomFields.test_lp07_custom_lhdn_status_field_exists` passes without error.

Root cause resolved: 'custom_lhdn_status' not found in ['employee_and_payroll_tab', 'section_break_6', 'employee', 'employee_name', 'company', 'department', 'designation', 'branch', 'column_break_obdl', 'posting_date', 'l
technicalNotes: Test category: unit:lhdn_payroll
Test ID: tests.unit.lhdn_payroll.test_payroll_doctypes.TestSalarySlipCustomFields.test_lp07_custom_lhdn_status_field_exists
Error type: AssertionError
Test description: LP-07: custom_lhdn_status field exists on Salary Slip.

userStories.title: Fix failing test: SalarySlipCustomFields â€” lp09 epf fields exist
userStories.description: Automated test `test_lp09_epf_fields_exist` is failing with status FAIL. This indicates a regression or missing implementation in the unit:lhdn_payroll suite. LP-09: EPF employee/employer fields exist on Salary Slip.
acceptanceCriteria: Test `tests.unit.lhdn_payroll.test_payroll_doctypes.TestSalarySlipCustomFields.test_lp09_epf_fields_exist` passes without error.
Root cause resolved: False is not true : Expected at least 2 EPF fields, got: []

userStories.title: Display EPF Akaun Fleksibel Three-Account Contribution Split on Payslip
userStories.description: Effective 11 May 2024, EPF contributions are automatically split across three accounts: 75% Akaun Persaraan (retirement), 15% Akaun Sejahtera (well-being), and 10% Akaun Fleksibel (flexible). Employee payslips must show the breakdown per account for transparency and to allow employees to verify their allocations match EPF records. The existing EPF i-Akaun upload file story covers contribution submission but does not address the three-account display on payslips.
acceptanceCriteria: Payslip shows three separate EPF line items: Akaun Persaraan (75%), Akaun Sejahtera (15%), and Akaun Fleksibel (10%) of the total EPF contribution amount
Calculation correctly splits employee contribution (11% or 9% depending on age/salary) and employer contribution (12% or 13%) into the three-account proportions
For employees above age 55 who have not joined the three-account restructuring, payslip shows the two-account legacy split
Three-account breakdown appears in both PDF payslip and Employment Act S.61 payslip export
Unit test verifies RM1,000 employee EPF contribution splits as RM750 / RM150 / RM100 across the three accounts
technicalNotes: KWSP confirmed the 75/15/10 three-account structure applies to all contributions after 11 May 2024
The split ratio is fixed by EPF policy â€” no employer or employee customisation is permitted
Foreign employees under the 2% mandatory EPF rule (effective October 2025) follow the same three-account split
Implement as computed display fields on Salary Slip â€” actual routing to sub-accounts is performed by EPF, not employer
dependencies: Generate EPF i-Akaun Electronic Upload File
Generate Employment Act S.61 Compliant Payslip with All Mandatory Fields

userStories.title: Implement PDPA 2024 Amendment Payroll Data Protection Compliance Module
