userStories.description: The Personal Data Protection (Amendment) Act 2024 (Royal Assent October 2024, fully effective 1 June 2025) introduces mandatory Data Protection Officer (DPO) appointment, data breach notification to PDPC within 72 hours, increased fines up to RM1 million, and employee right to data portability. Payroll systems process sensitive personal data (NRIC, salary, bank account, tax file, EPF number) and must implement consent tracking, access audit logs, and breach response workflows. No existing PRD story covers PDPA compliance.
acceptanceCriteria: System records employee consent for payroll data processing (NRIC, salary, bank, EPF, SOCSO numbers) with timestamp and consent version
All payroll data access events (Salary Slip read, export, third-party transmission to LHDN/EPF/SOCSO portals) are logged with user, timestamp, and data category
Company DocType has a DPO contact section (name, email, phone); DPO receives alert emails on high-risk data events such as bulk export or unusual access
Payroll data retention enforcer: system flags Salary Slips older than the configured retention period (default 7 years per MOHR and LHDN requirements) for archival review
A 'Data Subject Request' workflow allows HR Manager to export a single employee's complete payroll data history as a portable JSON/CSV (data portability right)
Unit test confirms an audit log entry is created on every Salary Slip read, print, and email action
technicalNotes: PDPA Amendment Act 2024 â€” mandatory DPO appointment effective 1 June 2025 for organisations processing large volumes of sensitive personal data
Section 12 of amended PDPA: data controllers AND data processors now have direct PDPA obligations
Breach notification obligation: notify PDPC 'as soon as practicable'; notify affected data subjects if breach likely to cause significant harm
Penalties increased to RM1 million (5x previous maximum); 3 years imprisonment for serious violations
Implement as: (1) Employee Payroll Consent DocType, (2) Frappe audit trail hooks on Salary Slip read/export, (3) Company-level DPO settings tab
dependencies: Implement Payroll Wage Register with 6-Year Retention and LHDN/MOHR Audit Export

userStories.title: Employer Statutory Registration Onboarding Checklist with Deadline Alerts
userStories.description: When a new company begins operations with employees, it faces strict statutory registration deadlines with significant penalties for non-compliance: EPF within 7 working days of first hire (penalty: RM10,000 / 3 years jail), SOCSO within 30 days (penalty: RM5,000 / 2 years jail), LHDN E-Number via e-Daftar within 7 days, EIS automatic with SOCSO, and HRDF from date of liability for 10+ employees. No existing PRD story guides new employers through the registration checklist or alerts on pending/overdue statutory registrations.
acceptanceCriteria: Company DocType has a Statutory Registration tab with fields for EPF Employer Number, SOCSO Employer Number, LHDN E-Number, EIS Registration Number, and HRD Corp Registration Number
When a company has active employees but EPF Employer Number is blank for more than 7 working days from the first employee hire date, system flags as OVERDUE with daily email escalation to HR Manager
When a company has active employees but SOCSO Number is blank for more than 30 days from first hire date, system raises a compliance warning
When Malaysian employee count on a Company crosses 10, system prompts HR Manager to register with HRD Corp
Registration checklist shows status (Registered / Pending / Overdue) for each statutory body with clickable links to official registration portals
Scheduled daily job scans all companies and employees to detect registration gaps
technicalNotes: EPF: Employees Provident Fund Act 1991 â€” 7 working days from date first employee commences employment
SOCSO: Employees' Social Security Act 1969 Section 7 â€” register within 30 days of first hire
LHDN e-Daftar: employers must register within 7 days of employing first staff; application cancelled if docs not received in 14 days
HRDF: PSMB Act 2001 Regulation 4(1) â€” compulsory from date employer becomes liable (10+ Malaysian employees in covered sectors)
Implement as scheduled Frappe job (daily check) with notification escalation pattern

userStories.title: Implement 60-Day PCB Exemption Counter for Short-Term Non-Resident Workers
userStories.description: Under LHDN tax rules, non-resident employees are fully exempt from income tax (and PCB) if their total employment period in Malaysia is 60 days or fewer within a calendar year. This exemption applies to all non-residents regardless of DTA coverage and is a distinct mechanism from the DTA 183-day tracker. When the 60-day threshold is exceeded mid-year, the employee retroactively becomes a taxable non-resident at 30% flat rate. Directors are explicitly excluded from this exemption. Employers must track cumulative working days in Malaysia per non-resident employee.
acceptanceCriteria: Non-resident Employee records have a 'Days in Malaysia (YTD)' counter, auto-incremented from the number of payroll days covered in each submitted Salary Slip
When counter is â‰¤ 60 days, PCB is set to RM0 and payslip notes 'PCB Exempt â€” Short-Term Non-Resident (S.8(3) ITA 1967)'
When counter crosses 60 days, system flags the employee for PCB correction: generates a corrected CP39 row at 30% of total YTD gross income and deducts the shortfall from the next payroll run
Directors (where Employee Type = Director) are blocked from 60-day exemption regardless of day count
System raises a pre-emptive alert when a non-resident employee is projected to cross 60 days within the next 5 working days
Unit test: employee with 55 days in Malaysia has RM0 PCB; employee whose 61st working day is processed triggers 30% flat-rate PCB correction
technicalNotes: Section 8(3) of the Income Tax Act 1967 â€” employment income exempt if employment period in Malaysia is 60 days or less per calendar year
LHDN 'Tax Treatment Residents & Non-Residents' publication explicitly confirms 60-day rule; directors and public entertainers are excluded
Distinct from the DTA 183-day tracker: the 60-day rule is a domestic exemption; DTA exemptions require treaty-country residency certification
When threshold is crossed mid-year, employer must submit corrected CP39 rows for all prior months via e-PCB Plus amendment flow
Days straddling two calendar years are counted independently per year
dependencies: Add non-resident flat-rate 30% tax flag and handling
Implement DTA 183-Day Residency Tracker and Short-Term Assignee PCB Exemption Workflow
e-PCB Plus Prior Month PCB Amendment â€” Trigger Corrected CP39 Row on Salary Slip Resubmission

userStories.title: Alert on EPF New Employee Registration 7-Day Statutory Deadline
userStories.description: Employers must register every new employee with EPF within 7 working days from commencement of employment. Failure to comply attracts a fine of up to RM10,000 or imprisonment of up to 3 years. While the PRD covers SOCSO Borang 3 new employee notification, it does not address EPF's shorter 7-working-day registration window. A daily alert system should identify employees whose EPF Member Number has not been recorded within the statutory window and escalate to HR Manager.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType has an EPF Member Number field; if blank after 7 working days from Date of Joining, a desk notification and email alert is triggered to HR Manager
Alerts distinguish between 'Approaching Deadline' (day 5-6) and 'OVERDUE' (day 7+) with increasing urgency
Scheduled daily job scans all active employees with no EPF Member Number and hire date older than 5 working days
Alert email includes employee name, department, hire date, working days elapsed, and a direct link to the KWSP i-Akaun employer portal
Once EPF Member Number is entered on the Employee record, alert auto-resolves and logs the resolution date and user
Unit test confirms 7-day window is computed in working days, excluding Gazetted Public Holidays from the calendar
technicalNotes: Employees Provident Fund Act 1991 â€” employer must register each new employee within 7 working days from date employment commences
Penalty: fine not exceeding RM10,000 OR imprisonment not exceeding 3 years, or both
Registration via KWSP i-Akaun (employer portal) â€” employee's NRIC is required to generate EPF member number
Separate from SOCSO Borang 3 (30-day window); EPF has a stricter 7-working-day deadline
Implement as a scheduled Frappe job (daily) with the same notification pattern as other LHDN compliance alerts
dependencies: Implement SOCSO Borang 3 â€” New Employee Notification to PERKESO
Track Statutory Compliance Submission Calendar and Auto-Escalate Overdue Filings

userStories.title: Track Flexible Work Arrangement Requests and 60-Day Employer Response Deadline
userStories.description: Employment Act 2022 Amendment (Sections 60P and 60Q, effective 1 January 2023) grants employees the right to apply in writing for flexible working arrangements (FWA) covering hours, days, or place of work. Employers must respond within 60 days in writing; rejections must state reasons. Approved FWA changes to hours or location affect ORP and overtime computations. Updated MOHR/TalentCorp FWA Implementation Guidelines were issued in December 2024. No existing PRD story addresses FWA request tracking or compliance.
acceptanceCriteria: New DocType 'FWA Request' linked to Employee with fields: application date, FWA type (hours/days/place), current arrangement, requested arrangement, decision (Approved/Rejected), decision date, written rejection reason, and FWA duration (temporary/permanent/rotational)
System calculates 60-calendar-day response deadline from application date and sends HR Manager alerts at day 50 (upcoming) and day 61+ (overdue)
Approved FWA records update the employee's contracted hours/location fields used in ORP and overtime calculations
Rejection without a written reason is blocked at DocType level (rejection reason is mandatory when status = Rejected)
FWA request log is viewable per employee with full audit trail suitable for MOHR inspection
Unit test confirms that an approved FWA cannot reduce statutory leave entitlements or eliminate overtime eligibility
technicalNotes: Employment Act 1955 Section 60P â€” employee right to apply for FWA; Section 60Q â€” employer must respond within 60 days in writing
MOHR and TalentCorp launched updated FWA Implementation Guidelines in December 2024
Rejection must state specific grounds; employer cannot simply decline without documented reason under the amended Act
An approved FWA changes the employee's working pattern baseline used for ORP calculation â€” ensure ORP engine reads the approved FWA terms
Reference Act text: https://jtksm.mohr.gov.my/sites/default/files/2023-11/Akta%20Kerja%201955%20(Akta%20265)_0.pdf
dependencies: Implement Ordinary Rate of Pay (ORP) Calculator and Overtime Validation

userStories.title: Support HRD Corp Voluntary Levy Registration for 5-9 Employee SMEs at 0.5% Rate
userStories.description: Employers with 5â€“9 Malaysian employees may voluntarily register with HRD Corp and contribute at 0.5% of monthly wages (versus the mandatory 1% for 10+ employees). Voluntary registration grants SMEs full access to training grants and HRD Corp eTRiS subsidies. The existing HRDF implementation in the PRD covers only the mandatory 1% threshold. A voluntary registration pathway with the lower 0.5% rate, SME eligibility prompts, and levy calculation differentiation is required.
acceptanceCriteria: Company HRDF settings includes a 'Registration Type' field with options: Not Registered / Voluntary (5-9 employees, 0.5%) / Mandatory (10+ employees, 1%)
When Malaysian employee count is between 5 and 9 and Registration Type is Not Registered, system prompts HR Manager about optional HRD Corp voluntary registration with a training grant benefits note
HRDF levy calculation uses 0.5% rate when Registration Type is Voluntary and 1% when Mandatory
Levy base is identical for both tiers: basic salary + fixed allowances; excludes overtime, bonus, and commissions
When headcount crosses 10 Malaysian employees, system recommends upgrading Registration Type to Mandatory
Monthly Borang PSMB/6 payment file reflects the correct 0.5% or 1% rate based on Registration Type
technicalNotes: PSMB Act 2001 (Amended 2021): mandatory for 10+ Malaysian employees; voluntary for 5-9 employees at 0.5%
Payment deadline: 15th of following month for both voluntary and mandatory registrations
Voluntary registrants access the same HRD Corp eTRiS grant portal as mandatory contributors
Employers with fewer than 5 Malaysian employees are not eligible for any HRDF registration
Implement as additional enum option in existing HRDF configuration on Company DocType
dependencies: Fix HRDF Levy Rate â€” 1% Mandatory for All Employers with 10+ Employees
Implement HRDF Levy Contribution Base Classifier (Fixed Allowances vs. Variable Pay)

userStories.title: Capture Concurrent Employment Secondary Income for PCB Aggregation
userStories.description: When an employee holds concurrent employment with two or more employers simultaneously (e.g., a director also employed elsewhere, or intra-group multi-company employment), each employer independently calculates PCB only on its own payroll. This causes systematic under-deduction because the combined income pushes the employee into higher tax brackets. LHDN guidance requires employers to consider declared additional income when computing MTD. The existing TP3 story covers sequential prior-employer income; this story addresses concurrent same-period income from another employer.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee profile has a 'Concurrent Employment' section with fields: secondary employer name, monthly gross income from secondary employer, and monthly PCB deducted by secondary employer
When secondary income is declared, the primary employer's PCB calculator uses the aggregate monthly income (primary + secondary) to determine the applicable tax bracket and correct MTD amount
The additional PCB burden (from aggregation) is deducted from the primary employer's payslip and clearly labelled as 'Additional PCB â€” Concurrent Income'
System warns if the declared secondary employer PCB appears insufficient for the secondary income (below minimum rate for the income band)
EA Form and CP8D from primary employer reflect primary employer income only; a note indicates that employee has declared concurrent income
Unit test: employee with RM6,000 primary + RM2,000 declared secondary income computes PCB on RM8,000 aggregate monthly salary
technicalNotes: LHDN MTD guidelines â€” employer must base PCB on total remuneration including other employment income declared by the employee
Section 83(1) ITA 1967 places MTD responsibility on each employer for their own payments; concurrent aggregation requires voluntary employee declaration
Distinct from TP3 (prior employer YTD carry-forward): TP3 is for income earned earlier in the same year by a former employer; concurrent income is simultaneous
Employee declaration of secondary income can be captured via a TP1-style form, or a standalone Concurrent Income Declaration document
Reference: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/employers/responsibility-of-employer/
dependencies: Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration
Implement TP3 Carry-Forward Declaration for New Hires (Prior Employer YTD)

userStories.title: Track Employment Act S.60F Sick Leave Entitlement per Service Band on Payslip
userStories.description: Employment Act 1955 Section 60F mandates paid sick leave based on length of service: 14 days (under 2 years), 18 days (2â€“5 years), 22 days (over 5 years), plus 60 additional hospitalisation days annually. Sick leave is paid at the employee's ordinary rate of pay (ORP). Effective 1 January 2023, payslips must disclose leave entitlement and balance. The existing PRD tracks maternity/paternity leave but does not cover statutory sick leave entitlement computation, ORP-based sick pay validation, or the mandatory payslip disclosure requirement under the Employment Act.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record automatically computes sick leave entitlement from Date of Joining: 14 days (<2 years service), 18 days (2â€“5 years), 22 days (>5 years), plus 60 days hospitalisation entitlement
Payslip shows: Sick Leave Annual Entitlement, Days Taken YTD, Remaining Balance for both standard sick and hospitalisation categories
Sick leave taken within statutory entitlement is paid at ORP; sick leave days that exceed entitlement are automatically marked as unpaid with a flag on the Salary Slip
Service band entitlement upgrades automatically on the employee's service anniversary date (not calendar year-end)
System alerts HR Manager when an employee has exhausted sick leave entitlement and a new sick leave absence is recorded
Unit test: employee with 14 months service gets 14-day entitlement; employee with 27 months service gets 18-day entitlement after service anniversary upgrade
technicalNotes: Employment Act 1955 Section 60F â€” sick leave entitlement tiers based on continuous service period; resets annually on employment anniversary
Section 60F(1A) â€” additional 60 days hospitalisation leave per year (separate from standard sick leave)
Section 61 â€” employer must maintain a leave register for MOHR inspection; payslip must disclose leave balance (mandatory from 1 January 2023)
Sick leave pay must be at ORP per working day â€” same formula as other EA leave entitlements defined in the ORP calculator
Distinct from maternity (Section 37) and paternity (Section 37A) leave already in the PRD
dependencies: Implement Ordinary Rate of Pay (ORP) Calculator and Overtime Validation
Generate Employment Act S.61 Compliant Payslip with All Mandatory Fields
Track Maternity/Paternity Leave and Validate Maternity Pay Rate

userStories.title: Update TP1(1/2026) Relief Categories and PCB Computation Engine for YA2026 Budget Changes
userStories.description: LHDN published the TP1(1/2026) form and MTD Computation Specification 2026, effective January 2026. Key changes that affect PCB calculation: children's life/medical insurance now included under existing insurance relief limits; childcare relief extended to ages 6â€“12 for registered after-school programmes (previously only age â‰¤6); autism/learning disability child relief ceiling increased from RM6,000 to RM10,000; domestic tourism and cultural attraction RM1,000 relief added (YA2026â€“2027 only, time-bounded); vaccine relief expanded to all NPRA-approved vaccines. Tax brackets are unchanged for YA2026. All payroll from January 2026 must use the updated form.
acceptanceCriteria: TP1 data entry form includes a 'Children Life/Medical Insurance' sub-field under existing insurance relief (respecting combined RM3,000/RM4,000 ceilings)
Childcare relief field accepts children aged 6â€“12 enrolled in Ministry of Education-registered after-school care programmes
Autism/learning disability child relief ceiling updated from RM6,000 to RM10,000 in the PCB deduction engine
Domestic tourism and cultural attraction RM1,000 relief field is present, with an expiry guard preventing application after 31 December 2027
Vaccine relief field is no longer restricted to a hardcoded list â€” accepts any NPRA-approved vaccine
PCB calculation uses updated relief totals from new TP1(1/2026) fields for all payroll from January 2026 onwards
Old YA2025 TP1 records are preserved for historical audit without affecting 2026 PCB computation
technicalNotes: Official TP1(1/2026) form PDF: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/gjuhcbc2/bi-tp1-form-2026.pdf
Official MTD Computation Specification 2026: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/arvlrzh5/spesifikasi-kaedah-pengiraan-berkomputer-pcb-2026.pdf
YA2026 individual income tax brackets are UNCHANGED from YA2025 â€” only relief items and the TP1 form structure change
Tourism relief is time-bounded (YA2026â€“2027 only): store effective_from = 2026-01-01 and expires_on = 2027-12-31 as system constants
dependencies: Update PCB TP1 Relief Tables for YA2025 (New Housing Loan Interest Relief and Increased Disability Amounts)

userStories.title: Extend Minimum Wage Enforcement to Apprenticeship Contract Workers (NWCC Amendment 2025)
userStories.description: The National Wages Consultative Council (Amendment) Act 2025, effective 1 August 2025, extended the RM1,700 minimum wage to workers on apprenticeship contracts (6â€“24 month systematic trade training programmes). Previously, apprentices could legally be paid below minimum wage. All payroll validation must now apply the RM1,700 floor to apprenticeship contract employee types, with penalties up to RM10,000 per affected worker for non-compliance.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee type 'Apprentice' or 'Contract Trainee' triggers minimum wage validation at RM1,700/month on Salary Slip save
A pre-payroll warning is raised when any apprenticeship salary is below RM1,700 before the payroll run is confirmed
Domestic workers remain the sole exempt category â€” apprentices are NOT exempt from minimum wage
Enforcement effective date is gated to 1 August 2025 â€” no false positives for historical payroll runs before this date
technicalNotes: Add 'Apprentice' as a recognised contract type in Employee master with a minimum_wage_applies = 1 flag
Reuse existing minimum wage validation service; extend employee_type scope to include Apprentice
NWCC Amendment Act 2025 passed concurrently with the micro-employer universal extension (same gazette date: 1 August 2025)
dependencies: Extend Minimum Wage Validation to Cover Micro-Employers from August 1, 2025

userStories.title: Update EPF i-Akaun Employer Contribution CSV Upload Format for Three-Account (75/15/10) Allocation
userStories.description: EPF restructured from two accounts to three accounts effective 11 May 2024: Akaun Persaraan (75%), Akaun Sejahtera (15%), Akaun Fleksibel (10%). The i-Akaun employer contribution upload file must now include three account allocation amount fields per employee row. Systems still submitting on the old two-account (70/30) basis produce incorrect allocation records in EPF member accounts. This affects all contribution months from May 2024 onwards and includes the October 2025 foreign worker mandatory contributions.
acceptanceCriteria: EPF CSV/TXT upload file generated by the system includes three separate account contribution amount columns per employee row
For standard Malaysian employees, allocation is: Akaun Persaraan = 75%, Akaun Sejahtera = 15%, Akaun Fleksibel = 10% of total contribution
For foreign workers (from October 2025 onwards), same 75/15/10 split is applied to their flat 2%+2% contributions
EPF remittance report in ERPNext shows per-employee breakdown across all three accounts per month
Validation rejects or warns when generated account allocation amounts do not sum to the total contribution amount
technicalNotes: EPF Account Restructuring announcement: https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/member/account-centre/account-restructuring
The old two-account format (70/30 split) is no longer valid for submissions after 11 May 2024
For employees aged 55+ the split ratio may differ â€” confirm against the current EPF i-Akaun Employer upload specification document
Existing payslip display story covers the UI (three-account split on payslip); this story covers the CSV upload file format sent to EPF
dependencies: Generate EPF i-Akaun Electronic Upload File
Display EPF Akaun Fleksibel Three-Account Contribution Split on Payslip

userStories.title: Enforce 30-Day EPF i-Akaun Registration Deadline Alert for Newly Eligible Foreign Workers (Oct 2025)
userStories.description: Effective October 2025, all foreign workers on valid work passes must contribute to EPF. EPF regulations require employers to register newly eligible foreign employees in i-Akaun Employer within 30 days of the mandatory commencement date (or within 30 days of hire for new foreign workers engaged from October 2025 onwards). Failure to register within the deadline exposes the employer to late registration penalties. The existing foreign worker EPF story covers contribution calculation; this story covers the registration deadline enforcement specifically.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing active foreign workers with hire dates before October 2025 trigger a one-time alert: 'EPF Registration Required by 14 November 2025'
New foreign worker hires from October 2025 onwards trigger a 30-day countdown alert from their Date of Joining for EPF i-Akaun registration
Employee master includes an 'EPF i-Akaun Registration Confirmed' flag with confirmation date for foreign worker records
When 30 days pass without the confirmation flag being set, a high-priority compliance alert is sent to the HR Manager role
Domestic servants (maids, cooks, cleaners, babysitters, drivers) are excluded from EPF registration alerts â€” no false positives
technicalNotes: EPF mandatory for non-Malaysian citizens: https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/employer/responsibilities/non-malaysian-citizen-employees
EPF announced automatic registration via the work-pass system but employers must still verify registration status in i-Akaun Employer portal
Valid work passes triggering mandatory EPF: Employment Pass, Work Permit, Professional Visit Pass, Residence Pass-Talent
Domestic worker exclusion is in the Third Schedule of the Employees Provident Fund Act 1991
dependencies: Implement Foreign Worker EPF Mandatory Contribution (Effective October 2025)

userStories.title: Build Platform SOCSO Deduction Engine Skeleton for Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872)
userStories.description: The Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872) was gazetted December 2025 with commencement date to be set by the Minister. It will require platform providers (ride-hailing, delivery, freelance platforms) to deduct Self-Employment Social Security Scheme contributions from gig worker earnings and remit to PERKESO monthly. Unlike standard employment SOCSO, this is a worker-funded scheme only with no employer share. The system must build the deduction engine now so it can be activated by effective date when commencement is gazetted.
acceptanceCriteria: A new worker category 'Gig / Platform Worker' is available in Employee master with a SEIA (Self-Employment) contribution flag
SOCSO Self-Employment Scheme contribution (worker-funded only, zero employer share) is correctly computed per the SEIA rate schedule
A monthly PERKESO remittance file for gig worker contributions is generated separately from standard employer SOCSO bulk upload
A commencement date guard in LHDN Settings prevents SEIA deductions from activating until the official gazette date is entered
Domestic gig workers (home cleaning, babysitting platforms) can be flagged as excluded per the SEIA exemption schedule
technicalNotes: Self-Employment Social Security Act 789 (SEIA) governs the contribution rate schedule; SOCSO Self-Employment Scheme rates apply
Platform providers remit on behalf of gig workers â€” different deduction logic from standard employer-employee payroll
Implement with a feature flag (effective_date field) in LHDN/PERKESO Settings so it activates on gazette
PERKESO ASSIST upload format for SEIA may differ from standard employer SOCSO bulk upload â€” verify before implementation
Gig Workers Act 2025 key features: https://www.rahmatlim.com/perspectives/articles/31531/mykh-key-features-of-s-gig-workers-bill-2025

userStories.title: Implement TalentCorp Career Comeback Tax Exemption PCB Reduction Workflow
userStories.description: The Career Comeback Tax Exemption programme (TalentCorp / Ministry of Finance, valid for employment contracts 2023â€“2028) grants full income tax exemption to women returning to full-time employment after a career break of at least 2 consecutive years between October 2017 and December 2026. Once the employer receives a Ministry of Finance approval letter for a qualifying employee, they must reduce that employee's PCB to RM0 for the approved period. Qualifying conditions: full-time contract of 24+ months, minimum gross salary RM5,000/month, employer must not be a related company to the employee.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee master has a 'Career Comeback Exemption' section with: approval letter reference, valid_from date, valid_until date, and MoF approval status
When the exemption is active, PCB calculation produces RM0 for that employee throughout the approval period
An alert is generated 30 days before the exemption approval expires so HR can manage renewal or PCB resumption
Exemption flag deactivates automatically after the valid_until date; PCB resumes at normal computed rates in the next payroll run
A validation guard prevents the exemption from being applied to employees with gross salary below RM5,000/month
PCB audit trail records the exemption reference for each month the zero-PCB is applied
technicalNotes: TalentCorp Career Comeback application portal: https://www.talentcorp.com.my/careercomebacktax/
MoF approval letter contains: employee name, NRIC, approval period, and conditions â€” store these on the Employee DocType
Exemption is prospective from the approval date â€” no retroactive application is permitted
Programme covers employment contracts signed between 1 January 2023 and 31 December 2028
Store MoF reference number in the PCB Change Audit Trail DocType for LHDN inspection readiness
dependencies: Implement PCB Change Audit Trail DocType

userStories.title: Implement BIK Motor Vehicle 50% Prescribed Value Discount for Company Cars Over 5 Years Old
userStories.description: LHDN Public Ruling 11/2019 specifies that under the Prescribed Value Method, the annual taxable BIK value of a company motor car â€” including the associated fuel benefit â€” is reduced by 50% when the vehicle is more than 5 years old from its original purchase date. Employers frequently misapply the full prescribed value to aged vehicles, over-reporting employee taxable income on EA Forms and PCB calculations. The fuel benefit (RM1,800/year) is halved for qualifying vehicles; the driver benefit (RM7,200/year) is assessed separately and is not discounted.
acceptanceCriteria: Company car record in the BIK module includes a 'Date of Original Registration/Purchase' field for vehicle age computation
BIK prescribed value calculation automatically applies a 50% discount when vehicle age exceeds 5 years at the payroll period date
The fuel benefit prescribed value (RM1,800/year) is also halved for vehicles meeting the >5 year threshold
The driver benefit (RM7,200/year) does NOT receive the 50% discount â€” it is computed separately at full prescribed rate
EA Form BIK section reflects the age-discounted taxable value for all qualifying aged vehicles
technicalNotes: LHDN Public Ruling 11/2019, paragraph 10: for a motor car older than 5 years, the employer shall use 50% of the prescribed value
This rule applies to the Prescribed Value Method only; the Formula Method (cost/lifespan Ã— 80%) does not use this discount
Vehicle age is measured from the ORIGINAL registration date, not from when the current employer acquired or leased it
Public Ruling 11/2019 PDF: http://lampiran1.hasil.gov.my/pdf/pdfam/PR_11_2019.pdf
dependencies: Implement Benefits-in-Kind (BIK) Prescribed Value Calculation Module
BIK Motor Vehicle Engine Capacity Prescribed Value Table Lookup for EA Form

userStories.title: Implement e-PCBN PCB Non-Deduction Certificate Processing for Self-Assessing Employees
userStories.description: LHDN may issue an e-PCBN (Perakuan Tidak Potongan PCB â€” PCB Non-Deduction Certificate) to an employee who elects to settle income tax via self-assessment (Form B/BE) rather than through monthly employer PCB deduction. Upon receiving an e-PCBN, the employer must immediately cease PCB deduction for that employee for the specified validity period. At certificate expiry, PCB deduction must automatically resume. Continuing to deduct PCB after receiving an e-PCBN constitutes over-withholding and creates a liability to refund the employee.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee master has an 'e-PCBN Certificate' section with: LHDN reference number, issue date, valid_from, and valid_until
PCB calculation produces RM0 for employees with an active e-PCBN during the validity window
An alert is generated 30 days before e-PCBN expiry to prompt HR to confirm whether to resume PCB or note a renewal
After e-PCBN valid_until date passes, PCB automatically resumes at normal computed rates in the next payroll run
Payslip displays 'PCB Exempted â€” e-PCBN Ref: [reference]' in place of the PCB deduction line to make the reason explicit
technicalNotes: e-PCBN is issued by LHDN to the employee upon the employee's application â€” the employer must act on the certificate promptly
Certificate specifies the exemption period (typically one calendar year or the remaining portion of the year)
Store e-PCBN reference number in PCB Change Audit Trail DocType for LHDN inspection readiness
PCB Computation Specification 2025 references the e-PCBN mechanism: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/mdahzjwi/spesifikasi-kaedah-pengiraan-berkomputer-pcb-2025.pdf
LHDN employer responsibilities: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/employers/responsibility-of-employer/
dependencies: Implement PCB Change Audit Trail DocType

userStories.title: Store e-CP22 and e-SPC LHDN Acknowledgement Reference Numbers on Employee Record
userStories.description: When CP22 is submitted via MyTax e-CP22 or CP21/CP22A via e-SPC, LHDN returns an acknowledgement containing a submission reference number and processing status. Employers must retain these reference numbers as proof of timely statutory filing. The existing stories for CP22 and CP21/CP22A submission cover triggering the submission, but do not capture and persist the LHDN acknowledgement response â€” leaving no audit evidence if LHDN later questions whether the notification was sent on time.
acceptanceCriteria: After e-CP22 submission for a new hire, system stores: LHDN reference number, submission timestamp, and status (Received/Processing/Accepted/Rejected) on the Employee record
After e-SPC submission for cessation (CP21/CP22A), the same acknowledgement fields are stored on the Employee termination record
Submission status and reference number are visible in a 'LHDN Statutory Filings' tab on the Employee DocType
Rejected submissions trigger a high-priority task assigned to the HR Manager role with LHDN rejection reason and the deadline for resubmission
All e-CP22 and e-SPC submissions appear in the Statutory Compliance Calendar audit view
technicalNotes: If API-integrated: store raw LHDN JSON response body and HTTP status code per submission attempt
If manual portal submission: add an 'Upload Acknowledgement PDF' attachment field as fallback evidence
LHDN e-CP22 portal: https://mytax.hasil.gov.my
LHDN e-SPC cessation portal: https://mytax.hasil.gov.my
LHDN typically takes up to 14 working days to change status from 'Received' to 'Accepted'
dependencies: Auto-Submit CP22 via e-CP22 MyTax Within 30 Days of New Employee Hire
Auto-Submit CP21/CP22A/CP22B via e-SPC MyTax at Cessation or Departure

userStories.title: Implement Year-End Payslip BIK-to-EA Form Reconciliation Check Before EA Form Generation
userStories.description: The annual EA Form must declare the total monetary value of Benefits-in-Kind received by each employee, which should equal the sum of monthly BIK amounts computed on payslips throughout the year. Discrepancies arise when mid-year vehicle changes occur, the valuation method switches between prescribed and formula, or manual overrides are applied on individual months. A pre-EA Form generation reconciliation check is needed to catch and resolve mismatches before EA Forms are distributed or filed.
acceptanceCriteria: Year-end BIK reconciliation report shows per employee: sum of monthly payslip BIK amounts vs EA Form BIK computation, with a variance column
Discrepancies above RM0.01 are flagged with the specific month of divergence and the likely cause (e.g., method switch, vehicle change, manual override)
Report can be run at any point during the year â€” not only at year-end â€” for ongoing BIK accuracy monitoring
EA Form generation is blocked (or displays a prominent warning) for any employee with an unresolved BIK reconciliation discrepancy
HR can mark a discrepancy as 'Reviewed and Accepted' with a mandatory reason note, which removes the EA Form generation block for that employee
technicalNotes: BIK monthly amounts are stored as a custom salary component on Salary Slip
Sources of discrepancy: prescribed vs formula method switch mid-year, vehicle replacement, retrospective Salary Slip corrections, or manual amount overrides
LHDN Public Ruling 11/2019 governs BIK valuation methodology: http://lampiran1.hasil.gov.my/pdf/pdfam/PR_11_2019.pdf
This check is an extension of the existing BIK calculation module and EA Form generation feature
dependencies: Implement Benefits-in-Kind (BIK) Prescribed Value Calculation Module
Rebuild EA Form to Include All Mandatory LHDN Section A/B/C/D Fields

userStories.title: Alert on Stale TP1 Relief Declarations When New Assessment Year Begins Without Employee Re-Submission
userStories.description: LHDN requires employees to submit a TP1 form for each assessment year. Relief declarations from a prior year are not automatically carried forward. If an employee has not submitted a new TP1 by 31 January of the new year, the employer should default to computing PCB using only the statutory basic personal relief (RM9,000) and no additional claims. Many employers inadvertently continue applying the prior year's reliefs, creating technically non-compliant PCB calculations that may under-withhold tax.
acceptanceCriteria: TP1 declaration record has an Assessment Year field and is year-scoped â€” a 2025 TP1 cannot be used for 2026 computation
On 1 January each year, the system flags all employees whose active TP1 is from the prior year in a 'Stale TP1 Declarations' report
Payroll run shows a per-employee warning for each stale TP1 before the payroll batch is confirmed
HR can choose: (a) acknowledge and apply prior-year TP1 with an audit warning logged, or (b) default to basic RM9,000 relief only for the employee
Employees can be sent a system-generated reminder to re-submit their TP1 before January payroll confirmation
technicalNotes: Assessment year field on TP1 DocType must be indexed to enable this cross-year check
LHDN technically requires new-year TP1 from January, but tolerates brief carry-forward in practice until the CP39 for January is filed
LHDN employer responsibilities: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/employers/responsibility-of-employer/
dependencies: Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration

userStories.title: Classify Employer-Sponsored Elderly Care Benefit for BIK and Budget 2026 Tax Deduction Recording
userStories.description: Budget 2026 introduced a new employer tax deduction for companies providing elderly care services for employees' aged parents (e.g., day care centres, home care, rehabilitation). From the employee's perspective, this benefit may be a taxable perquisite (cash) under S.13(1)(a) or a BIK (in-kind payment to care provider) under S.13(1)(b). The payroll system must correctly classify the payment type and apply the appropriate income treatment to determine PCB inclusion and EA Form reporting.
acceptanceCriteria: Salary component type 'Employer Elderly Care Provision' is available with a classification radio: 'Cash to Employee (S.13(1)(a))' vs 'In-Kind Direct to Provider (S.13(1)(b) BIK)'
When classified as Cash to Employee, the amount is included in the PCB computation base as employment income
When classified as In-Kind, the benefit is assessed as BIK at actual cost or LHDN prescribed value and disclosed on EA Form under the BIK section
EA Form correctly reports elderly care BIK value under the appropriate section (B or C) without double-counting as cash income
System displays a note on the component configuration: 'Confirm treatment with LHDN private ruling if benefit structure is novel â€” Finance Act 2026 exact provisions to be verified'
technicalNotes: Budget 2026 employer deduction for elderly care provision â€” confirm exact gazette/Finance Act 2026 wording before implementing as a hard exemption
KPMG Budget 2026 summary: https://kpmg.com/xx/en/our-insights/gms-flash-alert/flash-alert-2025-207.html
Cash vs in-kind distinction follows the general S.13(1)(a)/S.13(1)(b) income classification principle under the Income Tax Act 1967
Store component amount and classification in PCB audit trail for annual EA Form cross-check
dependencies: Implement Benefits-in-Kind (BIK) Prescribed Value Calculation Module

userStories.title: Track Employment Contract Stamp Duty Compliance via e-Duti Setem MyTax (Mandatory from 2026)
userStories.description: From 1 January 2026, the Stamp Duty Self-Assessment System (SAS) mandates that all employment contracts be stamped within 30 days of signing via the new e-Duti Setem portal on MyTax. The old STAMPS portal closed on 31 December 2025. Employment contracts are subject to a fixed RM10 stamp duty under Item 4, First Schedule of the Stamp Act 1949. The Finance Bill 2025 increased the exemption threshold to RM3,000/month (contracts for employees earning RM3,000/month or less are stamp duty exempt from 1 January 2026). LHDN will conduct widespread audits covering instruments from 2022â€“2024. Payroll systems must flag newly-signed contracts requiring stamping and track stamping status to ensure audit readiness.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee master has an 'Employment Contract' section with: contract signing date, gross monthly salary at time of signing, stamp duty exemption flag (auto-set to true if salary â‰¤ RM3,000), e-Duti Setem stamp reference number, and stamping date
When a new employment contract is recorded with gross salary > RM3,000/month and no stamping reference is set within 30 days of contract date, a compliance alert is sent to HR Manager
A 'Contracts Pending Stamping' report lists all employees with unsigned/unstamped contracts within the 30-day window
Contracts dated 1 January 2025â€“31 December 2025 that were stamped by 31 December 2025 are correctly flagged as compliant with no penalty
Contracts for employees earning â‰¤ RM3,000/month are auto-marked as exempt and excluded from stamp duty tracking
technicalNotes: Stamp Duty SAS effective date: 1 January 2026 â€” e-Duti Setem via MyTax portal (mytax.hasil.gov.my)
Fixed stamp duty: RM10 per contract under Item 4, First Schedule of the Stamp Act 1949
Late stamping penalties: 31â€“90 days = RM50 or 10% (whichever is higher); >90 days = RM100 or 20% (whichever is higher)
2026 grace year: LHDN will not impose penalties for late stamping on applications submitted 1 Januaryâ€“31 December 2026
Exemption threshold raised from RM300 to RM3,000/month effective 1 January 2026 by Finance Bill 2025
Crowe Malaysia guide: https://www.crowe.com/my/insights/redefining-compliance---stamp-duty-under-malaysias-self-assessment-regime

userStories.title: Mid-Year Tax Residency Status Change Workflow â€” Switch Between Resident and Non-Resident PCB Rates
userStories.description: An employee who becomes non-resident mid-year (leaves Malaysia, drops below 182 days) or conversely achieves residency mid-year triggers a PCB rate switch. Non-residents pay a flat 30% on all employment income with no personal reliefs. Residents use the progressive rate scale with reliefs. When an employee's residency status changes, all prior-month PCB amounts for the current year must be reviewed and the remaining months must use the new rate. LHDN requires the employer to adjust withholding prospectively from the month of status change. If the employee becomes non-resident, the employer must also withdraw all TP1 relief applications for that year.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee master has a 'Tax Residency Status' field (Resident / Non-Resident / Pending Determination) with effective date
When status is changed to Non-Resident with an effective date, PCB for all subsequent months uses the 30% flat rate; all TP1 reliefs are suspended for that year
A residency change event is logged in the PCB audit trail with the effective date, previous status, new status, and the HR user who made the change

When Non-Resident status is set, the system generates a warning: 'Prior-month PCB for this year should be reviewed â€” non-resident rate is not retroactively applied by the employer; advise employee to seek LHDN guidance for year-end reconciliation'
Re-setting status back to Resident re-enables TP1 reliefs from the effective date with a warning noting that LHDN approval may be required for the mid-year switch back
technicalNotes: LHDN Tax Treatment for Residents & Non-Residents: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/if4noy3t/tax-treatment-residents-non-residents.pdf
Residency determination is based on physical presence in Malaysia: 182 days rule under S.7 of the Income Tax Act 1967
Special rules exist for day-counting across calendar year boundaries (S.7(1)(b) and S.7(1)(c))
Flat 30% non-resident rate applies to ALL employment income including bonus and BIK â€” no exemptions for personal relief
PCB Computation Specification 2025: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/mdahzjwi/spesifikasi-kaedah-pengiraan-berkomputer-pcb-2025.pdf
dependencies: Implement PCB Change Audit Trail DocType
Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration

userStories.title: Add Life/Medical Insurance Relief Extension to Children on TP1 Form for YA2026
userStories.description: Budget 2026 expanded two existing income tax reliefs to cover children in addition to self and spouse: (1) Life insurance and takaful premium relief (previously RM3,000 for self and spouse) now includes premiums on policies contracted for the lives of children; and (2) Education and medical insurance premium relief (previously RM4,000 for self and spouse) now also covers children. Both reliefs retain their existing aggregate ceilings. Since these are TP1-declarable reliefs that reduce monthly PCB, the TP1(1/2026) form and the PCB computation engine must be updated to capture the child insurance premium sub-category before January 2026 payroll.
acceptanceCriteria: TP1 entry form has a sub-field under Life Insurance/Takaful Relief for 'Child Life/Takaful Premium' with an aggregate ceiling check against the combined RM3,000 limit for self + spouse + children
TP1 entry form has a sub-field under Education & Medical Insurance Relief for 'Child Education/Medical Insurance Premium' with an aggregate ceiling check against the combined RM4,000 limit
PCB computation correctly reduces chargeable income by the declared child insurance premiums up to the respective ceilings
Validation prevents the combined (self + spouse + child) insurance relief from exceeding the statutory cap even if individual sub-entries are below the cap
Historical YA2025 and earlier TP1 records do not show child insurance fields â€” the sub-fields are gated by assessment year
technicalNotes: Budget 2026 tax measures appendix (official): https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2026/ucapan/tax-measures.pdf
Life insurance/takaful relief: RM3,000 aggregate for self + spouse + children (effective YA2026)
Education and medical insurance relief: RM4,000 aggregate for self + spouse + children (effective YA2026)

This story is a sub-task of 'Update TP1(1/2026) Relief Categories and PCB Computation Engine for YA2026 Budget Changes' but warranted as a separate story due to the relief mechanics requiring aggregate ceiling logic across multiple sub-categories
LHDN official PCB computation spec 2026: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/arvlrzh5/spesifikasi-kaedah-pengiraan-berkomputer-pcb-2026.pdf
dependencies: Update TP1(1/2026) Relief Categories and PCB Computation Engine for YA2026 Budget Changes
Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration

userStories.title: Implement Gig Worker i-Saraan Plus EPF Contribution Deduction for Platform Providers
userStories.description: EPF launched i-Saraan Plus effective 1 January 2026 specifically for e-hailing and p-hailing drivers. Platform providers (e.g., Grab, foodpanda, Lalamove) must facilitate EPF membership registration and voluntary contribution deductions for their gig workers at rates chosen by the workers. The government provides a 20% matching incentive up to RM600/year (lifetime cap RM6,000). Platform providers acting as payroll intermediaries must deduct and remit contributions monthly and ensure EPF registration is completed for each driver. ERPNext implementations for ride-hailing or delivery platform operators require a dedicated i-Saraan Plus deduction engine separate from standard employer EPF contributions.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker category 'Platform Gig Worker (i-Saraan Plus)' is available in the system with EPF membership registration fields
Workers can specify their monthly i-Saraan Plus contribution amount (flexible, driver-chosen rate)
Monthly contribution deduction is computed, reflected on the earnings statement, and included in EPF remittance
EPF upload file includes i-Saraan Plus contributions coded under the correct EPF scheme identifier (separate from mandatory employer contribution)
A YTD tracker shows each worker's total i-Saraan Plus contribution for the calendar year against the RM3,000 threshold for maximum RM600 government incentive
technicalNotes: i-Saraan Plus is for e-hailing and p-hailing drivers only â€” separate from the regular i-Saraan (general self-employed) scheme
Government matching: 20% of total annual voluntary contribution, max RM600/year, lifetime cap RM6,000 â€” EPF handles the government top-up, not the employer
EPF i-Saraan Plus official page: https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/member/savings/i-saraan-plus
Intersects with Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872) platform provider obligations â€” coordinate with the SOCSO deduction engine story
Malay Mail Budget 2026 announcement: https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2025/10/10/budget-2026-gig-workers-to-get-up-to-rm600-a-year-in-epf-matching-under-new-i-saraan-plus-scheme/194145
dependencies: Build Platform SOCSO Deduction Engine Skeleton for Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872)

userStories.title: Section 107D ITA â€” WHT 2% Deduction, Monthly Remittance, and CP58 Integration for Agent/Dealer Payments
userStories.description: Section 107D of the Income Tax Act 1967 requires payers to deduct 2% withholding tax (WHT) at source from monetary and non-monetary incentive payments to agents, dealers, or distributors exceeding RM5,000 per year. The WHT must be remitted to LHDN by the 15th of the following month and the deducted amount reported on the annual CP58 statement under Section 83A. This is a separate compliance workflow from the existing CP58 statement generation story, requiring payment tracking and remittance scheduling.
acceptanceCriteria: System identifies payments to recipients classified as Agent/Dealer/Distributor and accumulates annual total per recipient
When cumulative payments to a recipient exceed RM5,000 in a calendar year, 2% WHT is computed on each subsequent payment
Monthly WHT remittance schedule is generated per payer company showing total WHT payable and recipient breakdown
WHT amounts are linked to corresponding CP58 records for annual reporting
LHDN payment reference (after WHT remittance) is stored on each remittance record
System tracks non-monetary incentive values (e.g., vehicles, property) at cost incurred by payer for WHT base calculation
technicalNotes: Section 107D ITA 1967 and Section 83A ITA 1967 (CP58 reporting obligation)
WHT rate: 2% of monetary/non-monetary incentive value; threshold: RM5,000/year per recipient
Remittance deadline: 15th of the following month; late payment attracts 10% penalty
CP58 must be issued to the agent/dealer by 31 March of the following year
Different from employment income PCB (Schedule A); targets commission-based non-employment income
dependencies: Implement CP58 Agent/Dealer Non-Employment Income Statement

userStories.title: Gig Workers Act 2025 â€” Mandatory Service Agreement Compliance Tracker for Platform Providers
userStories.description: The Gig Workers Act 2025 (passed by Parliament, 2025) requires platform providers (contracting entities) to sign written service agreements with each gig worker containing 7 specific mandatory terms: parties to the agreement, period, services provided, obligations, rate and payment method of earnings, and entitled benefits. Payment processing must be linked to a validated service agreement record, and agreements must be maintained for MOHR inspection.
acceptanceCriteria: Gig Worker Service Agreement DocType captures all 7 mandatory terms specified in the Act
Payment processing for a gig worker is blocked until a valid signed service agreement is linked to their record
System flags service agreements that are expired (past end date) and blocks new payment cycles
Compliance report lists all active gig workers with agreement status (valid/expired/missing)
Service agreement records are retained for minimum 7 years for MOHR inspection
Alert is raised 30 days before any service agreement expiry to prompt renewal
technicalNotes: Gig Workers Act 2025 â€” passed by Malaysian Parliament; exact gazetted date to be confirmed
Contracting entities must also establish internal grievance mechanisms (30-day complaint window for workers)
Platform deactivation of gig workers requires adequate notice and hearing; max 14-day suspension before inquiry
Service agreement requirement applies to all platform providers using gig workers regardless of worker count
dependencies: Build Platform SOCSO Deduction Engine Skeleton for Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872)

userStories.title: Gig Workers Act 2025 â€” Auto-Register Gig Workers Under PERKESO SKSPS via ASSIST Portal API
userStories.description: Under the Gig Workers Act 2025, platform providers must ensure mandatory registration of each gig worker under PERKESO's Self-Employment Social Security Scheme (SKSPS) before any SKSPS contributions can be deducted. This registration must be completed via the PERKESO ASSIST portal before the first SKSPS deduction is processed. This is a prerequisite step distinct from the contribution calculation engine.
acceptanceCriteria: New Gig Worker onboarding triggers PERKESO ASSIST SKSPS registration API call automatically
Registration status (pending/approved/rejected) is stored on the Gig Worker record
SKSPS contribution deduction is blocked until registration status is confirmed as Active
PERKESO SKSPS registration reference number is stored on the Gig Worker profile
Failed registrations generate an HR task with specific PERKESO error code and resolution steps
Bulk registration batch job is available for onboarding multiple gig workers simultaneously
technicalNotes: PERKESO ASSIST portal: assist.perkeso.gov.my
Self-Employment Social Security Act 2017 (Act 789) and Gig Workers Act 2025
SKSPS covers employment injury and invalidity protection for gig/self-employed workers
PERKESO ASSIST API endpoint for SKSPS registration to be confirmed from PERKESO developer documentation
dependencies: Build Platform SOCSO Deduction Engine Skeleton for Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872)
Gig Workers Act 2025 â€” Mandatory Service Agreement Compliance Tracker for Platform Providers

userStories.title: Gig Workers Act 2025 â€” SKSPS Per-Transaction Contribution Calculation and PERKESO Monthly Aggregate Remittance
userStories.description: Under the Gig Workers Act 2025, platform providers must deduct SKSPS contributions from each completed gig transaction (ride, delivery, freelance job) and remit the monthly aggregate to PERKESO. Unlike standard SOCSO (monthly salary-based), SKSPS is computed per-transaction at 1.25%, requiring a distinct computation engine that accumulates transaction-level deductions and generates a monthly PERKESO remittance file.
acceptanceCriteria: SKSPS deduction at 1.25% is computed per completed gig transaction value and attributed to the gig worker
Failed, cancelled, or refunded transactions are excluded from the SKSPS contribution base
Monthly aggregate SKSPS amount per gig worker is computed and held for PERKESO remittance
PERKESO monthly remittance file is generated with worker-level breakdown (NRIC/passport, SKSPS number, amount)
Transaction-level SKSPS deductions are reflected in the gig worker payslip with total SKSPS deducted
Monthly remittance is due by the 15th of the following month consistent with SOCSO payment cycle
technicalNotes: SKSPS contribution rate: 1.25% per transaction; full amount borne by platform provider
Contracting entity is responsible for both computing and remitting SKSPS (not split with worker)
Remittance to PERKESO via PERKESO ASSIST portal; file format to be confirmed from PERKESO documentation
Self-Employment Social Security Act 2017 (Act 789) and Gig Workers Act 2025
dependencies: Gig Workers Act 2025 â€” Auto-Register Gig Workers Under PERKESO SKSPS via ASSIST Portal API

userStories.title: Gig Workers Act 2025 â€” 7-Day Payment Deadline Alert for Informal Gig Engagements Without Payment Schedule
userStories.description: The Gig Workers Act 2025 requires contracting entities to remit payment to gig workers within 7 days of service completion when no formal agreement specifies a payment schedule. This is distinct from Employment Act S.19 7-day rule (which applies to regular employees under contracts of service). Payroll must track per-transaction payment deadlines and alert platform operators before violations occur.
acceptanceCriteria: Gig transactions where no payment schedule is specified in the service agreement are flagged for 7-day payment deadline tracking
Alert is raised to the platform operator 1 day before the 7-day payment deadline lapses per transaction
Overdue gig payments (past 7 days without remittance confirmation) appear in a compliance violation log
Dashboard shows pending gig worker payments grouped by days remaining until 7-day deadline
Payment completion records the actual remittance date against the service completion date
Compliance report exportable for MOHR inspection showing all gig payment timelines and any overdue records
technicalNotes: Gig Workers Act 2025 â€” payment obligation clause for informal/no-agreement payment schedule engagements
Distinct from Employment Act S.19 7-day rule (which covers employees with contracts of service)
7-day clock starts from the date of service completion or delivery confirmation by platform
Failure to pay within 7 days constitutes a criminal offence under the Gig Workers Act 2025
dependencies: Add Wage Payment Deadline Compliance Alerts (Employment Act S.19 7-Day Rule)
Gig Workers Act 2025 â€” Mandatory Service Agreement Compliance Tracker for Platform Providers

userStories.title: Enforce Part-Time Employee Minimum Hourly Rate Against RM1,700 Monthly Wage Floor Proration
userStories.description: The Minimum Wages Order sets RM1,700/month as the floor for all employees including part-time workers. The equivalent minimum hourly rate must be prorated: RM1,700 Ã· 26 working days Ã· 8 hours = RM8.17/hour. The payroll system currently calculates part-time ORP (earned wages based on hours worked) but does not validate that the agreed hourly rate meets the statutory minimum floor. This is a compliance gate preventing underpayment penalties from MOHR.
acceptanceCriteria: Part-time employees' configured hourly rate is validated against the computed minimum hourly rate (RM1,700 Ã· 208 hours = RM8.17/hour) at payroll processing
Alert is raised for any part-time employee whose hourly rate falls below the minimum hourly rate
Payroll processing is blocked for non-compliant part-time employees unless an exemption reason is recorded
Exemptions (e.g., apprentices, disabled worker schemes, trainees) can be flagged on the Employee record to suppress the block
Minimum hourly rate is derived from the system-configured monthly minimum wage constant so it auto-adjusts when minimum wage changes
Compliance report lists all part-time employees with their actual hourly rate vs the minimum floor with pass/fail status
technicalNotes: Minimum Wages Order 2022 (P.U.(A) 268/2022): RM1,700/month effective 1 February 2023
Part-time proration formula: RM1,700 Ã· 26 working days Ã· 8 hours = RM8.17/hour minimum
Distinct from ORP proration story (which computes earned wages for part-timers based on hours worked, not validates minimum)
Micro-employer extension from August 2025: applies to ALL employers including those with fewer than 5 employees
NWCC Act exemptions: apprentices under the National Apprenticeship Act, disabled workers under specific MOHR schemes
dependencies: Implement Part-Time Employee ORP Proration and Overtime Eligibility under EA Third Schedule
Update Minimum Wage Validation to RM1,700 (Feb 2025 Amendment)

userStories.title: Employment Act S.60A â€” Enforce 104-Hour Monthly Overtime Cap with Pre-Payroll Violation Alert
userStories.description: Section 60A(4) of the Employment Act 1955 (post-2022 amendment) limits overtime to a maximum of 104 hours per month for covered employees. The payroll system currently calculates OT pay amounts but does not validate whether total OT hours in a month exceed the statutory cap. Exceeding this cap without Director General approval is an offence; a pre-payroll cap check is required.
acceptanceCriteria: Overtime hours entered per employee per month are accumulated and checked against the 104-hour statutory cap
Early warning alert is raised when any covered employee's monthly OT reaches 90 hours (threshold configurable)
Hard cap alert is raised when any covered employee's monthly OT exceeds 104 hours
Payroll finalisation is blocked (or requires documented manual override with justification) when cap is exceeded
Compliance report lists employees by OT hours for the month with cap utilisation percentage
System applies cap check only to employees covered under the Act (monthly wage â‰¤RM4,000 or manual labourers); managerial staff are excluded
technicalNotes: Employment Act 1955 Section 60A(4): maximum 104 OT hours per month
Covered employees: those earning â‰¤RM4,000/month, manual labourers regardless of wages, vehicle operators, supervisors of manual labourers
Does NOT apply to managerial/executive employees earning above the EA coverage threshold
Post-2022 amendment also reduced normal working week from 48 to 45 hours
Director General of Labour approval required to lawfully exceed 104-hour cap
dependencies: Implement Ordinary Rate of Pay (ORP) Calculator and Overtime Validation

userStories.title: e-PCB Plus Successful Submission â€” Store Acknowledgement Reference Number on Salary Slip for LHDN Audit Trail
userStories.description: After a successful monthly PCB data submission via e-PCB Plus on MyTax, LHDN issues a submission acknowledgement reference number. This reference must be stored on the corresponding Salary Slip records to create an LHDN-traceable audit trail. This is distinct from the existing rejection tracking story (which records failures) â€” this story captures successful submission evidence needed during LHDN audits or PCB reconciliation queries.
acceptanceCriteria: Successful e-PCB Plus submission response stores the acknowledgement/receipt reference number on the submission batch record
Acknowledgement reference is propagated to each Salary Slip included in the submitted batch
Salary Slip form displays e-PCB Plus submission reference as a read-only field in the LHDN/Statutory tab
PCB audit export report includes the e-PCB Plus acknowledgement reference per employee per month
If a month is resubmitted as an amendment, both the original and amendment acknowledgement references are retained (not overwritten)
Salary Slips can be searched by e-PCB Plus acknowledgement reference number for cross-referencing during LHDN queries
technicalNotes: e-PCB Plus accessible via MyTax portal (mytax.hasil.gov.my), operational from 3 February 2025
Distinct from MyInvois acknowledgement numbers (e-invoices) and from EIS/EPF submission acknowledgements
Distinct from existing 'e-PCB Plus Post-Submission Rejection Record Tracking' story (this covers successes, that covers rejections)
Submission acknowledgement format to be confirmed from e-PCB Plus API/portal response documentation
dependencies: Implement e-CP39 API Submission to LHDN MyTax / e-PCB Plus
e-PCB Plus Post-Submission Rejection Record Tracking and Targeted Resubmission Workflow

userStories.title: Budget 2026 â€” Track Senior Citizen Employee Hiring Tax Deduction Eligibility Extended to YA2030
userStories.description: Malaysia Budget 2026 extended the employer additional income tax deduction for hiring senior citizens (aged 60 and above) for a further 5 years to YA2030. To claim this corporate tax deduction, employers must track which employees qualify, their period of employment, and wages paid. The payroll system must identify eligible senior citizen employees and generate an annual summary for corporate tax return attachment.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee records where the employee is aged 60 or above are automatically flagged as 'Senior Citizen' in payroll (based on date of birth, not manual entry)
Annual payroll report generates a summary of senior citizen employees with total wages paid per year of assessment
Report covers YA2026 to YA2030 as the eligible years for the extended additional tax deduction
Senior citizen employee count and total wages are breakdow by department and entity for corporate tax attachment
System alerts when a senior citizen employee's contract end date approaches (to flag potential deduction eligibility lapse)
Historical flag captures which months/years each senior citizen was employed for the relevant YA
technicalNotes: This is an employer-side corporate income tax deduction (Form C/CE), not an employee PCB relief
Budget 2026 (announced October 2025): additional deduction extended to YA2030 (previously expiring earlier)
Senior citizen threshold: employee aged 60 and above at the time of employment
Double deduction typically applies on wages paid to eligible senior citizen employees â€” confirm rate from IRB public ruling
Claim documented via employer's annual corporate income tax return, not via PCB/CP39 monthly submission

userStories.title: Apply LHDN PCB 2025 Specification Amendment â€” Revised TP1 Deduction Items and OKU Relief Update
userStories.description: LHDN published the official 2025 PCB Computerised Calculation Method Specification amendment (Spesifikasi Kaedah Pengiraan Berkomputer PCB 2025). The amendment includes updates to TP1 deduction items, an increase in disability (OKU) tax relief amounts, expanded scope of medical treatment relief, and a new food waste composting machine relief (RM2,500 permanent cap). These changes must be verified against the current PCB calculation engine to ensure the YA2025 computation is accurate before any Year-End EA Form generation or Borang E submission.
acceptanceCriteria: PCB computation for YA2025 includes updated OKU disability relief amounts as per the 2025 spec amendment
Medical treatment relief scope is updated to reflect the 2025 expansion (confirm exact items from spec document)
Food waste composting machine relief (RM2,500, permanent limit) is added as a TP1 line item for YA2025
EV charging facility relief scope expansion is applied as per 2025 spec amendment
Unit test: employee with OKU status and composting machine relief â€” PCB reflects both correctly for YA2025
PCB calculation engine version is tagged as '2025 Spec Compliant' in system settings for audit reference
technicalNotes: Official document: Spesifikasi Kaedah Pengiraan Berkomputer PCB 2025 (hasil.gov.my/media/mdahzjwi/spesifikasi-kaedah-pengiraan-berkomputer-pcb-2025.pdf)
Amendment is effective for YA2025 computations; MTD formula itself is unchanged
Changes include: TP1(2024)/TP3(2024) form amendments for deduction items
Distinct from YA2026 Budget changes story â€” this covers the official 2025 technical spec, not Budget 2026 proposals
Priority: must be applied before EA Form 2025 generation season (EA form due March 1, 2026)
dependencies: Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration

userStories.title: Generate SOCSO Borang 2 First-Time Employee PERKESO Registration for ASSIST Portal
userStories.description: When an employer hires a new employee who has never been registered with PERKESO before, the employer must complete Borang 2 (Employee Registration Form) within 30 days of the hire date and submit it via the PERKESO ASSIST portal. The existing PRD covers Borang 3 (new employment notification for already-registered employees) and Borang 4 (termination), but not Borang 2 for first-time registrations. Failure to register within 30 days is an offence under the Employees' Social Security Act 1969.
acceptanceCriteria: New Employee record creation triggers a check: if employee has no existing SOCSO/PERKESO number, system flags for Borang 2 registration
Borang 2 PDF is generated with employee's IC/passport, full name, date of birth, address, and employer details pre-filled
30-day registration deadline alert is raised on Day 1, Day 20, and Day 28 if Borang 2 status remains unsubmitted
PERKESO registration number field on Employee record is populated after successful ASSIST portal submission
If employee already has a PERKESO number (prior employer), system routes to Borang 3 (new employment notification) instead
Compliance report lists employees without PERKESO numbers who have been employed for more than 30 days
technicalNotes: PERKESO ASSIST portal: assist.perkeso.gov.my â€” employer submits Borang 2 online
Employees' Social Security Act 1969: Section 7 requires employer registration of new employees within 30 days
Borang 2 is for first-time PERKESO registration; Borang 3 is for Notification of New Employment (employee already registered)
Foreign workers require a separate Foreign Worker Registration Form (not Borang 2) â€” distinguish by nationality
Domestic servants have separate registration form as per Social Security Act exemption amendments
dependencies: Implement SOCSO Borang 3 â€” New Employee Notification to PERKESO

userStories.title: Update Employment Contract Stamp Duty Exemption Threshold to RM3,000 Monthly Wage (Budget 2026)
userStories.description: Malaysia's Finance Bill 2025 (Budget 2026) raises the monthly wage exemption threshold for employment contract stamp duty from RM300 to RM3,000, effective 1 January 2026. Under the new self-assessment e-Duti Setem (e-DS) system launching on the same date, employers must self-assess, determine whether each new contract is exempt, and stamp dutiable contracts within 30 days of signing via MyTax. The existing stamp duty tracker must be updated to apply the new RM3,000 threshold for all contracts executed from 1 January 2026 onwards.
acceptanceCriteria: Employment contracts for employees with monthly gross salary â‰¤RM3,000 are classified as EXEMPT from stamp duty for contracts dated on/after 1 January 2026
Employment contracts for employees with monthly gross salary >RM3,000 are classified as DUTIABLE (RM10 stamp duty per copy) for contracts dated on/after 1 January 2026
Contracts dated before 1 January 2026 continue to use the old RM300 exemption threshold
Payroll onboarding workflow shows stamp duty status (Exempt/Dutiable) automatically based on salary and contract date
For dutiable contracts, system generates a 30-day deadline alert from the contract signing date for e-Duti Setem submission
Threshold constant is configurable in system settings with effective-from date so future changes require no code change
technicalNotes: Finance Bill 2025 / Budget 2026: stamp duty exemption threshold raised from RM300 to RM3,000 monthly wage â€” effective 1 January 2026
e-Duti Setem (e-DS) self-assessment system launches 15 December 2025, mandatory from 1 January 2026
Stamp duty amount: RM10 per copy of employment contract (Item 4, First Schedule, Stamp Act 1949)
30-day stamping deadline from signing date; late stamping attracts penalty from 1 January 2026 under self-assessment regime
Relevant to the existing 'LHDN STAMPS Employment Contract Digital Stamp Status Tracker' story â€” update threshold logic
dependencies: Track Employment Contract Stamp Duty Compliance via e-Duti Setem MyTax (Mandatory from 2026)

userStories.title: New Hire Statutory Compliance Checklist with Multi-Agency Deadline Tracker
userStories.description: When a new employee is hired, Malaysian law requires multiple statutory notifications and registrations across four agencies (LHDN, KWSP, PERKESO, and HRD Corp where applicable), each with different deadlines. Currently the system has individual features for each notification, but there is no unified onboarding compliance checklist that tracks all outstanding obligations in a single view with escalation alerts. Employers frequently miss one of the deadlines (e.g., SOCSO within 30 days, EPF within 7 days, CP22 within 30 days) because each is managed in isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: On new Employee record creation, a New Hire Compliance Checklist is auto-generated with line items: CP22 submission (30 days), EPF new employee registration (7 days), SOCSO Borang 2/3 (30 days), EIS Form IA (30 days), HRD Corp notification (if applicable)
Each checklist item shows deadline date, current status (pending/submitted/overdue), and a quick-link to the relevant submission tool
Overdue items are highlighted in red on the Employee record and appear in a dedicated Compliance Alerts dashboard
Checklist auto-clears each item when the corresponding submission reference is recorded in the system
Daily digest email/notification is sent to HR Manager listing all employees with overdue new-hire compliance items
Checklist export can be used as a MOHR/statutory audit evidence document showing on-time compliance
technicalNotes: CP22: notify LHDN of new employment within 30 days â€” via e-CP22 MyTax portal
EPF: register new employee with KWSP within 7 days of employment commencement
SOCSO: register new employee within 30 days â€” Borang 2 (first-time) or Borang 3 (existing PERKESO member)
EIS: register new employee within 30 days via PERKESO ASSIST portal (Form IA)
HRD Corp: notify within 30 days if employer has 10+ Malaysian employees (compulsory levy payer)
This is an orchestration layer on top of the existing individual submission stories â€” not replacing them
dependencies: Auto-Submit CP22 via e-CP22 MyTax Within 30 Days of New Employee Hire
Alert on EPF New Employee Registration 7-Day Statutory Deadline
Generate SOCSO Borang 2 for First-Time Employee PERKESO Registration via ASSIST Portal
Auto-Generate EIS Form IA New Employee Registration File for PERKESO ASSIST

userStories.title: Implement Returning Expert Programme (REP) 15% Flat PCB Rate on Employee Profile
userStories.description: TalentCorp's Returning Expert Programme (REP) grants approved returning Malaysians a flat 15% PCB rate on chargeable employment income for 5 consecutive years, as specified in their SATK (Surat Akuan Tarikh Kembali). Unlike TalentCorp's Career Comeback programme (for women), the REP applies to Malaysian professionals who have been based overseas and is approved via the MyHEART portal. Employers must apply the flat 15% rate instead of the graduated Schedule 1 tax table for the duration of the approval period.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record has REP status checkbox and SATK reference number field with approval start/end year
PCB calculation applies flat 15% on chargeable income (after EPF/TP1 deductions) when REP flag is active
System uses graduated Schedule 1 rates after approval period ends (auto-reversion based on approval end year)
EA Form correctly reflects the flat 15% PCB treatment with note of REP approval for employee
CP39 monthly submission uses REP employee's actual PCB amount (15% calculation) â€” not graduated
Alert raised to HR when REP approval period is within 3 months of expiry so renewal or reversion can be planned
technicalNotes: REP administered by TalentCorp Malaysia via MyHEART portal (myheart.my/rep/)
PCB rate: flat 15% on chargeable employment income for 5 consecutive approved years
SATK document specifies exact approval period; employer deducts at 15% from first employment month within approved window
Distinct from Career Comeback (for women returnees) â€” REP applies to any Malaysian returning from overseas employment
Employee files income tax via e-BT form each year; surplus PCB refunded by LHDN
Relevant ITA section: Section 25A incentive via Income Tax (Exemption) Order
dependencies: Implement MTD Method 2 (Year-to-Date Recalculation Formula)

userStories.title: Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag and Wage Escalation Compliance Report
userStories.description: Malaysia's Progressive Wage Policy (PWP), launched June 2024 and expanded in 2025 with a RM200M allocation, incentivises MSMEs to raise employee wages through structured escalation. Participating employers must maintain minimum 6% annual salary increases for employees earning RM1,500â€“RM4,999/month and meet training requirements. As PWP adoption grows (50,000+ workers targeted by 2027), ERP payroll must be able to flag enrolled employees, track wage progression, and generate audit reports for the Ministry of Human Resources.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record has PWP enrolment flag and scheme entry date field
Payroll annual review computes wage increase percentage from prior year base for each PWP employee
Alert raised if a PWP employee's annual wage increment falls below 6% threshold at year-end payroll cycle
PWP eligibility check: employee's monthly salary must be in RM1,500â€“RM4,999 range at time of enrolment
Annual PWP compliance report shows each enrolled employee's salary at enrolment, current salary, year-over-year percentage increase, and compliance status (met/failed)
Report is exportable in MOHR-compatible format for PWP application/renewal submissions
technicalNotes: PWP administered by Ministry of Human Resources (MOHR); application via jobsmalaysia.gov.my or MOHR portal
PWP is voluntary but employers receive incentives/matching funds for participation
Wage escalation requirement: minimum 6% increase per year for enrolled employees
Eligible employees: Malaysian citizens, monthly wage RM1,500â€“RM4,999, employed by MSME
Training compliance is also required (eTRIS or HRD Corp training records) â€” link to HRD Corp story
As of February 2026 report, over 51,000 workers benefit and median entry-level wage rose from RM1,900 to RM2,200
dependencies: Update Minimum Wage Validation to RM1,700 (Feb 2025 Amendment)

userStories.title: Track OKU (Disabled Employee) Kad OKU Status and Generate Double Deduction Annual Schedule
userStories.description: Employers who hire Malaysian citizens with a valid Disability Card (Kad OKU) from the Social Welfare Department are entitled to double deduction on remuneration paid to those employees, capped at RM48,000 per employee per year (applies when monthly remuneration does not exceed RM4,000). The payroll system must record OKU status, track the monthly remuneration cap, compute the eligible double deduction amount, and produce an annual supporting schedule for the employer's corporate income tax Form C submission.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record has OKU flag, Kad OKU number, and Kad OKU expiry date fields
Payroll calculation checks that OKU employee's monthly remuneration is â‰¤RM4,000 for double deduction eligibility
Annual double deduction amount is computed as min(total annual cash remuneration, RM48,000) per OKU employee
Warning raised when Kad OKU expiry date is within 60 days so employer can request renewal
