userStories.description: The June 2026 revised EP policy introduces maximum employment duration caps for the first time in Malaysian immigration history: EP Category III holders are limited to 5 years of employment in Malaysia and Category II to 10 years. This is a hard legislative ceiling â€” renewal will be refused once the maximum is reached regardless of employer need or employee performance. Employers who fail to track EP commencement dates against the new caps risk being unable to renew work authorisation for key expatriate staff, creating sudden operational gaps.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType stores ep_malaysia_employment_start_date (date when the employee first held an Employment Pass in Malaysia) and ep_category ('Category I' / 'Category II' / 'Category III')
System computes ep_max_tenure_expiry_date as ep_malaysia_employment_start_date + 5 years for Cat III or + 10 years for Cat II
Automated alerts fire at 12 months, 6 months, and 3 months before ep_max_tenure_expiry_date, routed to HR Manager and the assigned Employer Representative
EP Tenure Dashboard shows all EP Cat II/III employees with: employment start date, max expiry date, days remaining, and traffic-light status (green > 12 months, amber 3â€“12 months, red < 3 months)

Payroll validation fires a warning (not a block) if a Salary Slip is submitted for an EP employee whose ep_max_tenure_expiry_date has passed, prompting HR to confirm continued work authorisation exists
technicalNotes: Policy is not retrospective: only applies to new/renewal applications submitted on or after 1 June 2026
ep_malaysia_employment_start_date should represent the first Malaysian EP, not the current employer's engagement date â€” HR may need to source this from the physical EP card or IMI records
Cat I (RM20,000+) has no published maximum duration under the revised policy
For employees who may have held EP under a prior employer, the cumulative tenure counts â€” flag this ambiguity for HR input
Official IMI policy: https://esd.imi.gov.my/portal/latest-news/announcement/announcement-266-ep-salary-policy-2026/
dependencies: Employment Pass Minimum Salary Threshold Validator (June 2026 Revised EP Policy)
Employment Pass Succession Plan Compliance Tracker for EP Categories II and III

userStories.title: CP8D Annual Pre-Submission Employee Data Completeness Validator
userStories.description: LHDN requires employers to upload CP8D (Annual Remuneration Return) via MyTax by 25 February to enable automatic pre-population of employees' individual tax forms, with final Form E submission due by 31 March (or 30 April for e-filers). An incomplete CP8D â€” missing employee TIN, income type breakdown, PCB deducted, EPF/SOCSO/EIS amounts, or BIK values â€” causes LHDN's auto-population to fail and creates reconciliation failures that trigger systematic cross-checks and audit flags. The existing 'Pre-Submission e-PCB Plus Employee TIN and Tax Category Completeness Validation' story covers monthly e-PCB Plus but does not address the annual CP8D/Form E scope, which requires a broader field set including BIK, allowances, and income classification.
acceptanceCriteria: A CP8D Completeness Check tool (accessible from the Payroll module) scans all employees who received salary in the relevant calendar year and flags those missing any of: TIN (custom_employee_tin), employment_type, total_gross_salary (aggregated from Salary Slips), pcb_deducted_ytd, epf_deducted_ytd, socso_deducted_ytd, eis_deducted_ytd, hrdf_levy_ytd, bik_total_ytd, tax_exempt_allowances_ytd
The completeness report shows a traffic-light status per employee: Green (all fields present), Amber (non-critical fields missing), Red (blocking fields missing â€” TIN, income, PCB)
Clicking 'Fix' on any flagged employee navigates to the relevant record for correction
The tool enforces a 25 February soft deadline alert: fires 14 days before (11 February) reminding HR to run the check before the AIS upload window closes
After corrections, a 'Re-validate' button re-runs the check and updates statuses in the same session
Red-status employees are excluded from the CP8D export file with a warning; Amber-status employees are included with a caveat flag
technicalNotes: CP8D fields required by LHDN: refer to the official CP8D format specification on MyTax portal
BIK values must include motor vehicle, housing, utilities, telephone, driver, and other prescribed benefits at their prescribed values from the LHDN BIK public ruling
Tax-exempt allowances include: childcare (RM3,000), parking (RM3,600), meal (RM2,400), petrol/toll (RM6,000 combined), and others under the Income Tax (Exemption) Orders
This validator is distinct from the e-PCB Plus monthly validation which only checks TIN and tax category â€” CP8D requires a full-year income profile
LHDN deadline guide: https://pandahrms.com/malaysia-income-tax-submission-deadlines-2026-ya-2025-a-complete-employer-hr-guide/
dependencies: Pre-Submission e-PCB Plus Employee TIN and Tax Category Completeness Validation
Generate CP8D annual employee remuneration return
LHDN AIS Employer Upload â€” Auto-Submit Employee Remuneration Data to MyTax by 25 February Deadline

userStories.title: EA Form and Form E Cross-Reconciliation Pre-Submission Audit Validator
userStories.description: LHDN's 2026 compliance enforcement explicitly uses automated system-based cross-checking: payroll records must reconcile with individual EA Forms, and EA Form aggregate totals must reconcile with Form E (CP8D). Any divergence â€” omitted BIK values, incorrectly classified allowances, part-timer EA Forms not generated, or PCB amounts differing from payroll â€” is automatically flagged by LHDN and can trigger an audit without prior warning. Employers currently have no built-in pre-submission reconciliation check in ERPNext. Before finalizing and distributing EA Forms (28 February deadline) and submitting Form E (31 March/30 April), a cross-reconciliation report should flag data consistency failures.
acceptanceCriteria: An EA Form / Form E Reconciliation Check tool runs after all EA Forms are generated for the year and before Form E submission
Check 1 â€” Payroll-to-EA reconciliation: sum of all Salary Slip gross_pay for the year per employee matches EA Form B1 (gross salary); variance > RM1 is flagged as a Red error
Check 2 â€” PCB reconciliation: sum of all Salary Slip pcb_deducted for the year matches EA Form D1 (PCB deducted); variance > RM1 is flagged
Check 3 â€” BIK completeness: if any active employee has a custom_bik_vehicle or custom_bik_housing field populated, their EA Form must include a non-zero BIK value; missing BIK on EA is flagged
Check 4 â€” EA Form coverage: all employees (including resigned, part-time, and deceased) who received any salary during the year have an EA Form generated; missing EA Forms are listed
Check 5 â€” Form E aggregate: sum of all EA Form gross_salary values matches the Form E total remuneration field; sum of EA Form PCB values matches Form E total PCB field
A reconciliation summary report with pass/fail per check, total divergence amounts, and drill-down to the offending employee records is generated
The EA Form bulk-email action is blocked if any Red-status reconciliation failures exist, with a confirmation override for Payroll Manager role
technicalNotes: LHDN cross-check methodology: Payroll records â†’ EA Form â†’ Form E must reconcile; automated system detection â€” no manual review required on LHDN's side
Common audit triggers: omitted BIK (company car, phone), incorrect exempt allowance classification, part-timer/resigned employee EA Forms not issued
PandaHR audit trigger guide: https://pandahrms.com/tax-update-2026-form-e-form-ea-malaysia-employer-compliance-guide-and-common-mistakes-that-trigger-lhdn-audits/
KTP.com.my Form E submission 2026 guide: https://www.ktp.com.my/blog/form-e-submission-2026/29jan2026
Tolerance threshold for rounding differences should be RM1 (not zero) to account for Salary Slip rounding across multiple runs
dependencies: Generate EA Form (Borang EA) for employees
Generate Borang E (Form E) employer annual return
Rebuild EA Form to Include All Mandatory LHDN Section A/B/C/D Fields
Implement Benefits-in-Kind (BIK) Prescribed Value Calculation Module

userStories.title: SPIKPA Foreign Worker Health Insurance Annual Policy Renewal Tracker
userStories.description: All foreign workers legally employed in Malaysia are required by the Ministry of Health to maintain an active SPIKPA (Skim Perlindungan Insurans Kesihatan Pekerja Asing) policy annually. The premium is approximately RM120 plus 6% SST and RM10 stamp duty per worker per year, providing RM20,000 in hospitalisation and surgical coverage at MOH hospitals. Failure to maintain a current SPIKPA policy constitutes a breach of work permit conditions and can lead to cancellation of the worker's visa. Employers who deduct the premium from the worker's salary must first obtain written permission from the Director General of Labour (Peninsular Malaysia) under the Employment Act. Currently no story in the PRD tracks SPIKPA policy status, renewal dates, or DG Labour deduction consent per foreign employee.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType gains SPIKPA-related custom fields: custom_spikpa_policy_number (Data), custom_spikpa_policy_start_date (Date), custom_spikpa_policy_expiry_date (Date), custom_spikpa_premium_bearer ('Employer' / 'Employee Deduction'), custom_spikpa_dg_labour_permission_obtained (Check â€” visible only when bearer is 'Employee Deduction'), custom_spikpa_dg_labour_permission_date (Date)
Fields are visible only when nationality is not 'Malaysian'
Automated alert fires 30 days before custom_spikpa_policy_expiry_date, routed to HR Manager, with guidance to renew via the SPIKPA portal
On new foreign employee record Save, system prompts HR to enter SPIKPA policy details within 30 days if fields are blank

A Foreign Worker Insurance Compliance Report lists all foreign employees with: SPIKPA policy status (Active / Expiring Within 30 Days / Expired / Missing), policy expiry date, premium bearer, and DG Labour permission status
If custom_spikpa_premium_bearer is 'Employee Deduction' and custom_spikpa_dg_labour_permission_obtained is unchecked, a warning fires on payroll run to prevent unauthorized wage deduction
technicalNotes: SPIKPA premium: RM120 base + 6% SST (RM7.20) + RM10 stamp duty = approximately RM137.20 per worker per year (verify current rates at spikpa.moh.gov.my)
DG Labour permission for wage deduction is required under Employment Act S.24 â€” failure to obtain this makes the deduction unlawful
SPIKPA renewal is typically aligned with work permit renewal (annual) â€” custom_spikpa_policy_expiry_date should mirror work permit expiry
SPIKPA portal: https://spikpa.moh.gov.my/
Allianz Malaysia SPIKPA product details: https://www.allianz.com.my/corporate/group-protection-and-personal-accident/worker-and-employee-benefits/foreign-workers-insurance.html
dependencies: Implement Foreign Worker Levy Tracking (FWCMS / Multi-Tier Levy Model)
Add nationality field for foreign worker ISO country code

userStories.title: Form E e-Filing Conditional Deadline Engine: 30-Day Extension to 30 April for Electronic Filers
userStories.description: Under LHDN's filing programme, employers who submit Form E (Borang E) via the MyTax e-Filing portal automatically receive a 30-day extension, shifting the submission deadline from 31 March to 30 April of the following year. Employers who submit by post or physically retain the 31 March hard deadline. The current compliance calendar story ('Track Statutory Compliance Submission Calendar') exists but does not model this conditional rule. If the system shows 31 March as a universal deadline for all employers, electronic filers will receive premature overdue alerts and incorrect penalty warnings for the 1â€“30 April window during which they are fully compliant.
acceptanceCriteria: Company DocType gains a custom_form_e_submission_method Select field ('e-Filing via MyTax' / 'Postal / Manual') defaulting to 'e-Filing via MyTax'
The Compliance Submission Calendar computes Form E due date as: 31 March if custom_form_e_submission_method = 'Postal / Manual'; 30 April if 'e-Filing via MyTax'
Overdue alerts for Form E do not fire between 1 April and 30 April for companies flagged as e-Filing submitters
The Compliance Calendar dashboard displays the applicable deadline alongside the submission method for transparency
A Submission Method Confirmation task is auto-created each January, prompting the HR Manager to confirm or update the Form E submission method before the annual cycle begins
LHDN filing programme reference year (e.g., 2026) is stored on the compliance calendar entry to avoid applying prior-year extension rules incorrectly
technicalNotes: LHDN 2026 Return Form Filing Programme: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/fqog1423/rf-filing-programme-for-2026.pdf
The e-Filing extension applies to Form E (employer return), not CP8D separately â€” both are submitted together via MyTax
Extension is automatic upon e-submission; no separate application is needed from the employer
The Compliance Submission Calendar should model this as a conditional date rule, not a hardcoded 31 March entry
PandaHR deadline guide: https://pandahrms.com/malaysia-income-tax-submission-deadlines-2026-ya-2025-a-complete-employer-hr-guide/
dependencies: Track Statutory Compliance Submission Calendar and Auto-Escalate Overdue Filings
Borang E Nil Return Submission Workflow for Dormant and Zero-Employee Companies

userStories.title: HRD Corp Training Claim Focus Area Category Tagging for Industry 4.0, Green Technology, and Smart Economy (2026)
userStories.description: From 2026, HRD Corp officially expanded its approved training focus areas to explicitly include Industry 4.0, Green Technology and Renewable Energy, and Smart Economy disciplines. Training claims under the SBL-KHAS grant (and related schemes) submitted under these focus areas may qualify for enhanced or prioritised claim processing. Employers who do not tag their training claims with the correct HRD Corp focus area cannot demonstrate alignment with national training objectives, which affects their eligibility for targeted government matching grants and may impact Latihan MADANI levy utilization credit. The existing 'HRD Corp eTRIS Training Claim Recording and Levy Utilization Dashboard' story does not capture the focus area dimension.
acceptanceCriteria: The HRD Corp Training Claim DocType gains a custom_hrdcorp_focus_area Select field with options: 'Industry 4.0', 'Green Technology & Renewable Energy', 'Smart Economy', 'Digital Skills', 'Soft Skills / Leadership', 'Technical / Vocational', 'Other' (matching HRD Corp official category taxonomy)
Training claims with a defined focus area display the category tag on the Levy Utilization Dashboard
The Levy Utilization Dashboard allows filtering by focus area to show: total training hours, total levy claimed, and total employees trained per focus area
A Focus Area Coverage Report shows which HRD Corp categories the employer has utilized in the current and prior two years â€” useful for demonstrating breadth of training investment
When creating a training claim, the focus area field is mandatory if the Grant Type is 'SBL-KHAS' or 'SBL'
technicalNotes: HRD Corp 2026 digital learning focus areas officially claimable: Industry 4.0, Green Technology, Smart Economy â€” confirmed in HRD Corp eTRIS portal guidance
SBL-KHAS e-learning daily rate: RM875; in-person soft skills: RM10,500 per day â€” these rates do not change by focus area but focus area tags are needed for grant pre-approval forms
HRD Corp eTRIS portal: https://hrdcorp.gov.my/
BrioHR HRD Corp overview: https://briohr.com/blog/foreign-worker-levy/
disprz.ai digital learning HRD Corp 2026: https://disprz.ai/blog/hrdf-digital-learning-lms-malaysia
dependencies: HRD Corp eTRIS Training Claim Recording and Levy Utilization Dashboard
Track HRD Corp Levy Utilization Rate and Alert on 15% Under-Utilisation Penalty

userStories.title: PERKESO LINDUNG Scheme Rebranding â€” Update All System Labels from SKSPS/SKSSR to LINDUNG Family Names
userStories.description: On August 22, 2025, PERKESO officially rebranded all four protection schemes under the unified 'LINDUNG' umbrella: SKSPS (Self-Employed Social Security) â†’ LINDUNG KENDIRI; SOCSO employee scheme â†’ LINDUNG PEKERJA; EIS (Employment Insurance System) â†’ LINDUNG KERJAYA; SKSSR (Housewives Social Security) â†’ LINDUNG KASIH. All payroll system UI labels, report headers, form descriptions, fixture field options, and statutory correspondence that reference the old scheme names must be updated. This is not a cosmetic change â€” official PERKESO communications, contribution portals, and ASSIST Portal workflows now exclusively use LINDUNG branding, and mismatched labels create regulatory confusion for HR staff and auditors.
acceptanceCriteria: All UI labels, page titles, and report headers using 'SKSPS' are updated to 'LINDUNG KENDIRI'
All UI labels using 'SOCSO scheme' in the LINDUNG PEKERJA context are updated to 'LINDUNG PEKERJA'
All UI labels using 'EIS' in contribution context are annotated with 'LINDUNG KERJAYA (EIS)' where the old acronym must be retained for regulatory form compliance
Existing Gig Workers Act stories' UI references updated from 'SKSPS' to 'LINDUNG KENDIRI' in all user-visible strings
Internal code identifiers (field names, doctype names) retain old names for backward compatibility; only user-visible strings are updated
A mapping table is maintained in code comments: SKSPS=LINDUNG KENDIRI, SKSSR=LINDUNG KASIH, SOCSO=LINDUNG PEKERJA, EIS=LINDUNG KERJAYA
technicalNotes: PERKESO official rebranding announcement: August 22, 2025
The LINDUNG branding covers four schemes; only LINDUNG KENDIRI (gig/self-employed) and LINDUNG PEKERJA (employees) are directly relevant to payroll
Statutory forms (Borang 8A, Borang 34, etc.) still use 'SOCSO'/'PERKESO' â€” do not change those form names
PERKESO ASSIST Portal login URL unchanged: https://assist.perkeso.gov.my/
Gig Workers existing stories reference 'SKSPS' â€” coordinate with all three Gig Workers Act stories to update labels
dependencies: Gig Workers Act 2025 â€” Auto-Register Gig Workers Under PERKESO SKSPS via ASSIST Portal API
Gig Workers Act 2025 â€” SKSPS Per-Transaction Contribution Calculation and PERKESO Monthly Aggregate Remittance

userStories.title: EPF Foreign Worker Permanent Departure Withdrawal Advisory Checklist on Employment Termination
userStories.description: With mandatory EPF contributions for foreign workers effective October 2025 (2% employer + 2% employee), employers now accumulate EPF savings on behalf of non-Malaysian employees. When a foreign employee's employment terminates, resigns, or their work pass expires, they are eligible to withdraw their full EPF balance under the 'Leaving Country' provision. However, the application must be initiated at least 2 months before departure. If the employer fails to inform the employee of this entitlement, the foreign worker may forfeit time-sensitive withdrawal rights. The payroll system must auto-generate a structured withdrawal advisory checklist on termination of any non-Malaysian employee with EPF contributions.
acceptanceCriteria: On Employee record status change to 'Left' / 'Terminated' / 'Resigned' for a non-Malaysian employee (nationality != 'Malaysian'), system auto-creates an 'EPF Departure Withdrawal Advisory' task assigned to HR Manager
Advisory task includes: employee name, total EPF balance (if available), termination date, and a 60-day countdown from termination date to departure-assumed-date

Advisory checklist items: (1) Obtain work pass cancellation from Immigration, (2) Provide resignation/termination letter to employee, (3) Obtain income tax clearance (CP21), (4) Employee to apply via KWSP i-Akaun or KWSP office 2 months before departure
Task includes a link to the official KWSP Leaving Country withdrawal page

If the employee has no EPF contributions (joined before October 2025 mandatory date or is a domestic servant exclusion), advisory is still created but with a note: 'No mandatory EPF contributions â€” check if voluntary contributions exist'
Task closure requires HR to record: advisory_delivered_date and employee_acknowledged (Check)
technicalNotes: KWSP Act 1991 Section 54 â€” Leaving Country withdrawal provision
Application timeline: 2 months before departure; processing takes several weeks
Required documents: cancelled work pass OR termination letter, income tax clearance, bank account details
Domestic servants (maids, cooks, cleaners, drivers) are excluded from mandatory EPF contributions â€” flag via existing 'domestic_servant_exclusion' field
Coordinate with existing CP21/e-SPC workflow for tax clearance step
KWSP official Leaving Country guide: https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/member/account-centre/leaving-country
dependencies: Auto-Submit CP21/CP22A/CP22B via e-SPC MyTax at Cessation or Departure
Foreign Worker EPF Oct 2025 Mandate: Domestic Servant Category Exclusion Flag

userStories.title: e-Duti Setem Per-Employee Contract Stamp Duty Compliance Task with 30-Day Countdown (Effective January 2026)
userStories.description: From January 1, 2026, all employment contracts must be stamped within 30 days of signing via LHDN's new e-Duti Setem system (accessible through the MyTax Portal). A fixed stamp duty of RM10 applies per employment contract under Item 4, First Schedule of the Stamp Act 1949. Late stamping incurs escalating penalties. Contracts executed before January 1, 2025 are exempt. The existing story 'Track Employment Contract Stamp Duty Compliance via e-Duti Setem MyTax (Mandatory from 2026)' establishes the general framework but does not specify per-employee task auto-creation with countdown. This story implements the operational per-hire task generation and reference number recording.
acceptanceCriteria: On new Employee record creation with a hire_date of 2026-01-01 or later, system auto-creates a 'Contract Stamp Duty' compliance task assigned to HR Manager with due date = hire_date + 25 days (5-day buffer before the 30-day statutory deadline)
Task includes: employee name, hire_date (= contract signing date assumed), stamp duty amount (RM10), and a link to e-Duti Setem on MyTax portal
Employee record gains two fields: custom_stamp_duty_status Select ('Pending' / 'Completed' / 'Overdue') and custom_stamp_duty_ref (Data) for storing the LHDN e-Duti Setem reference number
When custom_stamp_duty_ref is populated, custom_stamp_duty_status auto-updates to 'Completed' and the compliance task is auto-closed
If task is not closed by due date, custom_stamp_duty_status changes to 'Overdue' and an escalation alert fires to Payroll Manager
Employees hired before 2026-01-01 are excluded from stamp duty task creation; a filter is applied on the hire_date
technicalNotes: Stamp Act 1949, First Schedule Item 4: RM10 fixed duty per employment contract
e-Duti Setem portal accessible via MyTax from December 15, 2025; mandatory stamping enforcement from January 1, 2026
30-day deadline runs from the date the contract is executed (signed), which is typically the hire_date or offer acceptance date
For contracts signed before January 1, 2026 but during 2025: no late penalty if stamped by December 31, 2025
LHDN e-Duti Setem announcement: https://stamps.hasil.gov.my/stamps/
The Vibes mandatory stamping announcement: https://www.thevibes.com/articles/news/109052/lhdn-to-enforce-mandatory-stamping-of-employment-contracts-from-2026
dependencies: Track Employment Contract Stamp Duty Compliance via e-Duti Setem MyTax (Mandatory from 2026)
New Hire Statutory Compliance Checklist with Multi-Agency Deadline Tracker

userStories.title: HRD Corp RM50,000 Levy Balance Threshold Tracker and 15% Auto-Deduction Ledger Reconciliation
userStories.description: Starting March 1, 2025, HRD Corp automatically deducts 15% of an employer's accumulated unused levy balance when two conditions are met: (1) the year-end levy balance exceeds RM50,000 AND (2) the training utilization rate is below the threshold. The auto-deducted amount funds the Latihan Madani programme. A two-month grace period (Januaryâ€“February each year) allows employers to utilize levy before the deduction applies. The existing story 'Track HRD Corp Levy Utilization Rate and Alert on 15% Under-Utilisation Penalty' focuses on a general alert but does not model the RM50,000 threshold condition, the automatic deduction mechanism (distinct from a fine), or the ledger reconciliation entry when HRD Corp actually executes the deduction.
acceptanceCriteria: HRD Corp Levy Ledger shows: monthly levy contributions, approved training grant claims, running balance, and year-to-date utilization rate (%)
Alert Type 1 (Threshold Warning): fires when accumulated year-end levy balance is projected to exceed RM50,000 AND utilization rate < 50%, triggered during Novemberâ€“December
Alert Type 2 (Grace Period Reminder): fires each January 1 if the employer met the deduction trigger conditions in the prior year, reminding HR to utilize levy before February 28

Reconciliation Entry: a HRD Corp Auto-Deduction record can be manually created when HRD Corp executes the 15% deduction, capturing: deduction_date, deduction_amount, hrdcorp_notification_ref, program ('Latihan MADANI'), and reducing the ledger balance accordingly
The levy dashboard shows a 'Deduction Risk' banner when balance > RM50,000 and utilization < 50%
Levy utilization rate is computed as: (sum of approved grant claims for current year) / (sum of levy contributions for current year) Ã— 100
technicalNotes: HRD Corp policy: 15% deduction applies only when levy balance â‰¥ RM50,000 (for the immediately preceding year's balance) AND utilization is low
This is an automatic deduction BY HRD Corp from the employer's levy account â€” not an administrative fine â€” and is used to fund Latihan MADANI
Grace period is January 1 to February 28 annually â€” employers can avoid deduction by spending down the levy on HRD Corp-approved training before March 1
Voluntary-registered employers (5â€“9 employees, 0.5% rate) have lower levy accumulation and are less likely to reach RM50,000
OTC Training Centre article on 15% deduction: https://otc.com.my/15-deduction-of-unused-hrd-corp-levy-starting-1-march-2025/
HRD Corp FAQs: https://hrdcorp.gov.my/faq
dependencies: Track HRD Corp Levy Utilization Rate and Alert on 15% Under-Utilisation Penalty
HRD Corp eTRIS Monthly Levy Payment Deadline Alert and Payment Reference Tracking

userStories.title: Gig Workers Bill 2025 â€” Platform Provider Registration as PERKESO Contributing Entity with Mandatory 1.25% LINDUNG KENDIRI Deduction on Royal Assent
userStories.description: The Malaysia Gig Workers Bill 2025, passed by the Senate on September 9, 2025 and awaiting royal assent, mandates platform providers (digital intermediary system operators) to register with PERKESO as a 'Contributing Entity' and integrate via API for automatic deduction of 1.25% of each gig worker's earnings for LINDUNG KENDIRI (formerly SKSPS). Unlike voluntary SKSPS contributions, this is a mandatory statutory obligation triggered on royal assent. The existing gig worker stories reference voluntary SKSPS registration; this story covers the mandatory Contributing Entity onboarding, the API integration for per-transaction contribution calculation, and the monthly aggregate remittance to PERKESO on behalf of all platform gig workers.
acceptanceCriteria: Company DocType gains a custom_is_gig_platform (Check) flag; when checked, a 'Gig Platform Contributing Entity' section appears with: custom_perkeso_contributing_entity_id (Data), custom_perkeso_api_credential_ref (Data), and custom_gig_platform_api_integrated (Check)
Platform-type employers can create Gig Worker records (separate from Employee) with fields: worker_name, nric_or_passport, perkeso_member_id, platform_join_date, contribution_rate (default 1.25%)
Per-transaction earnings recorded against each Gig Worker; monthly aggregate contribution = sum of all transaction earnings Ã— 1.25% for each gig worker
Monthly LINDUNG KENDIRI remittance report generates a per-worker contribution summary file for PERKESO ASSIST Portal submission
System validates that each active Gig Worker has a valid PERKESO LINDUNG KENDIRI registration before allowing monthly remittance
A 'Contributing Entity Registration' checklist task is auto-created on royal assent date (configurably set) prompting HR to register via PERKESO ASSIST Portal
technicalNotes: Gig Workers Bill 2025 passed Senate September 9, 2025; awaiting royal assent â€” implementation date = royal assent date
Contribution rate: 1.25% of gig worker earnings deducted from gig worker's payment and remitted to PERKESO LINDUNG KENDIRI
Platform providers must integrate via PERKESO API (spec not yet published); manual upload via ASSIST Portal as interim measure
Note: the Bill does NOT require platform providers to contribute employer-side EPF (unlike traditional employers) â€” only PERKESO deduction from gig earnings
Herbert Smith Freehills Gig Workers Bill analysis: https://www.hsfkramer.com/notes/employment/2025-posts/malaysia-gig-workers-bill-2025
DFDL Gig Workers Bill tabling: https://www.dfdl.com/insights/legal-and-tax-updates/malaysia-gig-workers-bill-2025-tabled-for-first-reading/
dependencies: Gig Workers Act 2025 â€” Auto-Register Gig Workers Under PERKESO SKSPS via ASSIST Portal API
Gig Workers Act 2025 â€” SKSPS Per-Transaction Contribution Calculation and PERKESO Monthly Aggregate Remittance
PERKESO LINDUNG Scheme Rebranding â€” Update All System Labels from SKSPS/SKSSR to LINDUNG Family Names

userStories.title: Budget 2026 â€” Double Tax Deduction for Hiring Prisoners Released on Licence and Drug Dependents (YA2026â€“YA2030)
userStories.description: Malaysia's Budget 2026 expanded the existing further deduction (double deduction) incentive for employers who hire vulnerable persons to include two new categories effective from YA2026 to YA2030: (1) prisoners released on licence under the Prisons Act 1995, and (2) drug/substance dependents and misusers currently undergoing approved treatment or rehabilitation programmes. Previously, the double deduction applied only to OKU (persons with disabilities), senior citizens, single parents, and ex-convicts under the Income Tax Act. The payroll/HR system must flag employees under these new categories, collect the required supporting documentation references, and include them in the annual double deduction schedule submitted as supporting evidence for the employer's tax return.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType gains two new custom fields: custom_prisoner_licence_status (Check, 'Prisoner Released on Licence under Prisons Act 1995') and custom_drug_rehab_participant (Check, 'Drug/Substance Dependent in Approved Rehabilitation')
Each flag field has a corresponding document reference field: custom_prisoner_licence_doc_ref (Data) and custom_drug_rehab_doc_ref (Data) for storing the approval or certification reference number
YA eligibility guard: flags can only be set for employees hired from YA2026 onward; a validation warning fires if set for employees with hire_date before 2026-01-01

The annual Double Deduction Schedule report (generated before employer tax filing) includes employees in all eligible categories: OKU + Senior Citizen + Single Parent + Ex-Convict + Prisoner on Licence + Drug Rehab Participant
Report groups employees by category with total annual remuneration paid â€” this is the double deduction base amount for the employer's income tax claim
A warning is triggered if either new flag is set without a corresponding document reference being populated
technicalNotes: Income Tax Act 1967 Schedule 4B (further deductions for employment of vulnerable persons); Budget 2026 gazette expected early 2026
Supporting documents: letter from Prisons Department (for Prisoners Released on Licence); letter from Ministry of Health / Welfare Department (for drug rehabilitation participants)
The deduction is on the employer's corporate tax return, not on the employee's PCB â€” this is an employer-side tax benefit, not an employee relief
Existing OKU double deduction tracking story: 'Track OKU (Disabled Employee) Kad OKU Status and Generate Double Deduction Annual Schedule' â€” extend the same module
Budget 2026 key highlights: https://www.crowe.com/my/-/media/crowe/firms/asia-pacific/my/crowemy/news/key-highlights-of-malaysias-budget-2026--for-individuals-and-businesses-newsletter.pdf
dependencies: Track OKU (Disabled Employee) Kad OKU Status and Generate Double Deduction Annual Schedule

userStories.title: MySIP 1:3 Internship Ratio Compliance Dashboard for Employers with Employment Pass Holders
userStories.description: TalentCorp's 1:3 Internship Policy (effective April 1, 2026 after pilot extension to March 31, 2026) requires employers holding Employment Passes for foreign nationals to offer MySIP structured internship placements to local students proportionally: 1 intern per EP Category I holder, 2 interns per EP Category II holder, and 3 interns per EP Category III holder, capped at 2% of total Malaysian workforce. Non-compliance will adversely affect future EP renewal applications â€” a critical operational risk. The existing MySIP story ('MySIP Structured Internship Stipend Minimum Threshold Validation') covers stipend amounts and tax deductions but does not track the ratio compliance logic against actual EP headcount. This story addresses the missing ratio tracking dimension.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType gains a custom_ep_internship_ratio_category Select field (None / EP Category I / EP Category II / EP Category III) for non-Malaysian EP holders
System computes monthly minimum MySIP internship obligation = (EP Cat I count Ã— 1) + (EP Cat II count Ã— 2) + (EP Cat III count Ã— 3), capped at floor(total_malaysian_headcount Ã— 0.02)

Active MySIP Intern count is tracked via a new MySIP Intern record linked to the Employer, with fields: intern_name, nric_or_student_id, institution, ep_holder_linked (Link to Employee), start_date, end_date, weekly_stipend
A MySIP Compliance Dashboard shows: required internship slots, active intern count, compliance status (Compliant / Shortfall / Not Applicable), and next EP renewal date
Alert fires 60 days before any EP holder's renewal date if the employer is in Shortfall status
A compliance flag is added to the EP holder's employee record: custom_internship_obligation_met (Yes / No / Not Applicable)
technicalNotes: Policy ratios: EP Category I â†’ 1:1; EP Category II â†’ 1:2; EP Category III â†’ 1:3 (i.e., 1 EP holder requires X interns)
2% workforce cap: if computed slots > 2% of Malaysian employee headcount, cap applies
Pilot phase extended to March 31, 2026; mandatory enforcement from April 1, 2026
Minimum stipend: RM600/month for degree-level interns, RM500/month for diploma-level interns
TalentCorp MySIP portal: https://www.talentcorp.com.my/mysip-incentives/
Fragomen policy guide: https://www.fragomen.com/insights/malaysia-new-internship-policy-to-require-employers-with-employment-pass-employees-to-hire-interns.html
dependencies: MySIP Structured Internship Stipend Minimum Threshold Validation and Double Tax Deduction Payroll Tagging
Employment Pass Minimum Salary Threshold Validator (June 2026 Revised EP Policy)

userStories.title: Workplace Harassment Penal Code Amendment â€” Criminal Offense Escalation Flag on HR Cases (Effective July 2025)
userStories.description: Effective July 11, 2025, Malaysia amended the Penal Code to make workplace bullying and harassment a criminal offense. This creates a two-track compliance obligation: (1) the internal investigation track under Employment Act Section 81F (30-day employer investigation, up to RM50,000 administrative penalty) and (2) the criminal escalation track under the Penal Code (police report, potential prosecution). The existing story 'Employment Act Amendment 2025 â€” Sexual Harassment Mandatory 30-Day Investigation Tracker with RM50,000 Penalty Alert' covers only the Employment Act track. Employers need to distinguish cases where the complainant or HR has escalated to a criminal complaint â€” different timelines, different actors, and different reporting obligations apply.
acceptanceCriteria: HR Harassment Case DocType gains a custom_case_track Select field: 'Internal Investigation (Employment Act S.81F)' / 'Criminal Complaint (Penal Code S.507A)' / 'Both Tracks'
If 'Criminal Complaint' is selected, a police_report_ref field (Data) appears and the 30-day internal resolution timer is paused (criminal proceedings take precedence)
Criminal track record stores: police_report_date, police_station, case_number, outcome (Ongoing / Charges Filed / Acquitted / Convicted / Withdrawn)
Annual Harassment Compliance Summary distinguishes internal-track vs. criminal-track cases in separate sections
When 'Both Tracks' is selected, both the 30-day internal timer AND the criminal outcome field are active simultaneously

A Legal Notice warning banner appears on the case record when criminal track is selected: 'This case has been escalated to criminal proceedings â€” engage legal counsel and notify Management immediately'
technicalNotes: Malaysia Penal Code Amendment effective July 11, 2025 â€” criminalized workplace harassment/bullying as new offense
Anti-Sexual Harassment Act 2022 also in force â€” independent complaints body separate from employer investigation
Employment Act S.81F: employer must investigate within 30 days; failure = RM50,000 fine
Criminal track: complainant or employer can lodge police report under amended Penal Code; this is independent of the internal investigation
Aliran article on no more silent suffering: https://m.aliran.com/newsletters/no-more-silent-suffering-malaysias-new-laws-tackle-harassment-head-on
Herbert Smith Freehills on Anti-Sexual Harassment Act: https://www.hsfkramer.com/notes/employment/2023-12/malaysia-anti-sexual-harassment-act-2022
dependencies: Employment Act Amendment 2025 â€” Sexual Harassment Mandatory 30-Day Investigation Tracker with RM50,000 Penalty Alert

userStories.title: Progressive Wage Policy â€” 21-Hour Annual Training Completion Tracking per Eligible Employee
userStories.description: Employers participating in Malaysia's Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) must ensure each eligible employee (local, full-time, earning RM1,700â€“RM4,999/month) completes a minimum of 21 training hours per calendar year as a condition for receiving the government wage subsidy (RM200 million budget, targeting 50,000 workers in 2025â€“2026). Training hours not completed in 2025 can be carried forward until December 2026. The system must track training hours per employee, link to HRD Corp eTRIS-approved training records, and validate the 21-hour threshold for each eligible employee before the PWP subsidy claim is generated. The existing 'Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag and Wage Escalation Compliance Report' story tracks wage levels but does not address the mandatory training hours dimension.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee DocType gains a custom_pwp_eligible Check field (auto-set when nationality='Malaysian', employment_type='Full-Time', and basic_salary between RM1,700 and RM4,999)
A PWP Training Log child table (or linked DocType) records training sessions per employee: date, course_name, provider, hours_completed, hrdcorp_etris_ref, and carry_forward_year flag
System computes per-employee: training_hours_this_year (sum of current-year logs) and carry_forward_hours (from prior year, valid until December 2026)

PWP Training Compliance Report lists all eligible employees with: total hours completed, carry-forward hours applied, shortfall vs. 21-hour target, and PWP subsidy claim readiness (Ready / Shortfall / Not Started)
Alert fires to HR Manager by October 31 each year for employees who have not yet met the 21-hour threshold, giving 2 months to schedule remaining training
PWP subsidy claim generation is blocked for employees with training shortfall; override requires Payroll Manager approval
technicalNotes: PWP launched June 2024; RM200 million budget for 2025; targets local private-sector employees in RM1,700â€“RM4,999 wage band
21-hour training requirement per employee per year; 2025 uncompleted hours carry forward to December 2026
Training providers must be HRD Corp or JTK-approved; government-linked companies and MNCs are excluded from the subsidy scheme
PWP managed by SOCSO; subsidy application via SOCSO portal â€” not through LHDN
Omni HR PWP guide: https://www.omnihr.co/blog/progressive-wage-model-malaysia
Malaysia Progressive Wage Policy ISIS analysis: https://www.isis.org.my/2024/03/25/malaysias-progressive-wage-policy/
dependencies: Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag and Wage Escalation Compliance Report
HRD Corp eTRIS Training Claim Recording and Levy Utilization Dashboard

userStories.title: VSS/MSS Employer EIS Loss-of-Employment Certification for Retrenched Employee EIS Benefit Claims
userStories.description: When an employer conducts a Voluntary Separation Scheme (VSS) or Mutual Separation Scheme (MSS) exercise, each affected employee is eligible for EIS (LINDUNG KERJAYA) job loss benefits including Job Search Allowance (JSA) for 3â€“6 months. For the employee to claim EIS benefits, the employer must provide a termination letter specifying the correct EIS Loss-of-Employment reason code ('VSS' or 'MSS' or 'Retrenchment') and notify PERKESO ASSIST Portal. If the employer fails to provide the correct documentation within 60 days, the employee cannot access EIS benefits. No existing story covers the VSS/MSS-specific employer obligations for EIS benefit facilitation; the current EIS stories cover general contributions and enhanced benefits display.
acceptanceCriteria: An 'Employee Termination' DocType (or workflow step on Employee record) captures termination_reason Select field including: 'VSS â€” Voluntary Separation', 'MSS â€” Mutual Separation', 'Retrenchment', 'Contract End', 'Dismissal', 'Resignation', 'Retirement'

When termination_reason is VSS, MSS, or Retrenchment, system auto-generates an EIS Termination Certificate for the employee containing: employer name, EPF/SOCSO number, employee IC, last working day, EIS reason code, and HR Manager signature line
Certificate template uses PERKESO-required format; includes a QR code link to PERKESO EIS claim page
System creates a 60-day task: 'Issue EIS Termination Certificate to Employee' â€” if not closed within 60 days, escalation alert fires
Bulk VSS Exercise support: HR can select multiple employees, apply the same termination_reason, and bulk-generate EIS Termination Certificates in a single action
A VSS/MSS Exercise record aggregates: exercise_name, start_date, affected_count, certificates_issued, certificates_pending
technicalNotes: EIS Act 2017 (LINDUNG KERJAYA): employees must apply for JSA within 60 days of Loss of Employment date
EIS Loss-of-Employment codes accepted by PERKESO: VSS, MSS, Retrenchment, Constructive Dismissal, Natural Disaster, Company Closure
Employer's primary obligation: provide clear termination letter with reason and SOCSO number; no API submission required â€” employee self-applies via MySOCSOPayroll portal
JSA benefit: 80% of wage for month 1, tapering to 50% by month 6; capped at EIS contribution ceiling (RM6,000)
PERKESO EIS official: https://www.perkeso.gov.my/en/benefits/employment-insurance-system.html
Malaysia.gov.my EIS guide: https://www.malaysia.gov.my/en/categories/aid-welfare-and-assistance/job-aid/employment-insurance-scheme-eis-assistance-under-perkeso
dependencies: EIS Amendment 2025 â€” Display Updated Statutory EIS Benefit Rates on Employee Termination Workflow
Implement Termination and Lay-Off Benefits Calculator

userStories.title: PDPA 2024 Payroll Data Retention Schedule â€” 7-Year LHDN Override vs. PDPA Data Minimization Conflict Resolution
userStories.description: The Personal Data Protection (Amendment) Act 2024 (effective June 2025) requires organisations to delete personal data when the purpose of processing is no longer needed (data minimization principle). However, ITA Section 82 mandates that employers retain payroll tax records for 7 years, and the Employment Act requires 6-year payroll wage register retention. These legal obligations are in direct conflict for ex-employee personal data: PDPA says delete when purpose expires, while LHDN and MOHR say retain for 6â€“7 years. The payroll system must explicitly model a 'Statutory Retention Lock' that prevents PDPA-driven data deletion for records within the mandatory retention window, and auto-releases the lock when the retention period expires.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record (and linked Salary Slips, EA Forms, EPF/SOCSO submissions) gains a custom_statutory_retention_until (Date) field computed as max(termination_date + 7 years [LHDN ITA S.82], termination_date + 6 years [EA wage register])

When custom_statutory_retention_until is in the future, a 'Statutory Retention Lock' banner appears on the Employee record preventing deletion and displaying the reason ('LHDN ITA S.82' or 'Employment Act S.61A')
System prevents any bulk-delete or anonymization action on locked employee records; delete action requires override by System Manager with documented justification
A Data Retention Dashboard lists all ex-employees with: termination date, retention_until date, retention_status (Locked / Unlocked), and days until lock expires
When custom_statutory_retention_until date passes, system sends a notification to Data Protection Officer (DPO) that retention obligation has expired and PDPA data minimization can be applied
DPO Compliance Log records: notification_sent_date, dpo_action (Anonymized / Deleted / Retained with justification), action_date, and reason_note
technicalNotes: PDPA 2024 Amendment: data minimization principle effective June 2025; DPO appointment mandatory for large-scale processors
ITA 1967 Section 82: employer must retain tax records (payroll, PCB, EA forms, CP39) for 7 years from the year of assessment
Employment Act 1955 implied through court precedent: payroll wage register retained 6 years (also covered by 'Implement Payroll Wage Register with 6-Year Retention' story)
The 7-year LHDN retention is the longer obligation and takes precedence â€” use max(6, 7) = 7 years as the retention lock period
PDPA 2024 Amendment Act A1727: https://www.pdp.gov.my/ppdpv1/en/akta/personal-data-protection-amendment-act-2024/
ASEAN Briefing PDPA enforcement from June 2025: https://www.aseanbriefing.com/news/malaysia-tightens-data-protection-from-june-2025/
dependencies: Implement Payroll Wage Register with 6-Year Retention and LHDN/MOHR Audit Export
Enforce ITA Section 82 7-Year Payroll Tax Record Retention (Separate from MOHR 6-Year)
PDPA 2024 DPO Appointment Registry and 72-Hour Payroll Data Breach Notification Workflow

userStories.title: EIS Amendment 2025 S.45F â€” Moratorium Phase Suppression and Voluntary Job Vacancy Reporting Module
userStories.description: The Employment Insurance System (Amendment) Act 2025 introduces Section 45F requiring employers to notify PERKESO in writing about job vacancies before hiring and confirm fill within 7 days of placement (penalty: RM10,000). However, PERKESO has announced a 2-year moratorium comprising (1) a design-and-consult phase and (2) a voluntary low-risk reporting phase before mandatory enforcement. The existing story 'EIS Amendment 2025 (S.45F) â€” Employer Job Vacancy Pre-Notification and 7-Day Post-Filling PERKESO Alert' covers the notification mechanism but not the moratorium management. During the moratorium, penalty alerts must be suppressed; employers who choose to voluntarily participate need an opt-in mechanism.
acceptanceCriteria: Company record gains a custom_eis_s45f_mode field: 'Moratorium â€” Not Reporting' / 'Voluntary Reporting (Early Adopter)' / 'Mandatory Reporting' with an effective_date for mode transitions
All EIS S.45F penalty alerts and notifications are suppressed when mode is 'Moratorium â€” Not Reporting'
When mode is 'Voluntary Reporting', vacancy notification records can be created and submitted; the system logs voluntary submissions separately for early-adopter audit trail
A system-wide compliance reminder fires 90 days before the moratorium end date (configured in System Settings as custom_eis_moratorium_end_date)
On moratorium expiry, custom_eis_s45f_mode auto-transitions to 'Mandatory Reporting' and S.45F notifications become active
Employer groups back EIS vacancy reporting reforms â€” moratorium phase shows in compliance calendar as 'Voluntary â€” No Penalty Until [date]'
technicalNotes: EIS (Amendment) Act 2025, new Section 45F â€” enacted 2025; penalty RM10,000 for non-compliance after enforcement
PERKESO moratorium: 2 years from Act commencement date â€” Phase 1: design/consult roundtables; Phase 2: voluntary use
SMEs are the primary target for the moratorium extension (Lexology, NST articles)
The reporting mechanism is to PERKESO ASSIST Portal; API endpoint not yet published
Lexology EIS amendment detail: https://www.lexology.com/library/detail.aspx?g=307fc1fa-bdbb-47aa-8a39-89fe02c562ce
NST employer groups backing reforms: https://www.nst.com.my/amp/business/corporate/2025/12/1334538/employer-groups-back-eis-vacancy-reporting-reforms-urge-practical-implementation
dependencies: EIS Amendment 2025 (S.45F) â€” Employer Job Vacancy Pre-Notification and 7-Day Post-Filling PERKESO Alert
Track Statutory Compliance Submission Calendar and Auto-Escalate Overdue Filings

userStories.title: EP June 2026 New Salary Threshold Pre-Renewal Alert for Existing Below-Threshold EP Holders
userStories.description: Malaysia's Ministry of Home Affairs revised EP minimum salary thresholds effective 1 June 2026: Category I jumps from RM10,000 to RM20,000; Category II from RM5,000â€“RM9,999 to RM10,000â€“RM19,999; Category III from RM3,000â€“RM4,999 to RM5,000â€“RM9,999. Existing EP holders whose current payroll salary falls below the new threshold for their category face renewal rejection unless salary is raised before renewal. The payroll system must proactively flag these employees distinct from the new-hire application validator.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee record stores EP Category (I / II / III) and EP expiry date fields
Payroll system computes whether current gross monthly salary meets the new threshold per EP category
Auto-alert raised 90 days before EP expiry when employee salary is below the new threshold for their category
Dashboard view shows all EP holders with: current salary, required threshold, monthly shortfall, and renewal date
Alert suppressed if employee's salary has already been updated to meet or exceed the new threshold
System stores the Cabinet-approved enforcement date (1 June 2026) as the threshold validation trigger date
technicalNotes: New thresholds effective 1 June 2026 (all new and renewal EP applications): Cat I â‰¥ RM20,000; Cat II RM10,000â€“RM19,999; Cat III RM5,000â€“RM9,999
Cabinet approval: 17 October 2025; announcement: ESD/MOHA January 2026

Distinct from existing 'Employment Pass Minimum Salary Threshold Validator (June 2026 Revised EP Policy)' which validates new applications â€” this story alerts for EXISTING holders approaching renewal
Category II and III holders also require succession plans filed with renewal application â€” succession plan status should be tracked alongside salary alert
Sources: KPMG flash alert 2026-017, Baker McKenzie, ESD official announcement
dependencies: Employment Pass Minimum Salary Threshold Validator (June 2026 Revised EP Policy)

userStories.title: PERKESO ASSIST e-Caruman API Response Parsing and Contribution Correction Workflow
userStories.description: When SOCSO/EIS contribution files are submitted via the PERKESO ASSIST portal, the portal returns a structured response indicating which employee contribution lines were accepted, rejected, or flagged for correction (e.g., missing PERKESO number, IC mismatch, wage ceiling exceeded). Without parsing this response, errors go undetected and contributions remain unposted to the employee's PERKESO account. The payroll system must parse the response and generate an actionable correction list for HR to resolve before the payment deadline.
acceptanceCriteria: After e-Caruman file submission, system reads the PERKESO ASSIST portal response and maps rejection codes to human-readable correction instructions
Rejected records listed with: employee name, IC, error code, error description, and recommended fix (e.g., 'Update PERKESO number on Employee record')
Correction workflow allows HR to fix errors inline and regenerate only the rejected lines for resubmission (delta re-upload, not full re-upload)
System stores submission status per employee per month: Pending / Accepted / Rejected / Corrected
Dashboard shows monthly SOCSO/EIS submission health: acceptance rate, count of pending corrections, deadline countdown
If no response is received within 24 hours of upload, system alerts HR to manually check ASSIST portal status
technicalNotes: PERKESO ASSIST portal: https://assist.perkeso.gov.my/

Common rejection reasons: employee not yet registered with PERKESO, IC mismatch, wage above RM6,000 ceiling without bracketed table correction, foreign worker not covered under SOCSO Employment Injury scheme
October 2024 PERKESO wage ceiling update to RM6,000 â€” submissions still using old RM5,000 ceiling are rejected
This story complements 'Generate SOCSO and EIS e-Caruman Upload File (PERKESO ASSIST Portal)' â€” that story generates the file; this story handles the response processing
EIS rejections may occur when employee is above age 60 or is a non-resident exempt from EIS â€” exemption flags must be pre-set on Employee record
dependencies: Generate SOCSO and EIS e-Caruman Upload File (PERKESO ASSIST Portal)
Enforce SOCSO/EIS Wage Ceiling Update to RM6,000 (October 2024 Amendment)

userStories.title: PTPTN Payroll Deduction Facilitation and Monthly Remittance Tracking Module
userStories.description: Employers who facilitate PTPTN (National Higher Education Fund) student loan repayments via salary deduction must obtain written employee consent under the PTPTN Act 1997 and Section 24 of the Employment Act before processing the deduction. The deducted amount must be remitted monthly to PTPTN separately from statutory deductions, and must be excluded from the PCB computation base and excluded from EA Form employment income columns.
acceptanceCriteria: Employee PTPTN consent record (written authorisation date, reference number, repayment schedule) stored on Employee DocType
Monthly PTPTN deduction is processed as a non-statutory deduction in Salary Slip â€” separate from EPF/SOCSO/EIS/PCB
PTPTN deduction amount is excluded from Gross Taxable Salary when computing PCB/MTD
Payslip clearly labels PTPTN deduction in a dedicated 'Other Authorised Deductions' section
Monthly PTPTN remittance schedule generated (employee IC, PTPTN reference number, deduction amount) for HR upload to MyPTPN portal
EA Form Part B does NOT include PTPTN deduction as an income reduction â€” it is purely a payroll facilitation pass-through
technicalNotes: PTPTN Act 1997 Section 22 authorises salary deduction with borrower consent â€” employer is a facilitator only, not a statutory collector
PTPTN repayment is NOT a tax-deductible relief for the employee (unlike EPF or life insurance); exclude from TP1 relief fields
Remittance file format: MyPTPN employer portal at https://myptptn.ptptn.gov.my/
Employment Act Section 24(2)(d) allows authorised deductions; combined total of all non-statutory deductions must not reduce net pay below RM1,700 statutory minimum wage

userStories.title: TP1 Form Version Enforcement â€” Reject Superseded TP1(1/2025) After January 2026 PCB Period
userStories.description: LHDN releases an updated TP1 form each year incorporating Budget changes. TP1(1/2026) became mandatory from January 2026 and contains new relief line items (life/medical insurance extended to children, domestic tourism RM1,000, EV charging RM2,500, autism relief RM10,000). Employees who submit the superseded TP1(1/2025) after January 2026 will declare incorrect reliefs. The payroll system must detect and reject outdated TP1 versions on upload and prompt employees to resubmit using the current form.
acceptanceCriteria: System stores TP1 form version metadata (1/2025 or 1/2026) when an employee submits a TP1 form
Any TP1(1/2025) submission received from January 2026 onwards triggers a validation warning and is not processed for PCB reduction
Employee shown actionable error: 'You submitted TP1(1/2025). Please download and resubmit using TP1(1/2026) from hasil.gov.my'
Link to official LHDN TP1(1/2026) PDF surfaced in the error message
Batch report shows all employees with an active but superseded TP1 on file as of January 2026
Form version check implemented as a configurable 'active TP1 version' setting in Company payroll config (e.g., '1/2026') to enable annual update without code deployment
technicalNotes: TP1(1/2026) official PDF: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/gjuhcbc2/bi-tp1-form-2026.pdf
TP1(1/2025) official PDF: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/qrulhwku/tp1-2025-en.pdf
Version indicator '(1/2026)' appears in the form title â€” detection can use submission date as proxy (TP1 submitted on or after January 2026 must use 1/2026 form)
PCB 2026 Specification confirms no MTD formula change but amended TP1 deduction items mandatory from January 2026
Same pattern repeats annually â€” configurable active version avoids annual code changes
dependencies: Update TP1(1/2026) Relief Categories and PCB Computation Engine for YA2026 Budget Changes

userStories.title: First Home Loan Interest Relief 3-Year Rolling Claim Window Tracker on TP1 (SPA Jan2025â€“Dec2027)
userStories.description: The Budget 2025 first home loan interest relief (RM7,000/year for homes â‰¤RM500K; RM5,000 for RM500Kâ€“RM750K) is claimable for three consecutive assessment years from the year of first interest payment, applicable only for SPAs executed between 1 January 2025 and 31 December 2027. The payroll system must track each employee's claim start year to prevent overclaiming beyond year 3 and enforce the SPA date and property value eligibility conditions.
acceptanceCriteria: TP1 captures: SPA execution date, property purchase price category (â‰¤RM500K or RM500Kâ€“RM750K), and Year of Assessment when first interest was paid
PCB engine applies RM7,000 or RM5,000 housing loan interest relief based on property value bracket
System enforces SPA date eligibility: SPA must be between 1 January 2025 and 31 December 2027
Relief is NOT applied in Year 4 or beyond â€” system blocks claim renewal after 3 consecutive YA periods from the first claim year
Property declared as primary residence (not income-generating); employer stores employee self-declaration against TP1 record
Year-end review flags employees who claimed in YA2025 that their eligibility window ends after YA2027
technicalNotes: Introduced in Budget 2025; effective from YA2025, continuing to YA2026 and YA2027 for eligible claimants
Relief is DISTINCT from any general housing loan interest relief â€” applies only to first residential home purchased under qualifying SPA
BERNAMA official announcement: https://bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2353589
Home must not be used to generate rental or business income â€” employee self-declaration stored against TP1 record
3-year window example: employee with SPA in March 2025 first pays interest in April 2025 (YA2025) â€” can claim for YA2025, YA2026, YA2027 only

This differs from the existing 'Update PCB TP1 Relief Tables for YA2025 (New Housing Loan Interest Relief...)' which adds the relief field â€” this story adds the 3-year period enforcement and eligibility date guard
dependencies: Update PCB TP1 Relief Tables for YA2025 (New Housing Loan Interest Relief and Increased Disability Amounts)

userStories.title: Statutory Contribution Compliance Certificate Bundle Export for Government Procurement Tender
userStories.description: Malaysian government and GLC procurement regulations require suppliers to provide proof of EPF, SOCSO, EIS, and LHDN PCB compliance before bid qualification. Procurement officers request recent contribution payment records and submission histories. The payroll module should auto-generate a formatted compliance evidence bundle covering the last 3â€“6 months of all statutory submissions, exportable as a structured PDF for tender submission without manual HR compilation.
acceptanceCriteria: Export covers EPF, SOCSO, EIS, and PCB for a configurable date range (last 3 or 6 months), with submission dates, payment references, and total amounts per month
Bundle header displays: company name, employer registration numbers (EPF No., SOCSO No., EIS No., PCB Employer No.), and the date range covered
PDF export formatted for government procurement submission: clear table per statutory body with monthly submission date, amount paid, and payment reference
Data sourced from existing submission tracking records already stored by the payroll module (no re-entry required)
Export generates an index page summarising all statutory bodies, total contributions, and last submission date
Report is accessible from the LHDN Payroll workspace as a one-click export
technicalNotes: Legal basis: Arahan Perbendaharaan (Treasury Instructions) and UKAS procurement guidelines for government suppliers in Malaysia
This report aggregates data from: CP39 remittance records, EPF Borang A records, SOCSO Borang 8A records, EIS submission records
Actual statutory body compliance letters (from KWSP, PERKESO, LHDN) must still be obtained directly from those bodies â€” this export provides the supporting payroll evidence
EPF compliance portal: https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/employer, SOCSO: https://assist.perkeso.gov.my/, LHDN: https://www.hasil.gov.my/en/employers/
dependencies: Generate CP39 monthly PCB remittance file
Generate EPF Borang A monthly contribution schedule
Generate SOCSO Borang 8A monthly contribution schedule

userStories.title: MyTax Wakil Majikan Authorization Annual Validity Review and Re-Registration Alert
userStories.description: The MyTax Employer Representative (Wakil Majikan) role authorisation â€” required for e-CP22, e-SPC (CP21/22A/22B), e-PCB Plus, and Borang E e-submissions â€” is person-specific and tied to an individual employee. If the authorised representative resigns or their access lapses without renewal, all LHDN e-service submissions on behalf of the employer are blocked. The payroll system must track the representative's employment status and alert HR to re-register before any submissions are blocked.
acceptanceCriteria: Company-level record stores Wakil Majikan name, IC number, and the date they were registered on MyTax
Annual alert raised 60 and 30 days after the anniversary of registration date, prompting HR to verify the representative is still an active employee with valid MyTax access

If the Wakil Majikan's employee record is terminated in the system, an immediate critical alert is raised: 'Wakil Majikan [Name] has left â€” re-register a new authorised representative on MyTax before next e-service submission'
Dashboard widget on LHDN Payroll workspace shows current Wakil Majikan status (Active / Requires Review / Unregistered)
Admin can update Wakil Majikan details after re-registration without developer intervention
Before any e-CP22, e-SPC, or e-PCB Plus batch submission, system checks that a Wakil Majikan is on record and alerts if missing
technicalNotes: Wakil Majikan role registration required via MyTax â€” roles include: PCB Administrator, Employer Representative

Relates to but extends existing story 'MyTax Employer Representative (Wakil Majikan) Configuration on Company for e-Services Submissions' â€” that story covers initial setup; this covers ongoing validity monitoring
LHDN e-PCB Plus transition guide: https://pandahrms.com/lhdns-early-announcement-transition-to-e-pcb-plus-system/
The authorisation does not have a published LHDN expiry date â€” but employee turnover makes annual review a compliance best practice
For companies with multiple branches, each branch may need a separate Wakil Majikan registration â€” consider multi-branch support
dependencies: MyTax Employer Representative (Wakil Majikan) Configuration on Company for e-Services Submissions

userStories.title: HRD Corp eTRIS Grant Approval Status API Sync for Automatic Levy Utilisation Ledger Update
userStories.description: Employers with HRD Corp training grants under SBL-KHAS or other schemes currently check grant status manually on the eTRIS portal. The internal levy utilisation ledger in the payroll system becomes stale between manual updates. An API-based or scheduled sync with the eTRIS portal should automatically pull grant approval status, claim payment dates, and confirmed utilisation amounts, keeping the HRD levy balance accurate without manual HR intervention.
acceptanceCriteria: eTRIS API or portal credentials stored securely on Company record (employer ID, eTRIS login reference)
Scheduled daily sync pulls: pending grant applications, newly approved grants, paid claims, and rejected claims from eTRIS
Levy utilisation ledger updated automatically: approved grants reduce available levy balance; confirmed paid claims mark utilisation as final
Alert raised when a previously approved grant is rejected by HRD Corp (e.g., post-training audit failure or documentation deficiency)
Training activity records linked to eTRIS grant application IDs for traceability
Manual sync option available for immediate refresh before levy balance review or internal audit
technicalNotes: HRD Corp eTRIS portal: https://etris.hrdcorp.gov.my/
HRD Corp FAQ: https://hrdcorp.gov.my/faq

Extends existing stories: 'HRD Corp eTRIS Training Claim Recording and Levy Utilization Dashboard' (manual tracking UI) and 'HRD Corp SBL-KHAS Grant Pre-Approval Compliance and 6-Month Post-Training Claim Deadline Alert' (deadline alerts) â€” this adds the automation/sync layer
If HRD Corp eTRIS does not expose a public REST API, sync can use authenticated browser automation (Playwright headless) as a fallback
Monthly levy payment (1% of Malaysian wages, due by 15th) is separate from this story â€” this handles utilisation tracking post-payment
HRD Corp RM50,000 forfeiture rule and 15% auto-deduction reconciliation (separate story) depend on accurate real-time levy balance from this sync
dependencies: HRD Corp eTRIS Training Claim Recording and Levy Utilization Dashboard
Track HRD Corp Levy Utilization Rate and Alert on 15% Under-Utilisation Penalty
HRD Corp RM50,000 Levy Balance Threshold Tracker and 15% Auto-Deduction Ledger Reconciliation

userStories.title: Progressive Wage Policy Monthly Payroll Evidence Package Assembly for SOCSO/JTK Compliance Audit
userStories.description: Employers participating in Malaysia's Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) receive a government wage subsidy (up to RM200â€“RM300/employee/month for 12 months) conditional on meeting occupation-based wage floors and providing at least 21 annual training hours per employee. Monthly payroll evidence (wage level records + training hour logs) must be maintained and are auditable by SOCSO and the Ministry of Human Resources. The payroll system should auto-assemble this evidence package monthly without requiring HR to compile records manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Monthly report lists all PWP-enrolled employees with: current monthly gross wage, minimum required wage per occupation code, compliance status (met/breach), and training hours accumulated YTD
Government subsidy received per employee per month recorded on Employee record and reconciled against SOCSO disbursement
Training hours tracked at employee level with date, hours, training provider, and HRD Corp grant reference â€” cumulative YTD total visible
Alert raised in Q4 when an enrolled employee has accumulated fewer than 21 annual training hours (with hours deficit shown)
Monthly evidence package export (PDF/Excel) contains: employee wage list, training log, and SOCSO subsidy receipts â€” formatted per SOCSO/JTK audit requirements
If SOCSO suspends subsidy for wage floor breach, alert HR with specific employee, reason, and corrective action required
technicalNotes: PWP launched January 2025; SOCSO administers the subsidy for eligible employees earning RM1,700â€“RM4,999/month in covered industries
Government subsidy: up to RM200/month (entry-level) or RM300/month (higher positions) for 12 months per enrolled employee
21-hour annual training must use HRD Corp-registered providers or TVET institutions â€” link with HRD Corp eTRIS training records
SOCSO as administrator: https://www.perkeso.gov.my/; employers must register participation via SOCSO

This story extends existing stories: 'Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag' (eligibility) and 'Progressive Wage Policy â€” 21-Hour Annual Training Completion Tracking' (annual tracking) â€” this adds monthly evidence assembly for audit
Reference: https://www.businesstoday.com.my/2026/02/23/over-51000-workers-benefit-from-progressive-wage-policy/
dependencies: Progressive Wage Policy (PWP) Eligibility Flag and Wage Escalation Compliance Report
Progressive Wage Policy â€” 21-Hour Annual Training Completion Tracking per Eligible Employee
Progressive Wage Policy Government Incentive Subsidy Claim Tracking Workflow

userStories.title: Mid-Year TP1 or TP3 Submission PCB Recalibration Notification to Employee
userStories.description: When an employee submits a TP1 (relief declaration) or TP3 (prior employer YTD income) mid-year, the PCB deduction for all subsequent months changes â€” sometimes significantly. Employees are frequently confused when their net pay changes without explanation, generating avoidable HR queries. The payroll system should auto-generate a concise PCB recalibration notice on the next payslip showing the old PCB, the new PCB, the reason for the change, and the effective date.
acceptanceCriteria: When a new TP1 or TP3 form is processed and changes the employee's PCB computation, the next salary slip is flagged for a recalibration notice
Notice on payslip displays: 'PCB Updated â€” Previous: RM X/month â†’ New: RM Y/month (effective [Date]). Reason: [TP1 tax relief declaration received / TP3 prior income declared]'
Notice appears only on the first payslip after the change â€” not repeated on subsequent payslips
If both TP1 and TP3 are processed simultaneously, a single combined notice summarises both impacts
Employee self-service portal shows a notification banner: 'Your PCB deduction has been updated â€” tap to see details'
HR can suppress the notice per employee via a checkbox on the Salary Slip for testing or special cases
technicalNotes: PCB recalibration mechanics: when TP1 received in month M, use updated YTD income and new reliefs to compute remaining months' PCB (Method 1 or Method 2 per config)
For TP3 (prior employer income declaration), the YTD taxable income base increases â€” PCB for remaining months often increases significantly to catch up
This is a UX/communication story rather than a new regulatory requirement â€” improves transparency and reduces HR query volume
LHDN PCB 2026 Specification (no formula change from 2025): https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/arvlrzh5/spesifikasi-kaedah-pengiraan-berkomputer-pcb-2026.pdf
Implementation: store 'previous PCB' on Salary Slip before recalibration and 'recalibration_reason' field; Jinja template in payslip format uses these to render the notice
dependencies: Implement TP1 Employee Relief Declaration DocType and PCB Integration
Implement TP3 Carry-Forward Declaration for New Hires (Prior Employer YTD)

userStories.title: ITA Section 83(3): Payroll Final Pay Retention Workflow â€” Block Release Until e-SPC Tax Clearance Letter Received
userStories.description: Under Section 83(3) of the Income Tax Act 1967, employers who have notified LHDN of an employee's cessation or departure (via CP21/CP22A/CP22B submitted through e-SPC) are legally required to withhold any moneys due to the employee for up to 90 days from notification, until LHDN issues the Surat Penyelesaian Cukai (SPC/Tax Clearance Letter). Releasing final pay before SPC receipt constitutes a statutory breach. This workflow is not covered by any existing CP21/CP22A story, which cover notification submission only.
acceptanceCriteria: When a CP21 or CP22A is submitted via e-SPC, system sets a 'Final Pay Hold' flag on the employee record with hold_expiry_date = submission_date + 90 days
Payroll processing for the terminated employee blocks final payment disbursement if SPC has not yet been recorded on the employee record
HR user can manually record SPC receipt (SPC reference number and date) which clears the hold and enables final pay release in next payroll run
System sends alert to HR Manager at 14-day and 7-day intervals before the 90-day hold expires if no SPC has been received
Audit log records: SPC receipt date, reference number, cleared-by user
technicalNotes: Income Tax Act 1967 Section 83(3): employer must retain moneys due to the employee for 90 days after notifying LHDN
e-SPC processing time is approximately 14 working days; employer should not release pay before SPC confirmed
Fields to add on Employee DocType: custom_spc_hold_start_date, custom_spc_hold_expiry_date, custom_spc_reference_number, custom_spc_received_date
Integrates with CP21/CP22A/CP22B workflow; from 1 January 2025 all three forms mandatory online via e-SPC on MyTax
dependencies: Auto-Submit CP21/CP22A/CP22B via e-SPC MyTax at Cessation or Departure

userStories.title: SOCSO/EIS RM6,000 Ceiling: Post-Grace Period Retroactive Contribution Correction Audit Tool
userStories.description: The SOCSO and EIS wage ceiling increased from RM5,000 to RM6,000 effective October 1, 2024, with a six-month grace period that expired on March 31, 2025. After the grace period, full compliance is mandatory with penalties applying. Employers who failed to update contributions for employees earning RM5,001â€“RM6,000 during the grace window may have underpaid. An audit tool to identify affected payroll periods and generate correction schedules is needed â€” distinct from the existing enforcement story which covers ongoing forward compliance only.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit report identifies all salary slips from October 2024 to March 2025 where employee gross wages were between RM5,001 and RM6,000 and SOCSO/EIS contributions were calculated on the old RM5,000 ceiling
Report quantifies the contribution shortfall per employee per month (employer portion and employee portion separately for SOCSO and EIS)
Report generates a suggested correction schedule showing additional contributions required to bring each affected period to compliance
Summary view shows total employer liability across all affected employees and periods
Corrected contribution amounts are exportable in PERKESO e-Caruman format for submission via PERKESO ASSIST portal
technicalNotes: Old ceiling: RM5,000; new ceiling: RM6,000; transition window: October 1, 2024 to March 31, 2025
Grace period confirmed: Crowe Malaysia advisory states no penalties during Oct 2024 â€“ Mar 2025 transition; full compliance required from April 1, 2025
Contribution table lookup must compare salary slips against both old and new Jadual Kadar Caruman tables
PERKESO ASSIST portal accepts corrected submission via e-Caruman batch upload
dependencies: Enforce SOCSO/EIS Wage Ceiling Update to RM6,000 (October 2024 Amendment)

userStories.title: TP3(1/2026) PCB Calculator Integration: Apply Prior Employer YTD Taxable Income, EPF, and PCB Paid to Reduce Current MTD
userStories.description: When a new employee submits TP3(1/2026), the new employer is obligated to incorporate the prior employer's Year-to-Date data â€” taxable income earned, EPF contributions paid, and PCB already deducted â€” into the PCB calculation for the remaining months of the year. Without this integration, the new employer over-deducts PCB. The existing story 'Implement TP3 Carry-Forward Declaration for New Hires (Prior Employer YTD)' creates the DocType; this story specifically implements the mathematical feed-through into the MTD calculation engine using the updated TP3(1/2026) form fields aligned with Budget 2026 relief line items.
acceptanceCriteria: When a TP3(1/2026) record exists for an employee, the MTD calculation engine adds the TP3 prior-employer taxable income to the YTD income base before computing remaining-month tax liability
The TP3 EPF YTD amount reduces the EPF deduction allowance available for the remaining months under the current employer
The TP3 PCB YTD amount is deducted from the total annual tax liability before spreading the balance across remaining months

PCB calculator test case: employee joining in June with TP3 showing RM30,000 prior income, RM1,650 EPF, and RM800 PCB; computed MTD for Julyâ€“December must reconcile correctly against annual liability
TP3 data entry form is updated to match FORM PCB TP3 (1/2026) field layout including new Budget 2026 relief line items (domestic tourism, childcare age update)
technicalNotes: TP3 formula per LHDN MTD spec: Annual tax on (P + prior_ytd_income) minus annual tax on (prior_ytd_income), distributed over remaining months
TP3(1/2026) form updated to reflect Budget 2026 relief categories; DocType must capture matching fields
LHDN requires employers to retain TP3 for 7 years; TP3 is not submitted to LHDN
FORM PCB TP3 (1/2026) available for download from hasil.gov.my employer payroll data specification page
dependencies: Implement TP3 Carry-Forward Declaration for New Hires (Prior Employer YTD)
Implement MTD Method 2 (Year-to-Date Recalculation Formula)

userStories.title: TP1(1/2026): Add CCTV Systems to EV Charging Relief and Enforce Biennial (Once per 2-YA) Claim Restriction
userStories.description: Budget 2026 amended the EV Charging Facility Relief under TP1 to include CCTV systems for household use as newly eligible expenses alongside EV charging equipment. Additionally, the relief may only be claimed once every two Years of Assessment (YA), not annually. The existing story covers the YA2027 extension and RM2,500 cap but does not implement the CCTV category addition or the biennial claim frequency guard.
acceptanceCriteria: TP1 form UI and PCB calculator accept 'CCTV systems for household use' as a valid EV Charging Relief expense category alongside existing EV charging equipment

System enforces that a claim for EV Charging Relief is only accepted once per two consecutive Years of Assessment per employee; if claimed in YA2026, system rejects YA2027 claim and generates an alert
Claim frequency tracker stores the last claimed YA per employee so validation persists across payroll periods
TP1(1/2026) form correctly labels and presents the combined EV + CCTV relief with the biennial restriction visible to HR users
technicalNotes: Store last_ev_charging_claim_ya on TP1 relief record; validate current YA != last_ev_charging_claim_ya AND current YA != last_ev_charging_claim_ya + 1
Relief cap remains RM2,500; CCTV addition does not increase the cap
Source form: FORM PCB TP1 (1/2026) from hasil.gov.my PCB 2026 specification
Extends existing story 'Budget 2026 TP1 â€” EV Charging Facility Relief (RM2,500) as a Distinct Line Item Extended to YA2027'
dependencies: Budget 2026 TP1 â€” EV Charging Facility Relief (RM2,500) as a Distinct Line Item Extended to YA2027

userStories.title: TP1(1/2026): Update Child Care Centre Relief Age Eligibility Validator from 6 to 12 Years
